column1: Hero, you should have seen how Rakeesh fought it!  His sword burned brightly as he fought that monster.  Rakeesh be a great warrior still!
column2: 英雄,你真該看看拉基什是怎麼跟它拚的!他跟那怪物廝殺時,劍身燒得耀眼通紅。拉基什到現在都還是個了不起的戰士!

column1: I'm afraid I have not the power to deal with the Elemental now.  It will require your skills and magic to overcome the thing, oh Defeater of Elementals.
column2: I'm afraid I have not the power to deal with the Elemental now.  It will require your skills and magic to overcome the thing, oh Defeater of Elementals.

column1: If you do seek to defeat the Earth Elemental which now walks our streets, you must first understand its Nature.
column2: If you do seek to defeat the Earth Elemental which now walks our streets, you must first understand its Nature.

column1: I hear you be plenty good and beat the elemental.  You be some Hero, for sure.
column2: I hear you be plenty good and beat the elemental.  You be some Hero, for sure.

column1: It was no little honor, being able to use Rakeesh's sword.  I will be giving it back, if you will be giving it to me.
column2: It was no little honor, being able to use Rakeesh's sword.  I will be giving it back, if you will be giving it to me.

column1: So, Hero, not being content with having merely saved the city, I see that you are about to attempt to save the land of Shapeir as well.
column2: 看來啊,英雄,光是救了這座城你還不滿足,你還打算連整片夏皮爾的土地一併救下來。

column1: The light shines brightly within you.  You have so far managed to walk the narrow line between Honor and Necessity.  May your deeds continue to shine, and not be dulled by the darkness you will soon encounter.
column2: The light shines brightly within you.  You have so far managed to walk the narrow line between Honor and Necessity.  May your deeds continue to shine, and not be dulled by the darkness you will soon encounter.

column1: You have proven yourself both brave and resourceful by your actions.
column2: 你的所作所為已證明了你既勇敢又機智。

column1: When first we met, I could feel there was dark within you.  Now I can see that the light is stronger, if you will only let it be so.  It is a narrow path between darkness and light, and you will always walk that tightrope in your soul.
column2: 我們初次相遇時,我便感覺到你心中有黑暗。如今我看見光明更為強大——只要你願意讓它如此。黑暗與光明之間是一條窄路,你的靈魂將永遠走在那條鋼索上。

column1: I have seldom met one with such Power and so much wisdom and courage as you have shown.  Few users of Magic dare face what you have faced to protect this city.
column2: I have seldom met one with such Power and so much wisdom and courage as you have shown.  Few users of Magic dare face what you have faced to protect this city.

column1: The journey you are about to make is to a very dark and dangerous place.  The foe you will face is very powerful in the ways of magic.
column2: 你即將踏上的旅程,通往一個極其黑暗而危險的地方。你要面對的敵人,在魔法之道上極為強大。

column1: Be wary and alert, for you will be surrounded by enemies.  Yet trust your instincts, my friend, for you may still make allies by your actions.
column2: 務必警醒戒備,因為你將四面皆敵。但相信你的直覺,我的朋友,你的作為仍可能為你贏得盟友。

column1: All of us in this city will be hoping for your safety, for all know of the seriousness of your mission.  May fortune smile upon you, %s.
column2: 這座城裡的每一個人都會盼著你平安,因為人人都明白你此行任務的重大。願好運對你微笑,%s。

column1: Keep your weapon handy, Hero, and don't let your enemies get you.  You and I maybe practice some more when you get back.
column2: 武器隨身帶好,英雄,別讓敵人逮著你。等你回來,咱倆或許還能再練幾手。

column1: If you will permit it, my friend, perhaps you and I could practice with weapons before you go.  I happen to know some skills with weapons that might help you on your mission.
column2: If you will permit it, my friend, perhaps you and I could practice with weapons before you go.  I happen to know some skills with weapons that might help you on your mission.

column1: The nature of the Earth Elemental is to endure.  It draws its strength from the very stones that make up this city.
column2: 土元素的本性就是堅忍不拔。它的力量正是來自構成這座城的一塊塊石頭。

column1: It cannot be defeated by a single blow, but must be worn away through your actions and its vulnerability to magical flame, such as a flaming sword.
column2: 它沒辦法一擊就打倒,得靠你一步步把它消磨掉,並且利用它對魔法火焰的弱點,比方說一把燃燒的劍。

column1: Fire disrupts the essence of earth and allows the Elemental to be damaged.  Without Flame, the Elemental simply cannot be defeated.
column2: Fire disrupts the essence of earth and allows the Elemental to be damaged.  Without Flame, the Elemental simply cannot be defeated.

column1: I fought against the Earth Elemental until it knocked me back and my leg collapsed.  The elemental just lumbered away from me.
column2: I fought against the Earth Elemental until it knocked me back and my leg collapsed.  The elemental just lumbered away from me.

column1: I damaged it, but it is still very strong and very dangerous.
column2: I damaged it, but it is still very strong and very dangerous.

column1: I come from a long line of proud warriors.  Fighting is our strength, and battle is our glory.
column2: 我出身於一支驕傲的戰士世家。戰鬥是我們的本領,廝殺是我們的榮耀。

column1: My leg was damaged a long time ago when I tried to fight a Demon that was destroying my people.  The Demon struck me with its poisoned tail.
column2: 很久以前,我為了對抗一隻正在屠戮我族人的惡魔,腿就傷在那時候。那惡魔用牠帶毒的尾巴擊中了我。

column1: The bone was shattered, and because of the poison, the leg did not heal correctly, even with magic.
column2: The bone was shattered, and because of the poison, the leg did not heal correctly, even with magic.

column1: The Demon was sent by a Wizard who sought to overthrow my kingdom.
column2: The Demon was sent by a Wizard who sought to overthrow my kingdom.

column1: I defeated the Demon and eventually the Wizard, but I had to give up the rule of my kingdom to my brother.
column2: I defeated the Demon and eventually the Wizard, but I had to give up the rule of my kingdom to my brother.

column1: I ruled the kingdom of Tarna, until I had to choose between ruling my people or preventing the Wizard from doing greater evil.
column2: I ruled the kingdom of Tarna, until I had to choose between ruling my people or preventing the Wizard from doing greater evil.

column1: I do not regret the choice I made.
column2: 我不後悔自己當初的抉擇。

column1: The Wizard turned out to be a Demon who sought through magical means to break the barriers between his world and ours.
column2: The Wizard turned out to be a Demon who sought through magical means to break the barriers between his world and ours.

column1: It took many years, but I at last managed to send the Demon-Wizard back to his own world.
column2: It took many years, but I at last managed to send the Demon-Wizard back to his own world.

column1: My brother became a most wise ruler of Tarna, and I learned what it is to be a Paladin.
column2: My brother became a most wise ruler of Tarna, and I learned what it is to be a Paladin.

column1: We are a proud race of strong warriors, although we do tend to be a bit vain and perhaps a bit boastful.
column2: 我們是個驕傲的強悍戰士民族,不過嘛,我們也確實有點愛慕虛榮,或許還有點愛吹噓。

column1: The kingdom of Tarna lies to the southwest many, many miles from here.  I have not seen it for a very long while.
column2: The kingdom of Tarna lies to the southwest many, many miles from here.  I have not seen it for a very long while.

column1: The Power of a warrior comes not from his strength, but rather from his Will, his inner nature.
column2: The Power of a warrior comes not from his strength, but rather from his Will, his inner nature.

column1: I find I am becoming less a warrior and more a philosopher as I get older.
column2: 我發現自己年紀越大,越不像個戰士,反倒越像個哲人了。

column1: The Will is the very essence of your soul.
column2: The Will is the very essence of your soul.

column1: It is getting far easier to sit and talk about how to solve problems than to do something about them, I'm afraid.
column2: It is getting far easier to sit and talk about how to solve problems than to do something about them, I'm afraid.

column1: I was given the sword, "Soulforge", when I first became a Paladin.  Only a Paladin has the Will to make it flame.
column2: I was given the sword, "Soulforge", when I first became a Paladin.  Only a Paladin has the Will to make it flame.

column1: It will flame for you because I wish it to.
column2: 它會為你燃燒,因為這是我的意願。

column1: The Way of the Paladin is to seek to know that which truly is.
column2: The Way of the Paladin is to seek to know that which truly is.

column1: The Paladin strives to learn his own inner nature and that of others.
column2: The Paladin strives to learn his own inner nature and that of others.

column1: The Paladin does whatever needs to be done to bring light to the world.
column2: The Paladin does whatever needs to be done to bring light to the world.

column1: Not for glory, not for gain, the Paladin Becomes a Paladin because it is his Will.
column2: Not for glory, not for gain, the Paladin Becomes a Paladin because it is his Will.

column1: My sword is called "Soulforge".  It is a flaming sword.
column2: My sword is called "Soulforge".  It is a flaming sword.

column1: She is my friend.  She is a good fighter and teacher, and you would do well to practice with her.
column2: 她是我的朋友。她既是好戰士,也是好老師,你能跟她過幾招對你大有好處。

column1: She can teach you much about the warrior skills.
column2: 她能教你許多戰士技藝。

column1: He reminds me of a Lion cub.
column2: He reminds me of a Lion cub.

column1: It is good to be around children.  They remind you what it is to be young, and teach you what it is to be alive.
column2: It is good to be around children.  They remind you what it is to be young, and teach you what it is to be alive.

column1: The Sultan's forces were destroyed by magic when Harun Al-Rashid sought to restore order to Raseir.
column2: The Sultan's forces were destroyed by magic when Harun Al-Rashid sought to restore order to Raseir.

column1: I feel that the dark magic which overwhelmed Raseir will soon try to do the same here.
column2: I feel that the dark magic which overwhelmed Raseir will soon try to do the same here.

column1: When I was younger, I did much adventuring and exploring.  That was, of course, before my leg was injured.  I do not travel much now.  I am here at the Sultan's request.
column2: 我年輕時,曾四處冒險探索。當然,那都是我這條腿受傷以前的事了。如今我不太遠行了。我來這兒,是應蘇丹之請。

column1: The Sultan summoned me to Shapeir to aid against the Dark Magic which even now hovers near this city.  I fear, though, that I am not the Hero I was once.  Perhaps you will become the Hero they need.
column2: The Sultan summoned me to Shapeir to aid against the Dark Magic which even now hovers near this city.  I fear, though, that I am not the Hero I was once.  Perhaps you will become the Hero they need.

column1: A true Hero knows what must be done when there is need, and he must be able to do it.
column2: A true Hero knows what must be done when there is need, and he must be able to do it.

column1: A Ghoul is a very dangerous thing to fight.  Its very touch draws your life from you.  You must be very quick to avoid its touch.
column2: 跟食屍鬼交手是非常危險的事。光是被牠碰一下,就會把你的性命抽走。你動作得快到能躲開牠的觸碰才行。

column1: The Terrorsaurus is very swift, and its powerful hindquarters can easily disembowel a man.
column2: 恐怖龍速度極快,牠那強而有力的後腿,輕易就能把人開膛破肚。

column1: The Griffin is a tough fighter, but it seldom attacks humans unless it is attacked.
column2: The Griffin is a tough fighter, but it seldom attacks humans unless it is attacked.

column1: Brigands search the desert for unwary travelers.  They will kill gladly, and steal whatever they can.
column2: Brigands search the desert for unwary travelers.  They will kill gladly, and steal whatever they can.

column1: When a pack of Jackalmen attack, it is the weakest which strikes first.   Keep your blade trained upon one at a time until that foe is defeated, or you will find yourself overwhelmed by their numbers.
column2: 一群豺狼人來襲時,打頭陣的往往是最弱的那個。把你的劍鋒一次只對準一個,直到把那敵手解決掉,否則你會被牠們的數量給淹沒。

column1: It is unwise to get into a fight with a Scorpion unless you have taken a Poison Cure Pill and are very skilled at dodging and parrying.  Few escape from combat with a Scorpion.
column2: It is unwise to get into a fight with a Scorpion unless you have taken a Poison Cure Pill and are very skilled at dodging and parrying.  Few escape from combat with a Scorpion.

column1: You can find pills at the local apothecary.  You must take the Poison Cure Pill before you are struck by the scorpion's venomous tail.
column2: You can find pills at the local apothecary.  You must take the Poison Cure Pill before you are struck by the scorpion's venomous tail.

column1: I have personally never encountered one.
column2: 我自己倒是從來沒碰上過一個。

column1: Perhaps there is someone else who would know more about that than I, for I know very little about it.
column2: 這事我所知甚少,或許另有他人比我懂得更多。

column1: I fear that I can add nothing further to your knowledge.
column2: 恐怕我沒辦法再為你補充些什麼了。

column1: This is a fine kingdom, and its ruler is very wise, but there are signs of dark magic seeking to overwhelm Shapeir.
column2: 這是個美好的王國,它的統治者也十分賢明,但已有跡象顯示,黑魔法正企圖吞噬夏皮爾。

column1: The rumors from Raseir are that Laws have become more important than men, and that it has become a crime to think.  Few go to that city now, and even fewer return.
column2: The rumors from Raseir are that Laws have become more important than men, and that it has become a crime to think.  Few go to that city now, and even fewer return.

column1: A Djinn is a creature of magic.  Some are good and some bad.  They are all dangerous because they have such power.
column2: 精靈是魔法生靈,有善有惡。但牠們個個都危險,因為手裡握著那等駭人的力量。

column1: To understand the nature of the Fire Elemental, speak to someone who understands the essence of Flame.
column2: To understand the nature of the Fire Elemental, speak to someone who understands the essence of Flame.

column1: To understand the nature of the Water Elemental, seek the person to whom Water is inner nature.
column2: To understand the nature of the Water Elemental, seek the person to whom Water is inner nature.

column1: There is someone else in this city whose knowledge of the nature of Air far surpasses my own.  Ask the one who has his head in the clouds.
column2: There is someone else in this city whose knowledge of the nature of Air far surpasses my own.  Ask the one who has his head in the clouds.

column1: The Earth Elemental is the magical essence of stone and rock.  It is extremely dangerous and difficult to defeat, unless you understand its Nature.
column2: The Earth Elemental is the magical essence of stone and rock.  It is extremely dangerous and difficult to defeat, unless you understand its Nature.

column1: An Elemental is formed by magic which gives being to the essence of an element.  It is chaotic and extremely destructive.
column2: 元素生靈是由魔法將某種元素的精髓賦予了生命而成。牠混亂無序,破壞力驚人。

column1: There are many monsters out on the desert.  They are all easy to avoid if you simply do not go out in the desert to look for them.
column2: 沙漠裡頭怪物多得是。不過要躲也容易——只要你別自己跑進沙漠去找牠們就行了。

column1: Magic surrounds all of us in this world and is a part of us also.  Aziza has many more answers than I.
column2: 在這世上,魔法環繞著我們每一個人,也是我們的一部分。這方面阿姿莎懂得比我多得多。

column1: The Sultan Harun Al-Rashid watches over his people and strives to make sure they are happy and content.
column2: The Sultan Harun Al-Rashid watches over his people and strives to make sure they are happy and content.

column1: It sorrows him greatly to think of Raseir.  It angers him greatly to think of the one behind Raseir.
column2: 一想到拉希爾,他便深感悲痛;一想到拉希爾背後那個人,他更是怒火中燒。

column1: The Emir Arus of Raseir was well-loved but not wise.  The current Emir Hassan of Raseir is neither loved nor wise.
column2: The Emir Arus of Raseir was well-loved but not wise.  The current Emir Hassan of Raseir is neither loved nor wise.

column1: The Palace of the Sultan is a very beautiful place.  I believe you will like it.
column2: 蘇丹的宮殿是個極美的地方,我想你會喜歡的。

column1: The caravan to Raseir leaves in a few weeks.  You should consult with the guards at the gate to the city.
column2: 前往拉希爾的商隊再過幾週就要出發。你該去城門口找衛兵打聽打聽。

column1: The desert is very dangerous to the unwary.  Even a powerful warrior equipped with plenty of water can perish if he travels during the heat of the day.
column2: 沙漠對粗心大意的人極其凶險。哪怕是帶足了水的強悍戰士,要是在大白天最熱的時候趕路,照樣可能命喪當場。

column1: Someone without much money may be far richer than someone with much money.
column2: 一個沒什麼錢的人,說不定遠比一個腰纏萬貫的人富有。

column1: That is a very dangerous subject about which to inquire.
column2: 那可是個打聽起來非常危險的話題。

column1: This place is very much a home for me.
column2: 這地方對我來說,很有家的味道。

column1: Your Katta friends have a most excellent Inn, from all I hear.
column2: Your Katta friends have a most excellent Inn, from all I hear.

column1: I find the warm weather here most pleasant on my old bones.
column2: 我這把老骨頭,覺得這裡暖洋洋的天氣舒服極了。

column1: The fountain which forms this city is indeed magical.  It gives life to all that live around it.
column2: 孕育出這座城的噴泉,確實帶著魔力。它賜予了周遭一切生靈生命。

column1: The stories told about Raseir lead one to suspect that it is not a very pleasant place to be.
column2: 關於拉希爾的種種傳聞,叫人不禁懷疑那地方住起來大概不怎麼舒坦。

column1: Her place is in the middle of the web of alleys to the southeast.
column2: 她的住處就在東南方那片像蛛網似的巷弄正中央。

column1: He seeks knowledge in the stars.  I find knowledge everywhere.
column2: 他在星辰之中尋求學問,而我則是處處都能尋得學問。

column1: She is indeed a person worth knowing.  She can tell you many things.
column2: She is indeed a person worth knowing.  She can tell you many things.

column1: Her house is just off the east end of Shmali Tarik.  She has the door with the eye upon it.
column2: Her house is just off the east end of Shmali Tarik.  She has the door with the eye upon it.

column1: There is much truth in what he speaks.
column2: 他說的話,有很多都是實情。

column1: The merchants will gladly sell you anything they have.
column2: 商人們可樂意把手上的任何東西賣給你。

column1: It is easy to underestimate the Katta.  They are shrewd, and see much.
column2: 人很容易小看卡塔族。他們精明得很,看得也透。

column1: The Dervish speaks.  All listen, but few understand.
column2: 苦行僧開口了。眾人都在聽,真正聽懂的卻沒幾個。

column1: My name is Rasha Rakeesh SahTarna.  I am called Rakeesh.  I am of the Liontaur people.
column2: 我的名字是拉夏·拉基什·薩塔納。人們喚我拉基什。我是獅人族的一員。

column1: The Adventurer's Log Book contains the stories of many great fighters' adventures.
column2: The Adventurer's Log Book contains the stories of many great fighters' adventures.

column1: Aziza is a very powerful enchantress, wise in the ways of the elements.  You might visit her house near the Shmali Tarik, if you can win her respect.
column2: 阿姿莎是位法力高強的女術士,深諳元素之道。要是你能贏得她的敬重,不妨去施瑪利塔里克附近的她家拜訪拜訪。

column1: You lower your weapon and yield the combat.
column2: 你放下武器,認輸了。

column1: You not be remembering rules of practice combat.  Come back when you ready to fight fair.
column2: 你忘了練習對戰的規矩。等你準備好公平打的時候再回來。

column1: A combat fought without honor brings no honor onto the combatant.  Perhaps we shall practice again when you have learned the meaning of honor.
column2: 不以榮譽而戰,便不能為戰士帶來榮譽。等你領悟了榮譽的真諦,我們或許可以再切磋一次。

column1: There's no need for an apology.
column2: 不必道歉。

column1: That's better.
column2: 這樣才像話。

column1: It's too late for that now.
column2: It's too late for that now.

column1: It is actually quite a drop.  You would do better to use the steps.
column2: 這落差其實還挺大的,你最好還是走階梯。

column1: The air is humid, and the decor is sea-green.  Aziza keeps a tidy house.
column2: The air is humid, and the decor is sea-green.  Aziza keeps a tidy house.

column1: It would be nice if there was some tea to drink.
column2: It would be nice if there was some tea to drink.

column1: The tea is cool and refreshing.
column2: 這茶清涼又提神。

column1: Please, you must sit down and relax before we shall speak of what you seek.
column2: 來,你得先坐下放鬆,我們再來談你所求之事。

column1: Welcome.  It is good to have a visitor who understands the proper way to enter conversation.
column2: 歡迎。能有一位懂得如何得體開口的訪客,真好。

column1: You tell Aziza about Al Scurva.
column2: 你把阿爾·斯庫瓦的事告訴了阿姿莎。

column1: Your words are distressing.  Ad Avis is a most dangerous Wizard.
column2: Your words are distressing.  Ad Avis is a most dangerous Wizard.

column1: You tell Aziza everything you know about the caged beast.
column2: 你把你所知關於那頭籠中野獸的一切,全告訴了阿姿莎。

column1: There is some form of magical protection around the cage which prevents me from detecting it.  It is clear that a powerful Magic User intends the creature to die.  The beast is probably enchanted.  You will need a Dispel Potion to find its true form.
column2: There is some form of magical protection around the cage which prevents me from detecting it.  It is clear that a powerful Magic User intends the creature to die.  The beast is probably enchanted.  You will need a Dispel Potion to find its true form.

column1: It is wise of you to have told me that.  The more information I have, the better I can judge things.
column2: 你把那件事告訴我,是明智之舉。我握有的消息越多,就越能把事情看得準。

column1: You are most brash, but I will assume that you mean only to flatter me.
column2: 你還真是莽撞,不過我就當你只是想恭維我罷了。

column1: I wish to speak with you tomorrow about Raseir.  I intend to work some magic tonight to gather information about the things you will face.  I will let you know at our meeting what I find out.
column2: 我想明天跟你談談拉希爾的事。今晚我打算施些法術,蒐集你即將面對的種種訊息。屆時相見,我會把探得的消息告訴你。

column1: But forgive me, I have forsaken my manners.  Come in, sit down, and we shall share tea and talk, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 不過,請原諒我失了禮數。進來吧,坐下,我們一同品茶敘話,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: Thank you for the kindness you have shown Julanar.  You are a true Hero for helping her.
column2: Thank you for the kindness you have shown Julanar.  You are a true Hero for helping her.

column1: Come in, sit down, and be welcome.
column2: 進來吧,坐下,歡迎你。

column1: I am disturbed that you are not yet a Wizard.  You very much need to pass the tests at the Wizard's Institute of Technocery if you wish to be more than just a Magic User.
column2: I am disturbed that you are not yet a Wizard.  You very much need to pass the tests at the Wizard's Institute of Technocery if you wish to be more than just a Magic User.

column1: Despite your previous behavior in my presence, you have proven yourself a hero.  I wish to discuss some information I have learned about Raseir.  Come in, sit down, and we shall speak.
column2: Despite your previous behavior in my presence, you have proven yourself a hero.  I wish to discuss some information I have learned about Raseir.  Come in, sit down, and we shall speak.

column1: You have saved the city through your resourcefulness and bravery.
column2: 你憑著機智與勇氣,拯救了這座城。

column1: You are very much a hero.  Welcome to my home, %s.  May your deeds be remembered forever.
column2: 你確實是位英雄。歡迎來到我家,%s。願你的事蹟永垂不朽。

column1: I worked some magic last night to uncover information about your trip to Raseir.  I need to discuss this with you.
column2: 昨夜我施了些法術,探出了關於你拉希爾此行的消息。我得跟你談談這件事。

column1: Come in, sit down, and we shall speak.
column2: 進來吧,坐下,我們好好談談。

column1: Grrrrrooooooonnnnnk!
column2: Grrrrrooooooonnnnnk!

column1: Welcome, stranger.  I shall be honored to have you for a guest.
column2: 歡迎你,陌生人。能請你作客,是我的榮幸。

column1: Gronkety gronk? (Perhaps he has a Saurus Throatus?)
column2: Gronkety gronk? (Perhaps he has a Saurus Throatus?)

column1: Come in, we shall all sit and see what we shall see.
column2: Come in, we shall all sit and see what we shall see.

column1: You must take the caravan to Raseir.  I will keep the Emir with me for safe-keeping, while you find the means to restore him to his rightful shape.
column2: You must take the caravan to Raseir.  I will keep the Emir with me for safe-keeping, while you find the means to restore him to his rightful shape.

column1: We all are hoping for your success, Hero, and eagerly await the pleasure of your return.  Farewell, %s.
column2: 我們都盼著你能成功,英雄,並殷切期待著你歸來的喜悅。再會了,%s。

column1: It is very dishonorable of you to try to cast a spell in my home.  It would be wise for you to leave at this time.
column2: 你竟想在我家裡施法,實在毫無榮譽可言。此刻你還是離開為妙。

column1: I will not tolerate such rudeness!
column2: 我絕不容許這般無禮!

column1: It is not polite of you to go where you are not invited.  If you wish to speak with me, then come sit down.  Otherwise, you are free to leave.
column2: 未受邀就擅闖,可不太禮貌。你若想跟我說話,就過來坐下;不然的話,你大可離去。

column1: Very well, if such is your choice.  What is it that you wish to know?
column2: 好吧,既然你這麼選。你想知道什麼?

column1: I believe I asked you a question, young man.
column2: 我記得我問了你一個問題,年輕人。

column1: Shall we share tea?
column2: 要不要一同喝杯茶?

column1: The tea is cool and refreshing.  You find yourself relaxing as you drink and listen to the sound of flowing water.
column2: 這茶清涼爽口。你一邊啜飲,一邊聽著潺潺水聲,不知不覺便鬆弛了下來。

column1: Now that we have shared tea, what is it for which you have come?
column2: 茶也喝過了,那麼,你此行所為何來?

column1: Perhaps it would be well to show you as well as to tell you about such things.
column2: 這種事,也許不只說給你聽,還該演示給你看才好。

column1: You did that already, remember?
column2: You did that already, remember?

column1: You must give her the gifts of Water, Earth, and a hug.
column2: You must give her the gifts of Water, Earth, and a hug.

column1: That is indeed a sad tale.  Let me show you of that which we speak.
column2: 這的確是個令人傷感的故事。讓我把我們所說的指給你看吧。

column1: You already are.
column2: 你已經是了。

column1: Yes, come sit at my table.
column2: 好,過來,坐到我的桌旁。

column1: First we shall enjoy our tea before we speak.
column2: First we shall enjoy our tea before we speak.

column1: You could not have forgotten already?
column2: 你不會這麼快就忘了吧?

column1: Since the beast is protected from my true sight, then it is probably not what it seems.  You will need a dispel potion to find its true form.
column2: 既然那頭怪物連我的真知之眼都看不透,那它八成不是表面看起來那樣。你需要一瓶驅散藥水,才能看出它的真面目。

column1: No bait.  Sorry.
column2: 沒有魚餌,抱歉。

column1: It has to be the biggest bunch of carp you've ever seen.
column2: 這準是你這輩子見過最大的一群鯉魚了。

column1: Hmm.  If you only had some chips...
column2: 唔,要是你再有些薯條就好了……

column1: It has been a pleasure conversing with you.  Perhaps we shall share tea again sometime.
column2: 與你交談十分愉快。或許改日我們能再一同品茶。

column1: It has been a pleasure.  I'm sorry we didn't converse at greater length.
column2: 很高興認識你。可惜沒能多聊一會兒。

column1: It is more polite to ask your leave by saying "Farewell" before you leave the table.
column2: It is more polite to ask your leave by saying "Farewell" before you leave the table.

column1: There are all sorts of odds and ends on the shelves.  For instance, you see %s
column2: There are all sorts of odds and ends on the shelves.  For instance, you see %s

column1: You see a jar of freeze-dried water.
column2: 你看到一罐冷凍乾燥水。

column1: You see a vial of saline solution.
column2: 你看到一小瓶生理食鹽水。

column1: You see a can of wetting solution.
column2: 你看到一罐潤濕液。

column1: You see a bottle of Eau de Anchovy.
column2: 你看到一瓶「鯷魚香水」。

column1: You see a Klein bottle.
column2: 你看到一個克萊因瓶。

column1: You see a bug in a bottle.
column2: 你看到一隻瓶中的蟲子。

column1: You see a 10-speed Oysterizer.
column2: 你看到一台十段變速的「牡蠣果汁機」。

column1: Amazing!  Somebody actually built a computer in a bottle.  How do they do that?
column2: 太神奇了!竟然真有人在瓶子裡造了一台電腦。這是怎麼辦到的?

column1: You see a note in a bottle.  Fanmail from some flounder?
column2: You see a note in a bottle.  Fanmail from some flounder?

column1: You see a bottle of butter-flavored syrup.
column2: 你看到一瓶奶油風味糖漿。

column1: You see a squirt bottle.
column2: 你看到一個擠壓噴瓶。

column1: You see a Deluxe Donut Dunker.
column2: 你看到一台豪華甜甜圈沾醬器。

column1: You see some moisturizing shampoo.
column2: 你看到一些保濕洗髮精。

column1: You see a purposeless porpoise.
column2: 你看到一隻無所事事的海豚。

column1: You see a gratuitous goblet.
column2: 你看到一只多此一舉的高腳杯。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles.  For instance, Aziza owns Volume %d of the Tide-Life Self-Kelp Series:  %s
column2: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles.  For instance, Aziza owns Volume %d of the Tide-Life Self-Kelp Series:  %s

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles.  For instance, Aziza owns %s
column2: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles.  For instance, Aziza owns %s

column1: Fire elemental
column2: Fire elemental

column1: Water elemental
column2: Water elemental

column1: Earth elemental
column2: Earth elemental

column1: Air elemental
column2: Air elemental

column1: Elemental
column2: Elemental

column1: Plant
column2: Plant

column1: When I try to use Far-see upon the city of Raseir, it is as though the city is wrapped in a web of blackness, which I cannot see through.
column2: 每當我試著對拉希爾城施展遠視,那城就彷彿被一張黑暗之網裹住,我怎麼也看不穿。

column1: Magic can be misused for ill purposes.  A foe can seem to be a friend, and the victim is helpless against the foe.  The more powerful the magic, the more likely that it can be used for evil purposes.
column2: 魔法可能被拿來作惡。敵人能裝成朋友,受害者卻對他毫無防備。魔法越強大,就越可能被用在邪惡的目的上。

column1: Spells such as the Summonings of Djinn and Elementals are easily abused.  There is a spell commonly used by the Djinn and those which command the Djinn which transforms a man into a beast, usually a creature similar to the man's nature.
column2: Spells such as the Summonings of Djinn and Elementals are easily abused.  There is a spell commonly used by the Djinn and those which command the Djinn which transforms a man into a beast, usually a creature similar to the man's nature.

column1: Magic can be a dangerous weapon to the Magic User who wraps his soul in darkness.
column2: Magic can be a dangerous weapon to the Magic User who wraps his soul in darkness.

column1: There have been many dark predictions concerning this land lately.  You should consult with the astrologer.
column2: There have been many dark predictions concerning this land lately.  You should consult with the astrologer.

column1: Iblis once sought to rule the world and created a great city in the middle of the desert, but Suleiman ben Daoud summoned all the Djinn to rise against Iblis.  Iblis was bound in a statue, and his mighty city was destroyed to become his tomb.
column2: 伊布利斯曾想統治世界,在沙漠正中央造了一座宏偉之城,但蘇萊曼·本·達烏德召集了所有精靈群起反抗他。伊布利斯被封印在一尊雕像裡,他那座雄城也被摧毀,成了埋葬他的墳塚。

column1: Suleiman ben Daoud was the mightiest and wisest of the Magician-Kings of ancient times.
column2: Suleiman ben Daoud was the mightiest and wisest of the Magician-Kings of ancient times.

column1: An Elemental trying to escape will be drawn to a container of an appropriate nature.  If there is no container, then it will either flee or dissipate and reappear elsewhere.
column2: 想逃脫的元素精靈,會被一個性質相合的容器所吸引。若沒有容器,牠便會逃走,或是消散後在別處重新現身。

column1: A container can be created by choosing an appropriate receptacle for the element (such as a waterskin for Water).  Once the Elemental has entered the container, its magical essence will be retained.
column2: A container can be created by choosing an appropriate receptacle for the element (such as a waterskin for Water).  Once the Elemental has entered the container, its magical essence will be retained.

column1: One who thus captures an Elemental will have power over it to use as he pleases.
column2: 如此擒住元素精靈的人,便能任意支配牠的力量。

column1: The contrary element or weakness of Fire is Water; the weakness of Water is Air; the weakness of Air is Earth; and the weakness of Earth is Fire.
column2: The contrary element or weakness of Fire is Water; the weakness of Water is Air; the weakness of Air is Earth; and the weakness of Earth is Fire.

column1: Weakening an Elemental will force it to seek escape and thus be captured in a suitable container.
column2: 削弱一隻元素精靈,會逼牠設法逃脫,如此便能將牠收進合適的容器裡。

column1: Miss Manners is a personal friend of mine.  We often have tea together.
column2: 禮儀小姐是我的私交好友,我們時常一起喝茶。

column1: Water is the most fundamental of all the elements.  It is the source of life and the essence of all that is beautiful.  Life without water is dull and dry as dust.
column2: Water is the most fundamental of all the elements.  It is the source of life and the essence of all that is beautiful.  Life without water is dull and dry as dust.

column1: The creatures of water are my friends, but whatever you do, don't mention this subject to the owner of the Magic Shop.
column2: 水中的生靈都是我的朋友,但無論如何,千萬別在魔法商店老闆面前提這檔事。

column1: Oh, just the kinds of knick-knacks and things we Enchantresses are liable to pick up at garage sales and professional seminars.
column2: Oh, just the kinds of knick-knacks and things we Enchantresses are liable to pick up at garage sales and professional seminars.

column1: That is something of which I have little knowledge.
column2: 那件事,我所知甚少。

column1: If you please, I would rather talk of something else.
column2: 若你不介意,我寧可聊些別的。

column1: This city was built around the magic fountain which flows freely.  It is protected by the mountains and the desert and has known only peace and prosperity until now.
column2: 這座城是繞著那座自由流淌的魔法泉建起來的。它有群山與沙漠護衛,直到此刻都只嘗過和平與繁榮的滋味。

column1: Raseir was like a twin to Shapeir until a year ago this month.  The Emir Arus was overthrown and disappeared.  His brother now sits upon the throne.  There is a darkness over the city through which I cannot see with all my magic.
column2: 直到一年前的這個月,拉希爾還像是夏皮爾的雙生姊妹。埃米爾阿魯斯被推翻、不知去向,如今坐上王位的是他的弟弟。那座城被一層黑暗籠罩,連我傾盡全部法力也看不透。

column1: The Djinn are very powerful magical beings who roam this land.  There are five types of Djinn:  Djann, Djinn, Shaitan, Ifreet, and Marid.
column2: 精靈(Djinn)是漫遊在這片土地上、法力強大的存在。精靈共分五類:迪安(Djann)、精靈(Djinn)、夏伊坦(Shaitan)、伊夫利特(Ifreet)、與瑪利德(Marid)。

column1: Djann are seldom seen, they take the shape of animals and hide in the mountains to avoid outsiders.  Djinn are more powerful and rule the air, living in the clouds.
column2: Djann are seldom seen, they take the shape of animals and hide in the mountains to avoid outsiders.  Djinn are more powerful and rule the air, living in the clouds.

column1: The Shaitan prefer streams and waterways.  The Ifreet are associated with fire, and as such, prefer the desert.
column2: The Shaitan prefer streams and waterways.  The Ifreet are associated with fire, and as such, prefer the desert.

column1: Most powerful and magical of all are the Marid, and the master of the Marid is Iblis.
column2: 其中法力最強的便是瑪利德,而瑪利德之主正是伊布利斯。

column1: Many things in this world are called monsters.  To my mind, the worst monster is a man who calls himself a friend in order to betray.
column2: 這世上有許多東西被叫作怪物。在我看來,最可怕的怪物,是那種自稱朋友、卻只為背叛的人。

column1: The magic I work with most is water magic, that of knowledge and true seeing.  As water can reflect, I too can reflect spells back upon the caster.  There is much magic in this land, some of a very dark nature.
column2: 我最常使用的是水之魔法,那是知識與真視之術。正如水能映照,我也能將法術反彈回施法者身上。這片土地上魔法繁多,其中有些本質極為黑暗。

column1: The Sultan Harun al-Rashid is a most wise ruler.  He often consults me for information about things.  He has a good deal of understanding about people, and he knows how best to use them.  He is doing everything in his power to prevent the ill omens from coming to pass.
column2: The Sultan Harun al-Rashid is a most wise ruler.  He often consults me for information about things.  He has a good deal of understanding about people, and he knows how best to use them.  He is doing everything in his power to prevent the ill omens from coming to pass.

column1: The Emir Arus was a foolish man who ruled more with his heart than his head.  There was magic involved in his disappearance.  Since he has been gone, the city of Raseir has fallen into dark times, and many have fled the new tyranny.
column2: 埃米爾阿魯斯是個愚昧的人,治國憑的是心而非腦。他的失蹤牽涉到魔法。自他離開後,拉希爾城便陷入黑暗時局,許多人為逃離新的暴政而出走。

column1: If you seek the Wizard's Institute of Technocery, you will need to use the very thing for which you seek it.
column2: 你若想找到巫師技術學院,就得用上你尋找它所要用到的那樣東西。

column1: The caravan is currently the only way most people can travel to Raseir or the lands beyond.
column2: 眼下,商隊是多數人前往拉希爾、或更遠之地的唯一途徑。

column1: The Ifreet make the hot desert sands their home.  They are seldom seen.
column2: 伊夫利特以炎熱的沙漠為家,極少現身。

column1: I am no friend to thieves.
column2: 我可不是盜賊的朋友。

column1: You would know more about that than I.
column2: 那件事,你可比我清楚多了。

column1: The magical fountain is a symbol for our city.
column2: 那座魔法泉,是我們這座城的象徵。

column1: I am not familiar with idle gossip.
column2: 我對那些無聊的閒話可不熟。

column1: The astrologer is fond of making predictions of things to come.  Some of the things of which he speaks actually come to pass.
column2: The astrologer is fond of making predictions of things to come.  Some of the things of which he speaks actually come to pass.

column1: My magic is very practical.  I know many things that go on in this land.  Unfortunately, Raseir has been closed to my Far-seeing since the Emir vanished.  Soon, I will try a different type of spell to see if I can gain more information.
column2: My magic is very practical.  I know many things that go on in this land.  Unfortunately, Raseir has been closed to my Far-seeing since the Emir vanished.  Soon, I will try a different type of spell to see if I can gain more information.

column1: His poetry speaks more than mere words.
column2: 他的詩,道出的遠不只是字句而已。

column1: The Katta are a clever people.  They work only at things they enjoy.
column2: 卡塔族是個聰明的民族。他們只做自己樂在其中的事。

column1: The dervish is a very odd man.  At times, I think he is just a fool, but every once in a while, he says something very wise.
column2: The dervish is a very odd man.  At times, I think he is just a fool, but every once in a while, he says something very wise.

column1: My name means "Highly Prized."  It was the name of my mother.
column2: My name means "Highly Prized."  It was the name of my mother.

column1: He is a most worthy and honorable Paladin.
column2: He is a most worthy and honorable Paladin.

column1: A Paladin is one who lives with honor and courage throughout Life.
column2: A Paladin is one who lives with honor and courage throughout Life.

column1: Keapon's magical abilities are great, but his style is much too childish.  Still, if you must purchase his sort of toy magic, his shop is the place to visit.
column2: 奇朋的魔法本事是不小,只是他的路數太過孩子氣。話雖如此,你若非得買他那種玩具魔法,他的店倒是該去的地方。

column1: Water is the element of healing and life.  I use it in all my magical workings.
column2: 水是治癒與生命的元素。我所有的魔法施作,都離不開它。

column1: My spells allow me to see things at a distance and to know their true nature.
column2: 我的法術能讓我遠觀萬物,並看清它們的真實本質。

column1: In the desert lands, Sharing drinks is a ritual of faith and trust.
column2: In the desert lands, Sharing drinks is a ritual of faith and trust.

column1: I know of a Wizard who once lived in these lands, although he has been gone for many years. He used people as tools to gain power.  His name is Ad Avis
column2: I know of a Wizard who once lived in these lands, although he has been gone for many years. He used people as tools to gain power.  His name is Ad Avis

column1: Ad Avis cares only for power.  It would not greatly surprise me if he were behind the Darkness in Raseir.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯只在乎力量。若拉希爾那片黑暗背後是他在主使,我也不會太意外。

column1: The Apothecary should be able to create such a potion to restore the true form to an enchanted creature.
column2: 那位藥師應該能調配出這樣一帖藥水,讓中了法術的生靈恢復真身。

column1: There are two types of enchantment - that of the body and that of the mind.  The one can change a persons' form, the other makes a person believe what the caster wishes that person to believe.
column2: There are two types of enchantment - that of the body and that of the mind.  The one can change a persons' form, the other makes a person believe what the caster wishes that person to believe.

column1: The portents are clouded.  There is nothing I can show you.
column2: The portents are clouded.  There is nothing I can show you.

column1: Just the facts, Ma'am.
column2: Just the facts, Ma'am.

column1: Right.
column2: Right.

column1: You could be the one to give her back her soul and free her heart from wood.
column2: You could be the one to give her back her soul and free her heart from wood.

column1: You will try this, Hero, will you not?
column2: You will try this, Hero, will you not?

column1: What's a pomegranate?
column2: 石榴是什麼?

column1: You will find her within a day's ride to the east, at the base of the mountains.  May you heal the healer, %s.
column2: 往東騎一天的路,在群山腳下你就能找到她。願你治癒那位醫者,%s。

column1: I know you can achieve the gift of Kindness, but you do not yet have a suitable gift of Sacrifice.  Perhaps you will be able to do this another time.
column2: I know you can achieve the gift of Kindness, but you do not yet have a suitable gift of Sacrifice.  Perhaps you will be able to do this another time.

column1: You are obviously enchanted, so we need now to see what is your true form.
column2: 你顯然中了魔法,所以我們得看看你的真面目究竟是什麼。

column1: Water of Wisdom, Liquid of Life, I call upon thee three times three, Mirror without what lies within, So we shall have the true to see.
column2: Water of Wisdom, Liquid of Life, I call upon thee three times three, Mirror without what lies within, So we shall have the true to see.

column1: Look into the water, and we shall see your true form.
column2: 凝視這水面,我們便能看見你的真形。

column1: Ah, the Emir Arus al-Din.  There was good reason why my spell could not locate you in Raseir.
column2: 啊,原來是埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁。難怪我的咒語在拉希爾找不到你,原來是有原因的。

column1: You are under a most powerful enchantment.  The caster must be a very skilled wizard to craft such a spell.
column2: 你身上罩著一道極強的魔咒。能編織出這種法術的施術者,必定是位身手高明的巫師。

column1: Water of Wisdom, Liquid of Life, Knowledge now is what I seek, Reveal the caster of the spell, upon the one which I bespeak.
column2: Water of Wisdom, Liquid of Life, Knowledge now is what I seek, Reveal the caster of the spell, upon the one which I bespeak.

column1: The essence of Fire is hunger.
column2: The essence of Fire is hunger.

column1: As long as there is something nearby to burn, it will be able to move freely.  It can consume the very bricks which make up the floor of the plazas.
column2: 只要附近有東西可燒,它就能自由移動。連鋪在廣場地上的磚塊,它都能一口吞噬。

column1: Until it is contained, it cannot be controlled.
column2: 在它被封住之前,沒人能控制它。

column1: The weakness of Fire is Water, but the Elemental of Fire will avoid water by moving away from its source.
column2: The weakness of Fire is Water, but the Elemental of Fire will avoid water by moving away from its source.

column1: Unless you find some way to drive or lure the Elemental away from the open plazas, where there is much it can burn, to a more confined area, you will not be able to use Water against it to capture it.
column2: Unless you find some way to drive or lure the Elemental away from the open plazas, where there is much it can burn, to a more confined area, you will not be able to use Water against it to capture it.

column1: Once it is captured and contained, it behaves as a magical flame.
column2: 一旦被捕獲封住,它就會變成一團魔法之火,乖乖聽話。

column1: The essence of Water is attraction.
column2: The essence of Water is attraction.

column1: The Elemental will draw all water into itself.  It can draw water from the very body of a human that approaches it.
column2: 這元素生物會把所有的水都吸進自己體內。連走近它的人,身上的水分都會被它抽乾。

column1: In the fountain, the Water Elemental will draw upon the boundless magical stream which is the lifeblood of the city.
column2: In the fountain, the Water Elemental will draw upon the boundless magical stream which is the lifeblood of the city.

column1: People will be dying of thirst as it grows in power enough to engulf the entire city in a flood.
column2: People will be dying of thirst as it grows in power enough to engulf the entire city in a flood.

column1: As long as the Water Elemental is in the fountain, the Elemental cannot be harmed or contained.  It is only vulnerable to Air.
column2: As long as the Water Elemental is in the fountain, the Elemental cannot be harmed or contained.  It is only vulnerable to Air.

column1: Once away from the fountain, it will be drawn to the nearest source of water.  You might then contain it.
column2: Once away from the fountain, it will be drawn to the nearest source of water.  You might then contain it.

column1: The essence of Air is freedom of movement.
column2: The essence of Air is freedom of movement.

column1: As long as there is a place for Air to go, it will continue to grow in destructive power.  Only when it is bound to the Earth can it be contained.
column2: As long as there is a place for Air to go, it will continue to grow in destructive power.  Only when it is bound to the Earth can it be contained.

column1: You should ask someone better versed than I in the nature of Air how to go about this.
column2: You should ask someone better versed than I in the nature of Air how to go about this.

column1: The essence of Earth is strength.
column2: The essence of Earth is strength.

column1: It must be worn away before it can be captured, and it is only vulnerable to flame.
column2: 它必須先被磨耗削弱才能捕獲,而它唯一的弱點就是火焰。

column1: It might be best contained in the type of cloth sack used to haul sand in this city.
column2: 要封住它,最好是用這城裡那種搬運沙子的布袋。

column1: I will tell you of a time long ago when women wore veils and were but wives and chattel to men.
column2: 我要跟你說一段很久以前的往事,那時候女人都蒙著面紗,不過是男人的妻子與財物罷了。

column1: There was a woman -- a beautiful woman, a loving woman -- who wished with her very soul to become a Healer, and thus aid others.
column2: There was a woman -- a beautiful woman, a loving woman -- who wished with her very soul to become a Healer, and thus aid others.

column1: Her father tried to dissuade her, saying that this was no task for a woman of her gentleness and sensitivity.  She would go about her duties quietly, but each evening she would tell him again that she wished to become a Healer.
column2: Her father tried to dissuade her, saying that this was no task for a woman of her gentleness and sensitivity.  She would go about her duties quietly, but each evening she would tell him again that she wished to become a Healer.

column1: At last her father gave in, and she was trained in the art of healing, despite the jeers and condescension of the male students and teachers.  She became a Healer, and removed her veil forever.
column2: At last her father gave in, and she was trained in the art of healing, despite the jeers and condescension of the male students and teachers.  She became a Healer, and removed her veil forever.

column1: She soon discovered that life was not easy for a woman Healer.  Many men distrusted her skills and refused to call upon her.  Some men called upon her only to try to take advantage of her beauty.
column2: She soon discovered that life was not easy for a woman Healer.  Many men distrusted her skills and refused to call upon her.  Some men called upon her only to try to take advantage of her beauty.

column1: One day, a man came to her and told the tale of his brother dying in the desert.
column2: One day, a man came to her and told the tale of his brother dying in the desert.

column1: She did not quite trust his manner of looking at her, but she could not refuse aid to one who might need it.  She followed him out of the city and into the desert.  She was led to an encampment of ragged tents.
column2: She did not quite trust his manner of looking at her, but she could not refuse aid to one who might need it.  She followed him out of the city and into the desert.  She was led to an encampment of ragged tents.

column1: She soon learned to her sorrow that the tale of the brother was but a lie.  These were brigands, and what they did with her, I shall not say.
column2: She soon learned to her sorrow that the tale of the brother was but a lie.  These were brigands, and what they did with her, I shall not say.

column1: She tried to escape them the next day, and ran to the mountains to hide.  They were swift to follow.
column2: She tried to escape them the next day, and ran to the mountains to hide.  They were swift to follow.

column1: They had nearly recaptured her when she called aloud for help.  A Djinni heard her cry, and transformed her into a tree.
column2: They had nearly recaptured her when she called aloud for help.  A Djinni heard her cry, and transformed her into a tree.

column1: She stands now near the mountains, her back turned away from mankind.  She has rejected all that she was:  human, woman, Healer.
column2: She stands now near the mountains, her back turned away from mankind.  She has rejected all that she was:  human, woman, Healer.

column1: She would be a source for the "Fruit of Compassion", but she no longer has any compassion.  A man ruined her, and only a man can restore her.
column2: She would be a source for the "Fruit of Compassion", but she no longer has any compassion.  A man ruined her, and only a man can restore her.

column1: There are three things you must give her to save her soul: a gift of Kindness, a gift of Magic, and a gift of Love.
column2: There are three things you must give her to save her soul: a gift of Kindness, a gift of Magic, and a gift of Love.

column1: The gift of Kindness is simple.  It is only what you would give to someone who has been in the desert far too long.
column2: The gift of Kindness is simple.  It is only what you would give to someone who has been in the desert far too long.

column1: You must then tell her about yourself, remind her what it is to be human.  Your words are as important as the gift, for they give meaning to the act.
column2: 接著你必須向她講述你自己,提醒她身為一個人是什麼滋味。你的話語和禮物同等重要,因為是它們賦予了這個舉動意義。

column1: The gift of Magic is the gift of sacrifice.  The gift must be something you gained through hardship and from aiding others.  It must also be something from which a plant can gain strength.
column2: The gift of Magic is the gift of sacrifice.  The gift must be something you gained through hardship and from aiding others.  It must also be something from which a plant can gain strength.

column1: Tell her then how you got the gift and why.  You will be reminding her of why she became a healer.
column2: 然後告訴她你是怎麼得到這份禮物的、又是為了什麼。你會藉此提醒她,當初她為何要成為一名醫者。

column1: The last gift again is simple, a mere gesture of affection.  Remind her that there are still people in the world who care about others.
column2: 最後一份禮物同樣簡單,不過是一個表達情意的舉動。提醒她,這世上仍有人懂得關心別人。

column1: There is only one thing you need to say to her now, to remind her who she is.  Her name is Julanar, "Flower of the Pomegranate."
column2: 現在你只需要對她說一句話,提醒她自己是誰。她的名字是茱拉娜爾,意思是「石榴之花」。

column1: When you have done this, your part in her tale will be complete.  Julanar will not be able to walk this land as a human woman until another frees her with the power of True Love, but you can at least free her spirit by giving her these gifts.
column2: When you have done this, your part in her tale will be complete.  Julanar will not be able to walk this land as a human woman until another frees her with the power of True Love, but you can at least free her spirit by giving her these gifts.

column1: There are four basic elements which make up the universe:  Water, Earth, Air, and Fire.
column2: There are four basic elements which make up the universe:  Water, Earth, Air, and Fire.

column1: An Elemental is formed when Magic shapes an element into a semblance of a living creature.
column2: An Elemental is formed when Magic shapes an element into a semblance of a living creature.

column1: Elementals are very powerful and destructive.  They can be weakened by the contrary Element, but they cannot be destroyed.
column2: Elementals are very powerful and destructive.  They can be weakened by the contrary Element, but they cannot be destroyed.

column1: Once weakened, the Elemental can be captured.  Once captured, the magical nature of the Elemental can be used for helpful rather than harmful purposes.
column2: 一旦被削弱,元素生物就能被捕獲。一旦被捕獲,它那股魔法本質便能化害為利,為人所用。

column1: You don't have the equipment to do that.
column2: You don't have the equipment to do that.

column1: There is no response.
column2: 毫無回應。

column1: Welcome, %s
column2: Welcome, %s

column1: Perhaps you have learned some manners since the last time you came calling.  That would be better.  Who are you that now seeks entrance to my house?
column2: 也許你上回造訪之後,學會了些禮數。那樣就好多了。如今想進我家的,究竟是何人?

column1: Who is it that seeks to enter?
column2: 是誰想要進來?

column1: Perhaps you should return when you have learned your name and some manners.
column2: 等你弄清自己的名字、學會點規矩,再回來吧。

column1: You have not answered my question.  Who are you that knocks upon my door?
column2: 你還沒回答我的問題。敲我門的,究竟是何人?

column1: Who is it that sent you here?
column2: 是誰派你來這兒的?

column1: How did the leg of Rakeesh get injured?
column2: How did the leg of Rakeesh get injured?

column1: What is Ja'afar's most crucial role for Omar?
column2: What is Ja'afar's most crucial role for Omar?

column1: What is the Element most appropriate to the owner of the Magic Shop?
column2: What is the Element most appropriate to the owner of the Magic Shop?

column1: Which of the elements do the Wizards of WIT command?
column2: Which of the elements do the Wizards of WIT command?

column1: What is the companion of Erasmus?
column2: What is the companion of Erasmus?

column1: Perhaps you should learn more about who sent you before you seek me.
column2: 在來找我之前,你或許該先弄清楚究竟是誰派你來的。

column1: I do not see strangers who come unbidden.
column2: 不請自來的生人,我概不接見。

column1: My first is the first, My second the last, Next comes Myself, Then back to the end, And beginning again. Who am I?
column2: My first is the first, My second the last, Next comes Myself, Then back to the end, And beginning again. Who am I?

column1: If you know not whom you seek, you will surely not find that person here.
column2: 你若連自己要找誰都不知道,在這兒是絕對找不著那人的。

column1: You may enter freely into my home, %s.
column2: 你可以自由進出我的家,%s。

column1: I do not welcome those who are rude in my house.
column2: 我不歡迎在我家裡無禮的人。

column1: You find that you cannot move.
column2: 你發現自己動彈不得。

column1: I do not welcome those who try to force their entrance into my house.
column2: 我不歡迎硬要闖進我家的人。

column1: That was exhausting.  You don't think you'd survive going through that again.
column2: 真是累壞了。你覺得自己再來一次恐怕撐不住。

column1: It is best not to anger a powerful Magic User.
column2: It is best not to anger a powerful Magic User.

column1: Persistence pays off, but this certainly isn't the payoff you had in mind!
column2: 堅持是有回報的,只不過這回報八成不是你想要的那種!

column1: Next time use your head... or maybe your knuckles!
column2: 下次動動腦子吧……或者,動動指關節敲門也行!

column1: There is a magical aura around the door.
column2: 門的四周縈繞著一股魔法氣息。

column1: As you gaze about the home of the Astrologer, you are reminded of a small boy's playroom, bunk bed, toys, and all.
column2: As you gaze about the home of the Astrologer, you are reminded of a small boy's playroom, bunk bed, toys, and all.

column1: You have lived a most interesting life.
column2: 你過著一段極其精彩的人生。

column1: I will look into what signs have guided your life thus far, and see how they will affect you in the future.
column2: 我會查查迄今引領你人生的是哪些星象,再看它們將如何影響你的未來。

column1: This will take many hours, so you will need to come back some other day.
column2: 這得花上好幾個鐘頭,你得改天再來。

column1: As I have said - you have lived a most interesting life.
column2: As I have said - you have lived a most interesting life.

column1: Thank you, a thousand thank-yous.  It is with your support that I may continue my vital work
column2: 謝謝你,千恩萬謝。全靠你的支持,我才能繼續這要緊的工作。

column1: You realize you don't have even a full Dinar to give.
column2: You realize you don't have even a full Dinar to give.

column1: It is my pleasure and my purpose to serve the ways of knowledge.
column2: 侍奉知識之道,是我的樂事,也是我的本分。

column1: Come in, come in, Hasan, come in.
column2: 進來,進來,哈山,快進來。

column1: Oh, that is not you, is it?  It is hard for me to tell at such a distance.
column2: 喔,原來不是你呀?隔這麼遠,我實在看不太清楚。

column1: Come in anyway, and welcome.  I am Abu Al-Njun and I am the "Astrologer of the Stars and Omens."
column2: Come in anyway, and welcome.  I am Abu Al-Njun and I am the "Astrologer of the Stars and Omens."

column1: Who are you that I might know you?
column2: 請問尊姓大名,讓我也好認識認識?

column1: It is good that you returned; there is much I need to tell you.
column2: 你回來得正好,我有許多話要告訴你。

column1: Alas, never before have I read such troubling signs as those which guide you.  Your future is very dark.
column2: 唉,我從未讀到過像指引你命運這般令人憂心的星象。你的未來,一片黑暗哪。

column1: Welcome.  May the stars smile upon you.
column2: 歡迎。願群星向你微笑。

column1: There are omens of fire throughout the sky, and many portents of destruction.  Now, what is it that you wish to know this bright day?
column2: 滿天都是烈火的徵兆,以及種種毀滅的預示。那麼,在這晴朗的一天,你想知道些什麼呢?

column1: A sign of wind rises in the east and the city is like a leaf before it.  How is it that I may help you?
column2: 東方升起了「風」之星象,這座城就像它面前的一片落葉。我能幫你什麼忙呢?

column1: I have seen signs of heavy, impending doom.  Now how else can I make your day?
column2: 我看見了沉重而迫近的厄運之兆。那麼,我還能為你做點什麼,讓你今天開心些呢?

column1: Water rules this day, both lack of it, and the surfeit of it.  You should either invest heavily in wine or waterwings.
column2: Water rules this day, both lack of it, and the surfeit of it.  You should either invest heavily in wine or waterwings.

column1: There will be a great rise in celebrations today, followed by a rapid decline tomorrow.  What else can I do for you?
column2: 今天慶典之事將大為興盛,而明天則會急轉直下。我還能為你做點什麼呢?

column1: What might I call you?
column2: What might I call you?

column1: I must know who you are before I can be of service to you.  What is your name that I might call you?
column2: 我得先知道你是誰,才能為你效勞。你叫什麼名字,好讓我稱呼你?

column1: What an interesting name, %s.  It certainly isn't one I would have expected you to bear.
column2: 真是個有趣的名字,%s。這可不是我料想得到你會頂著的名號。

column1: Ah, %s.  It is indeed a name for heroes.
column2: 啊,%s。這的確是個屬於英雄的名字。

column1: It is well that you have come, then.  The Signs show that we will soon need one here.
column2: It is well that you have come, then.  The Signs show that we will soon need one here.

column1: Just a moment.  I'm doing a very important derivation right now.
column2: 稍等一下。我現在正在做一道非常重要的推算。

column1: Let's see now, if the moon is in the seventh house (mumble, mumble)...
column2: 讓我想想,假如月亮落在第七宮(喃喃,喃喃)……

column1: ... hmm... and Jupiter aligns with Mars (mumble, mumble)...
column2: ……嗯……再加上木星與火星連成一線(喃喃,喃喃)……

column1: ... then peace shall guide our planet and love ...?!
column2: ... then peace shall guide our planet and love ...?!

column1: Naw, it'll never happen.
column2: 算了吧,這種事絕不可能發生。

column1: Now what was it you needed to know?
column2: 好,你剛剛是想知道什麼來著?

column1: If you need to know more about the future, visit me again when it arrives.
column2: 你若想多知道些未來的事,等未來到了,再來找我吧。

column1: I have told you all I presently know of your future.  However, I predict that I will know far more in the future.
column2: 關於你的未來,我目前所知的都已經告訴你了。不過我預言,將來我會知道得更多。

column1: If you wish to know your fortune, I will need to know your guiding signs.
column2: 你若想知道自己的命運,我得先知道你的主宰星象。

column1: Tell me about your past and the things you have done.
column2: 跟我說說你的過往,還有你做過的事。

column1: I have not yet had time to find your answers.
column2: 我還沒來得及替你找到答案。

column1: You must return another day.
column2: 你得改天再來一趟。

column1: The portents foretell you will be deceived by darkness and become its pawn in the destruction of all this world.
column2: The portents foretell you will be deceived by darkness and become its pawn in the destruction of all this world.

column1: Perhaps it would be wise to just hide under a rock for a few days or so.
column2: 或許躲到石頭底下避個幾天,才是明智之舉。

column1: Darkness will lead you into a tomb.  You will walk in fire, earth, water, and air, through temptation, and at last into despair and desolation.
column2: 黑暗將領你步入墳塚。你將行經火、土、水、風,穿越誘惑,最終墮入絕望與荒蕪。

column1: Darkness will triumph unless you stop the last light.
column2: 除非你阻止最後一道光熄滅,否則黑暗終將得勝。

column1: Maybe you should consider a new line of work and get out of this Hero business.  Have you considered accounting?
column2: Maybe you should consider a new line of work and get out of this Hero business.  Have you considered accounting?

column1: Please, I would prefer if you do not go up there.  That leads to my observatory on the roof where I keep very delicate instruments.
column2: 拜託,我希望你別往上頭去。那兒通往屋頂的天文台,我放了好些極為精密的儀器。

column1: The stars have very little to say about that.
column2: 關於那件事,群星沒什麼好說的。

column1: You know, I have never seen the stars in that particular configuration.
column2: 你知道嗎,我從沒見過群星排成那種奇特的格局。

column1: The Eye of the Shadow is over the city.  The Dark Hand is moving toward us.  There will be misfortune here shortly.
column2: 「影之眼」高懸城上。「黑手」正朝我們逼近。不久之後,此地將有災厄降臨。

column1: The Dark Hand is over Raseir now.  Its ruling planet has been missing since it passed across the Dragon.
column2: 「黑手」如今籠罩著拉希爾。自從它掠過「龍」之後,主宰它的那顆行星便消失無蹤了。

column1: The Djinn are ruled by the Moon.  In a short while, the Moon will pass between the Scorpion's claws and the Dragon's teeth.  The Moon will be weakened, and the Djinn made strong.
column2: The Djinn are ruled by the Moon.  In a short while, the Moon will pass between the Scorpion's claws and the Dragon's teeth.  The Moon will be weakened, and the Djinn made strong.

column1: The planet Lahib is ascending.  A Fire Elemental will gain more strength as it rises.
column2: 拉希卜行星正在上升。隨著它升起,火元素將獲得更強的力量。

column1: A Water Elemental at this time would be deadly.  The Dragon is high, and all things magical in nature are more powerful.
column2: 此刻若出現水元素,那可是致命的。「龍」高懸天際,凡帶有魔法本質的事物都會更加強大。

column1: The Wind sign approaches the city.  You would do well to avoid tornadoes today.
column2: 「風」之星座正逼近這座城市。今天你最好離龍捲風遠一點。

column1: Watch for falling rocks.
column2: 小心落石。

column1: Elementals are things of magic and thus are ruled by the Dragon.
column2: 元素生物本是魔法之物,因此受「龍」所主宰。

column1: Magic ebbs and flows with the positions of the stars.
column2: 魔法隨著群星的位置而消長起落。

column1: The Sultan (May His Name Be Printed On The Stars!) consults me regularly.
column2: The Sultan (May His Name Be Printed On The Stars!) consults me regularly.

column1: The sign of the Emir Arus Al-Din got crossed with that of the Saurus.  He has been missing ever since.
column2: The sign of the Emir Arus Al-Din got crossed with that of the Saurus.  He has been missing ever since.

column1: I visit the Palace each morning to tell of the night's events.
column2: 我每天清晨都會進宮,稟報昨夜星象所示之事。

column1: I will inform the Caravan Master when the omens are auspicious for the caravan.
column2: 等徵兆顯示商隊出行吉利時,我自會通知商隊隊長。

column1: I depend upon the generosity of my clients to maintain my lifestyle.  If you wish to pay me for the knowledge I have shared with you, I would be most grateful.
column2: 我這日子全仰賴主顧們的慷慨打賞。倘若你願意為我分享的這些學問付點酬勞,我可是感激不盡。

column1: The prediction for this night is mostly darkness with occasional light toward morning.
column2: 今夜的預報是:大致漆黑一片,近天明時偶有微光。

column1: I talk to the stars, and the things they tell are more than mere rumor.
column2: 我與群星對話,它們所告知的可不只是道聽塗說。

column1: I have been stargazing since I was little.  There is so much to learn from astrology.
column2: 我從小就開始觀星。占星之術,可有學不完的學問哪。

column1: The sign of the Katta is visible just after sunset.
column2: 卡塔族的星座,在日落之後便清晰可見。

column1: I am Abu al-Njun.
column2: 我是阿布·納瓊。

column1: I can only tell you what the stars say.  You are the one who has to figure out what they mean.
column2: 我只能告訴你群星所言。至於那是什麼意思,得靠你自己去參透。

column1: The stars predict that only a great hero may aid us through the upcoming darkness.
column2: 群星預示:唯有一位偉大的英雄,方能助我們渡過即將到來的黑暗。

column1: I have seen signs of great destruction over our city.  These are most disturbing.  It might be a good time to take a vacation.
column2: 我看見了預示本城將遭大難的星象。實在令人不安。眼下,或許正是出門度個假的好時機。

column1: The planet Salim has been missing from the sky for several days now.  Last night, the planet Amal was gone.  Instead, a large, fiery star blazed from the eye of the Dragon.  Even now, the Dragon and the Scorpion are rising.
column2: 薩林行星已經從天空消失好幾天了。昨夜,連阿瑪爾行星也不見了蹤影,取而代之的,是一顆碩大如火的星辰,從「龍」之眼處熊熊燃燒。即便此刻,「龍」與「蠍」也正冉冉升起。

column1: The planet Salim is a sign of Safety.  Amal is the ruling planet of Hope.  With those missing from the sky, we are indeed in trouble.  Make sure you keep in touch with your insurance agent.
column2: The planet Salim is a sign of Safety.  Amal is the ruling planet of Hope.  With those missing from the sky, we are indeed in trouble.  Make sure you keep in touch with your insurance agent.

column1: The Dragon is the sign of vast magical power.
column2: 「龍」是浩瀚魔力的徵兆。

column1: The Scorpion portends poison and deceit.
column2: 「蠍」預示著毒害與欺詐。

column1: My job is to reveal the future to those who seek it.  Perhaps you wish to know your own fortune.
column2: 我的本行,就是為求問者揭示未來。也許你也想知道自己的命運如何?

column1: These guides to future actions are everywhere in the sky.  The secret is to understand their meaning.
column2: 這些指引未來行動的徵兆,佈滿了整片天空。訣竅就在於——讀懂它們的含意。

column1: The relationships of the stars influence our lives constantly.
column2: 群星之間的關係,無時無刻不影響著我們的人生。

column1: Astrology is based on the principle of: "As above, So below."  The Heavens reflect the Earth, and vice versa.
column2: Astrology is based on the principle of: "As above, So below."  The Heavens reflect the Earth, and vice versa.

column1: They are small, burning objects which move constantly in the night sky.
column2: 它們是一些細小燃燒的物體,在夜空中不停移動。

column1: The stars which shape the Hand signify action.  When the Hand is dark, it means oppression.
column2: 構成「手」這個星象的群星,象徵著行動。而當「手」轉暗,那便意味著壓迫。

column1: The Planet known as the Eye has crossed the sign of the Shadow.  The Shadow is watching us, and who knows what evil lurks?  I sure don't.
column2: The Planet known as the Eye has crossed the sign of the Shadow.  The Shadow is watching us, and who knows what evil lurks?  I sure don't.

column1: Constellations are wonderful cars, but I personally prefer Novas and Galaxies.
column2: 「星座」牌的車子是不錯啦,不過我個人還是偏愛「新星」跟「銀河」這兩款。

column1: Hasan is my lens washer.  Wonderful boy, but a bit convexing at times.
column2: 哈山是我的鏡片清潔工。是個好孩子,只是有時候腦筋有點「凸」槌。

column1: Personally, I don't subscribe to that particular theory.
column2: 就我個人而言,我並不認同那套說法。

column1: Avoid it whenever possible.
column2: 能避就避,千萬別碰。

column1: Actually, my stockbroker gave me the chart you see on the wall.
column2: 說實話,你看牆上那張圖,是我的股票經紀人送的。

column1: I never was very good at those things.
column2: 那些東西我向來不太在行。

column1: What a beauty, eh?  I love being up high, nearer the stars.
column2: 美吧?我就愛待在高處,離星辰更近些。

column1: The orrery works on magnetic principles.  It was told to me that it describes an obscure system, containing such planets as Xenon, Ortega, Pestulon, Phleebhut, and Uranus.  I don't know what to think about Uranus.
column2: 這台太陽系儀靠磁力原理運轉。有人告訴我,它描繪的是一個冷僻的星系,裡頭有像氙星、歐特加、佩斯圖隆、弗利布特,還有天王星這些行星。天王星嘛……我實在不知該怎麼看待它。

column1: The casting of darts is the Astrologer's version of the Scientific Method.
column2: The casting of darts is the Astrologer's version of the Scientific Method.

column1: You may go no further.
column2: 你不得再往前一步。

column1: Testing...
column2: Testing...

column1: To our regret, she has not answered our summons for many years.  You must choose another sponsor.
column2: 很遺憾,她已多年未回應我們的召喚。你必須另選一位舉薦人。

column1: Zara Shashina, Wizard of the Eastern Woods, Emissary of the Faerie Folk:  do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocracy?
column2: 東方森林之巫師、精靈族使者薩拉·夏希娜:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: Outside of the few spells I gave him, I have no knowledge of his skills or abilities.  I will not accept responsibility for him.
column2: 除了我教他的那幾道法術之外,我對他的技能和能耐一無所知。我不會為他擔負這份責任。

column1: You must choose another sponsor.
column2: 你必須另選一位舉薦人。

column1: Aziza, Enchantress of Shapeir, Wizard of Water:  do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 夏皮爾的女術士、水之巫師阿姿莎:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: I cannot pass judgement upon the candidate until I have worked with him for some months.  I am unwilling to have someone I do not know well as my initiate.
column2: I cannot pass judgement upon the candidate until I have worked with him for some months.  I am unwilling to have someone I do not know well as my initiate.

column1: Trismagister Merlyn Ambrosius, Wizard of the Isle of Gramarye:  do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 三重大師梅林·安布羅修斯,格拉瑪萊島之巫師:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: Have we met before?  No, I don't believe so.  Sorry.
column2: 我們以前見過嗎?不,我想沒有。抱歉。

column1: Ad Avis, Wizard of the Southern Sands, Enchanter of the Elements:  do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 南方沙漠之巫師、元素的施法者阿德·阿維斯:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: You hear a man's voice, deep and sonorous.
column2: 你聽見一個男人低沉而洪亮的嗓音。

column1: So, a Want-to-Be Wizard seeks me for a master?  I am not interested in half-trained novices.
column2: So, a Want-to-Be Wizard seeks me for a master?  I am not interested in half-trained novices.

column1: However, should you actually become a Wizard, you might be of use to me.  There is much I can teach you.
column2: However, should you actually become a Wizard, you might be of use to me.  There is much I can teach you.

column1: Until that time, find someone else to paper-train you.
column2: 在那之前,另找個人來幫你打基礎吧。

column1: Houdini, Prestidigitator Paramount of far-away lands, Master of Escape:  do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 胡迪尼,遠方異域的至尊幻術師、脫逃大師:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: You hear a man's distant voice, apparently under great stress.
column2: 你聽見一個男人遙遠的聲音,顯然正承受著極大的壓力。

column1: Mmmmph!  I'm (gasp) a bit tied up (m-m-mmmmph!) at the moment, uh... uh...  (ack!)  The name escapes me.  Now, you really MUST excuse... (choke)... me.
column2: 唔唔!我(喘)現在有點……脫不開身(嗚嗚嗚!),呃……呃……(啊!)名字一時想不起來。現在,你真的得讓我先……(嗆)……失陪了。

column1: There is a moment of silence.
column2: 一陣沉默。

column1: Do you actually request such a ... whimsical Wizard to be your sponsor?
column2: Do you actually request such a ... whimsical Wizard to be your sponsor?

column1: It was the choice he made.
column2: 這是他自己的選擇。

column1: But you know what Erasmus is like.  Do we have to endure his sense of levity?
column2: 可你也知道伊拉斯謨那德性。我們非得忍受他那一套輕浮的玩笑不可嗎?

column1: It was the initiate's decision.  Let him abide with it.
column2: 這是入門弟子自己的決定。就讓他承擔吧。

column1: Very well.
column2: 好吧。

column1: Magister Erasmus of Zauberberg, Archmage Extraordinaire:  do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 札烏柏格的伊拉斯謨大師、非凡的大法師:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: You hear the familiar voice of Erasmus.
column2: 你聽見伊拉斯謨熟悉的嗓音。

column1: Let me see... %s... Ah, yes.  The young Hero who was so bright.
column2: Let me see... %s... Ah, yes.  The young Hero who was so bright.

column1: You hear the squeaky voice of Fenrus.
column2: 你聽見芬魯斯尖細的嗓音。

column1: You think anyone who laughs at one of your jokes to be intelligent.
column2: 凡是會被你的笑話逗笑的人,你都覺得他聰明。

column1: A good sense of humor is an excellent indicator of superior intellect.
column2: 良好的幽默感,正是過人智慧的絕佳指標。

column1: Yes, but the keyword is "good."  Heh, heh.
column2: Yes, but the keyword is "good."  Heh, heh.

column1: Erasmus!  Do you or do you not accept %s?
column2: 伊拉斯謨!你到底接不接受 %s?

column1: I, Magister Erasmus of Zauberberg, do hereby accept %s as my initiate for WIT.
column2: 我,札烏柏格的伊拉斯謨大師,在此正式接受 %s 成為我在 WIT 的入門弟子。

column1: Wizard's Institute of Technocery.
column2: 巫師技術學院。

column1: Of course.  Now, Hero of Spielburg, before you can be initiated into WIT...
column2: 當然。好了,史畢柏格的英雄,在你正式入門 WIT 之前……

column1: ... Wizard's Institute of Technocery, you must first prove you are magically and mentally worthy.  You will be presented with a choice of three objects.  Using the Spells at your command, you must chose the correct object, move it to the stand, and cause it to ring using only three spells.  The choice of the first two spells should be self-evident, and the third is also...
column2: ... Wizard's Institute of Technocery, you must first prove you are magically and mentally worthy.  You will be presented with a choice of three objects.  Using the Spells at your command, you must chose the correct object, move it to the stand, and cause it to ring using only three spells.  The choice of the first two spells should be self-evident, and the third is also...

column1: Erasmus!
column2: Erasmus!

column1: Oh, all right. Well, good luck, young hero, and remember the joke about the man who grew more mellow as he grew balder ...
column2: Oh, all right. Well, good luck, young hero, and remember the joke about the man who grew more mellow as he grew balder ...

column1: He no longer had hair-brained schemes?
column2: 因為他不再有那些毛躁的餿主意(hair-brained)了?

column1: No, he no longer had such a hair-triggered temper.
column2: 不,是因為他不再那麼一觸即發(hair-triggered)了。

column1: Very well then, young hero, please step into the dark room.  Purely a formality.  Don't worry about a thing.
column2: Very well then, young hero, please step into the dark room.  Purely a formality.  Don't worry about a thing.

column1: That is not an appropriate sponsor.
column2: 那不是個合適的引薦人。

column1: How rude.
column2: 真沒禮貌。

column1: He doesn't seem to be house trained.
column2: 他似乎還沒學會規矩。

column1: I say we scatter him to the four winds.
column2: 我說,把他打散到四面八方去吧。

column1: No, he's merely young and impetuous.
column2: 不,他只是年輕氣盛罷了。

column1: Begone!  You may return when you have learned to behave.
column2: 退下!等你學會規矩,再回來吧。

column1: You have entered a very strange place  The path you walk upon seems to be carved from ivory.  The walls around you seem carved of marble.
column2: You have entered a very strange place  The path you walk upon seems to be carved from ivory.  The walls around you seem carved of marble.

column1: Between the walls and the path is a gap through which you view total darkness when you look down.  When you look up, you see the same limitless black.
column2: Between the walls and the path is a gap through which you view total darkness when you look down.  When you look up, you see the same limitless black.

column1: You have a funny feeling you aren't in Shapeir anymore.
column2: You have a funny feeling you aren't in Shapeir anymore.

column1: You have been walking through halls hung with the portraits of many Wizards.  At last, you come to some pictures that you recognize.
column2: You have been walking through halls hung with the portraits of many Wizards.  At last, you come to some pictures that you recognize.

column1: You hear several voices.
column2: You hear several voices.

column1: Who are you that enters the lofty portals of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 踏入巫師技術學院巍峨門戶的你,究竟是何人?

column1: Why have you sought to enter into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 你為何尋求進入巫師技術學院?

column1: Who do you call upon to be your sponsor and mentor in these hallowed halls of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 在這巫師技術學院的神聖殿堂裡,你要請誰當你的引薦人與導師?

column1: You have shown yourself to be resourceful in your use of spells under simple conditions.  To become a Wizard, you must prove that you are capable of using magic under time-critical and life-threatening conditions.
column2: You have shown yourself to be resourceful in your use of spells under simple conditions.  To become a Wizard, you must prove that you are capable of using magic under time-critical and life-threatening conditions.

column1: However, we see that you do not yet have knowledge of all the spells required.  Go then, and master the spells available to you.  Return when you think yourself ready.
column2: 然而,我們看出你尚未習得所有必要的法術。那就去吧,把你已能取得的法術都練到精通。等你自認準備妥當,再回來。

column1: You will be presented with a series of four tests.  They will require all your intelligence and magic.  Should you fail, your very life may be endangered.  Will you accept the challenge and walk the path of Wizardry?
column2: You will be presented with a series of four tests.  They will require all your intelligence and magic.  Should you fail, your very life may be endangered.  Will you accept the challenge and walk the path of Wizardry?

column1: Then you may leave, mere user of magic.
column2: 那你可以走了,區區一個耍魔法的。

column1: You have proven yourself worthy of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery and Erasmus' faith.  Congratulations.  You are now a Wizard Initiate.
column2: You have proven yourself worthy of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery and Erasmus' faith.  Congratulations.  You are now a Wizard Initiate.

column1: Now you have outgrown the childish Hero business and can devote yourself to years of metaphysical musings and esoteric exercises designed to invigorate your intellect and to manipulate your mental and magical muscles.
column2: Now you have outgrown the childish Hero business and can devote yourself to years of metaphysical musings and esoteric exercises designed to invigorate your intellect and to manipulate your mental and magical muscles.

column1: You have passed this day from the lesser mortal plane into the Realm of Wizards.  You are now qualified to begin your post-graduate magical training.  In fifteen or twenty years, you will be ready to return to the world of mere men.  Until that time, you will bask in the sunshine and security of academia, knowing you shall receive the best of all possible educations.
column2: You have passed this day from the lesser mortal plane into the Realm of Wizards.  You are now qualified to begin your post-graduate magical training.  In fifteen or twenty years, you will be ready to return to the world of mere men.  Until that time, you will bask in the sunshine and security of academia, knowing you shall receive the best of all possible educations.

column1: Ahead of you are the wonders of Wizardry.  Behind you are all the petty problems inherent in the mundane land.  You have passed beyond those weaklings who depended upon your skills and dared to call you "friend."  You will now learn the real Magic of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery -- knowledge gained through careful study.
column2: Ahead of you are the wonders of Wizardry.  Behind you are all the petty problems inherent in the mundane land.  You have passed beyond those weaklings who depended upon your skills and dared to call you "friend."  You will now learn the real Magic of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery -- knowledge gained through careful study.

column1: Walk away from the world of those fools who claim they need you.  You are more than just a Hero now.  You are a scholar!  You have only to take the oath that you will ignore and forget about those who said they needed you in the land of Shapeir and devote yourself to the improvement of your mind and magic.  Will you take the oath?
column2: Walk away from the world of those fools who claim they need you.  You are more than just a Hero now.  You are a scholar!  You have only to take the oath that you will ignore and forget about those who said they needed you in the land of Shapeir and devote yourself to the improvement of your mind and magic.  Will you take the oath?

column1: Welcome to the Wizard's Institute of Technocery, Wizard.
column2: Welcome to the Wizard's Institute of Technocery, Wizard.

column1: You walk the hallowed halls of the ivory towers of WIT and devote the next twenty years of your life to learning.  You become one of the greatest scholars of all time upon the subject of "Scrying Scrolls."
column2: You walk the hallowed halls of the ivory towers of WIT and devote the next twenty years of your life to learning.  You become one of the greatest scholars of all time upon the subject of "Scrying Scrolls."

column1: You never did return to Shapeir again, because, for some reason the WIT Wizards were unable to explain, the door to that land became permanently closed.  They believe it had something to do with the land being destroyed.
column2: 你再也沒能回到夏皮爾,因為基於某種連巫師技術學院的巫師也解釋不了的緣故,通往那片土地的門永遠關閉了。他們相信,這與那片土地的毀滅有關。
