column1: Well, it is obvious you think being a Hero is more important than being a scholar.  Perhaps someday when you have matured a bit, you will comprehend the enormity of the education you have ignored!  The Wizard's Institute of Technocery is for real Wizards rather than the real world!
column2: Well, it is obvious you think being a Hero is more important than being a scholar.  Perhaps someday when you have matured a bit, you will comprehend the enormity of the education you have ignored!  The Wizard's Institute of Technocery is for real Wizards rather than the real world!

column1: Congratulations, Wizard %s.  You have shown exceptional intelligence by refusing to take their silly oath.  After all, what good is magic or knowledge unless you use it?
column2: 恭喜你,巫師 %s。你拒絕立下那愚蠢的誓言,正展現了你過人的智慧。說到底,魔法與知識若不拿來用,又有什麼價值?

column1: My graduation present for you is the "Reversal" Spell.  You may recall the Kobold's use of it against you in Spielburg.
column2: My graduation present for you is the "Reversal" Spell.  You may recall the Kobold's use of it against you in Spielburg.

column1: Cast Reversal and magical attacks directed at you will rebound to their caster.  But take care -- it will not help against the indirect effects of other spells.
column2: 施展「反轉」,衝著你來的魔法攻擊便會彈回施法者身上。但要當心——它擋不住其他法術的間接效果。

column1: You feel the thrill of knowledge.  Now you know how to cast the Spell of "Reversal."
column2: You feel the thrill of knowledge.  Now you know how to cast the Spell of "Reversal."

column1: You should now return to Shapeir.  They really need a real Wizard-Hero like you there.  And, remember, "Those who can, do."
column2: You should now return to Shapeir.  They really need a real Wizard-Hero like you there.  And, remember, "Those who can, do."

column1: Those that can't, tell others what to do?
column2: 辦不到的人,就只會指使別人怎麼做?

column1: Fenrus, when is a rat not a rat?
column2: 芬魯斯,老鼠什麼時候不是老鼠?

column1: When it turns into its rathole?
column2: When it turns into its rathole?

column1: No, when it's e'rat'icated.  What did the rat say as he fell 12,000 feet straight down?
column2: 不,是牠被「鼠」除的時候。那隻老鼠直直墜下一萬兩千呎時,說了什麼?

column1: It's a long way to tip a ratty?  Or maybe, "Cheese it, I Swiss I hadn't gone this Whey."  Oh, all right, what did the rat say?
column2: It's a long way to tip a ratty?  Or maybe, "Cheese it, I Swiss I hadn't gone this Whey."  Oh, all right, what did the rat say?

column1: I'm not sure either, but I know a way to find out.  Care to volunteer?
column2: 我也不確定,不過我有法子查清楚。想自願試試嗎?

column1: I guess I Bleu it.  I'd Cheddar be Gouda for awhile, right?
column2: I guess I Bleu it.  I'd Cheddar be Gouda for awhile, right?

column1: Right.  At any rate, Wizard-Hero, good luck and farewell!
column2: 好。總之,巫師英雄,祝你好運,再會了!

column1: Walk the path of Wizardry.
column2: Walk the path of Wizardry.

column1: Magister Erasmus of Zauberberg, Archmage Extraordinaire, your would-be initiate has returned.
column2: 紹貝爾格的伊拉斯謨大師,非凡的大法師,您那位準入門弟子回來了。

column1: Very good.  Now, young Hero of Spielburg, remember:  To try and fail there is no shame ...
column2: Very good.  Now, young Hero of Spielburg, remember:  To try and fail there is no shame ...

column1: As long as you can pass the blame?
column2: 只要你能把過錯推給別人?

column1: As long as you improve your aim.
column2: 只要你能改進你的準頭。

column1: Just don't make your excuses lame.  Heh, heh!
column2: Just don't make your excuses lame.  Heh, heh!

column1: Ouch!  My nose!
column2: Ouch!  My nose!

column1: We do not accept liars in our tower.
column2: 我們的塔不容說謊者。

column1: We do not accept intruders in our tower.
column2: 我們的塔不容入侵者。

column1: You find yourself in a strange place.  For some reason, you cannot move your legs, although you see nothing constraining them.  A strange feeling passes through you, as though someone were examining you closely.
column2: You find yourself in a strange place.  For some reason, you cannot move your legs, although you see nothing constraining them.  A strange feeling passes through you, as though someone were examining you closely.

column1: Ah, I see now that you are not magically prepared for this test.  You need to go out in the world and learn a bit more before returning.
column2: Ah, I see now that you are not magically prepared for this test.  You need to go out in the world and learn a bit more before returning.

column1: Nothing happens.
column2: 什麼事也沒發生。

column1: However, before you can be considered a true Wizard, you must pass the trial of Initiation which the wizards of WIT will give you.
column2: However, before you can be considered a true Wizard, you must pass the trial of Initiation which the wizards of WIT will give you.

column1: I warn you, this test will take all of your wits and magical skills in order to succeed.  If you succeed, you will then face ...but I can say no more.
column2: I warn you, this test will take all of your wits and magical skills in order to succeed.  If you succeed, you will then face ...but I can say no more.

column1: Say no more.
column2: 別再說了。

column1: So, Hero, good luck!
column2: 那麼,英雄,祝你好運!

column1: Promises, promises.  You said you would say no more!  (heh, heh)
column2: 說好的呢、說好的呢。你說過不再多說的!(嘿嘿)

column1: Oh, Fenrus!
column2: Oh, Fenrus!

column1: You find yourself trapped between a second and infinity.  In this place, there is no time, and you cannot save it.
column2: You find yourself trapped between a second and infinity.  In this place, there is no time, and you cannot save it.

column1: You may use that spell only once here.  Try another.
column2: 那法術在這兒只能用一次。換一個試試。

column1: As you begin to cast the spell...
column2: 就在你開始施法之際……

column1: You hear the voice of Erasmus echoing in this chamber.
column2: 你聽見伊拉斯謨的嗓音在這間石室裡迴盪。

column1: The spells to cast and their proper order should be obvious.  They are to me.
column2: 該施哪些法術、按什麼順序施,應該很明顯。至少對我來說是。

column1: That's because you made up this test.
column2: 那是因為這測驗本來就是你出的。

column1: At any rate, Hero, you should take some time and think about the situation next time.  Adieu.
column2: 無論如何,英雄,下次你該花點時間好好想想眼前的狀況。再會了。

column1: You find yourself trapped between a second and infinity.  In this place, there is neither time nor space.
column2: You find yourself trapped between a second and infinity.  In this place, there is neither time nor space.

column1: That's almost halfway to Cleveland.
column2: 那都快走到克利夫蘭的半路上了。

column1: There used to be nothing here.  But not any more.
column2: There used to be nothing here.  But not any more.

column1: That's neither here nor there.
column2: 這既不在這兒,也不在那兒(無關緊要)。

column1: A dimensionless, endless void, trapped between space, time, and the restroom of a cheap diner.
column2: 一片無維無盡的虛空,被困在太空、時間,以及某家廉價小餐館的廁所之間。

column1: You've just managed to click on Roger Wilco, who is still floating around, lost in space, time, sequels, and every other dimension, waiting for you to purchase his latest adventure and help him out of his latest bind.
column2: You've just managed to click on Roger Wilco, who is still floating around, lost in space, time, sequels, and every other dimension, waiting for you to purchase his latest adventure and help him out of his latest bind.

column1: You've clicked on a speck of dimensionless void dust.
column2: 你點到了一粒無維無度的虛空塵埃。

column1: The last time you were in a dimensionless void, it was vast.  But this one's only half vast.
column2: 上回你身處無維虛空時,它浩瀚無垠(vast)。可這一片頂多只能算半吊子(half vast)罷了。

column1: You've never seen a dimensionless void this big before.  Not dimension this other void over here.
column2: You've never seen a dimensionless void this big before.  Not dimension this other void over here.

column1: The scenery is awe-inspiring, but unchanging.
column2: 這景象令人嘆為觀止,卻一成不變。

column1: You can't dazzle the creature if it isn't facing you.
column2: You can't dazzle the creature if it isn't facing you.

column1: You lack the concentration to cast that spell.
column2: 你的專注力不足以施那個法術。

column1: Carefully judging the distance between the approaching staff and the path, you decide there isn't enough room for a good-sized hero such as yourself.
column2: Carefully judging the distance between the approaching staff and the path, you decide there isn't enough room for a good-sized hero such as yourself.

column1: Why would you want to do that?
column2: 你為什麼要那樣做？

column1: In this place, you must rely upon only your magic.
column2: 在這個地方,你只能仰賴你的魔法。

column1: You find yourself walking on a narrow pathway leading to the edge of infinity.  You hear only your own footsteps.
column2: You find yourself walking on a narrow pathway leading to the edge of infinity.  You hear only your own footsteps.

column1: You must now withstand the fury of my flames.
column2: 現在,給我承受烈焰之怒吧!

column1: You must now overcome the subtle strengths of water and ice.
column2: 現在,你必須克服水與冰那難以察覺的力量。

column1: You must now face raging earth and stone.
column2: 現在,你必須面對狂暴的土石。

column1: You must now stand into the wind.
column2: 現在,你必須迎風而立。

column1: Well, it LOOKED easy enough to climb!
column2: Well, it LOOKED easy enough to climb!

column1: It's too slippery to climb.
column2: 太滑了,爬不上去。

column1: We see that you haven't enough magical power remaining to finish Our Tests.  We dismiss you from Our Presence.
column2: We see that you haven't enough magical power remaining to finish Our Tests.  We dismiss you from Our Presence.

column1: Are you CRAZY?
column2: Are you CRAZY?

column1: It seems to have no effect.
column2: 似乎毫無作用。

column1: Yep, it's magic, alright!
column2: Yep, it's magic, alright!

column1: The ends of the staff are spinning much too rapidly.
column2: 法杖兩端轉得實在太快了。

column1: It seems to have no effect on the wall.
column2: 對那面牆似乎毫無作用。

column1: The wall appears to have a spell placed upon it.
column2: 那面牆似乎被施了咒。

column1: You don't have the ability to fetch such a huge object.
column2: You don't have the ability to fetch such a huge object.

column1: The door is much too hot to climb.
column2: The door is much too hot to climb.

column1: You seem to have melted the hoarfrost.  Now you can see that the Iceberg is fractured.
column2: You seem to have melted the hoarfrost.  Now you can see that the Iceberg is fractured.

column1: You seem to just be melting some of the massive Iceberg.
column2: You seem to just be melting some of the massive Iceberg.

column1: Knock knock, who's there?
column2: 叩叩叩,是哪位?

column1: Heat.
column2: Heat.

column1: Heat who?
column2: 「熱」哪位?

column1: Heat who hesitates is lost!
column2: 猶豫不決的人就要被「熱」掉啦!

column1: Now stop wasting time.
column2: 好了,別再浪費時間了。

column1: Eee-ouch!  It feels pretty hot!
column2: Eee-ouch!  It feels pretty hot!

column1: The door will not respond to physical force.  You must use only magic to solve my test correctly.
column2: The door will not respond to physical force.  You must use only magic to solve my test correctly.

column1: This doesn't look good.  The fire is just getting hotter.
column2: 情況不妙,火越燒越燙了。

column1: You did it!  You're at WIT's end.
column2: 你辦到了!你已經走到 WIT 的盡頭。

column1: The flames have died down, only to reveal a hole burned into the pathway.
column2: 火焰漸漸熄滅,卻在通道上燒出了一個洞。

column1: It has a vague aura of magic.
column2: 它隱隱透出一絲魔法的氣息。

column1: You don't have the ability to move such a huge object.
column2: 你還沒本事搬動這麼龐大的東西。

column1: The Ice is too solid.  You will need to break the fissure first.
column2: The Ice is too solid.  You will need to break the fissure first.

column1: The Ice is now very relaxed.
column2: The Ice is now very relaxed.

column1: The door has swung out too far.  You can't reach it from the pathway.
column2: 門開得太外了,你從通道上構不著它。

column1: The door is already closed.
column2: The door is already closed.

column1: The door does not seem to be magic, but there is a glow behind it.
column2: 這門看來不帶魔法,門後卻透著一抹微光。

column1: There's nothing worth fetching.
column2: 沒什麼值得取的。

column1: It seems to have no effect on the closed door.
column2: 對那扇緊閉的門似乎毫無作用。

column1: Your Detect Magic spell has revealed what appears to be a magical door at the end of the hallway.  Or is it just an illusion?
column2: Your Detect Magic spell has revealed what appears to be a magical door at the end of the hallway.  Or is it just an illusion?

column1: This dead-end alley looks much like the other streets of Shapeir.  You feel a very slight tingling feeling, almost beyond the limits of sensation.
column2: This dead-end alley looks much like the other streets of Shapeir.  You feel a very slight tingling feeling, almost beyond the limits of sensation.

column1: You are walking through one of the many twisty streets of the city.
column2: You are walking through one of the many twisty streets of the city.

column1: You don't detect any doors here at the moment.
column2: You don't detect any doors here at the moment.

column1: A door appears.
column2: 一扇門出現了。

column1: That didn't have any effect on the door.
column2: 那對那扇門毫無作用。

column1: The wall seemed to absorb your spell's energies.
column2: 那面牆似乎把你法術的能量吸收掉了。

column1: You hear the voices.
column2: 你聽見了那些聲音。

column1: Our time is much too valuable to be wasted upon one with so little magic.  Do not bother returning until you have acquired more power.
column2: 我們的時間太寶貴,可不能浪費在魔力如此低微之人身上。等你修得更強的力量,再回來吧。

column1: You will certainly have to be more clever and more skilled if you hope to be initiated.  Perhaps you should practice your spells more.
column2: 你若想通過入門考驗,勢必得更機靈、更有本事才行。或許你該多練練你的法術。

column1: No one enters the gates of the Palace of the Sultan Harun al-Rashid (May he reign forever!) unless ordered to do so.
column2: No one enters the gates of the Palace of the Sultan Harun al-Rashid (May he reign forever!) unless ordered to do so.

column1: It is very well guarded, and only those whom the Sultan trusts and respects may enter.
column2: 那裡守衛森嚴,只有蘇丹所信任敬重之人才得入內。

column1: Although few see the Sultan, he sees all.  The Sultan knows everyone and everything in the city of Shapeir.
column2: 蘇丹深居簡出,卻洞燭一切。夏皮爾城裡的每一個人、每一樁事,都瞞不過他的眼睛。

column1: The courtyard plaza of the Palace of the Sultan is very clean.
column2: The courtyard plaza of the Palace of the Sultan is very clean.

column1: You can't use your rope very well underneath the balcony.
column2: 在陽台底下,你的繩子不太好使。

column1: The spell does not seem to affect the wind.
column2: 這法術似乎對風起不了作用。

column1: The dagger is easily tossed from the funnel.
column2: 那把匕首輕易地被漏斗風甩了出去。

column1: The wind catches the %s and spins it away.   It takes far more skill to throw into something than to throw at something.
column2: The wind catches the %s and spins it away.   It takes far more skill to throw into something than to throw at something.

column1: You have managed to throw the %s into the center of the funnel.
column2: You have managed to throw the %s into the center of the funnel.

column1: Hmm.  Nothing to grab on to.
column2: Hmm.  Nothing to grab on to.

column1: You force some earth into the center of the Air Elemental's funnel.
column2: You force some earth into the center of the Air Elemental's funnel.

column1: You force some sand into the center of the Air Elemental's funnel.
column2: You force some sand into the center of the Air Elemental's funnel.

column1: It's too windy to do that.
column2: It's too windy to do that.

column1: You manage to withstand the force of the wind.
column2: 你勉強頂住了風的衝力。

column1: You try to force yourself into the wind, but it is stronger than you, and you can't quite get to the center of the funnel.
column2: 你試著硬擠進風裡,可它比你還強,你怎麼也搆不到漏斗風的中心。

column1: You are just not strong enough to withstand the wind.
column2: 你的力氣就是不夠,擋不住這陣風。

column1: You scoop some dirt into the funnel.
column2: You scoop some dirt into the funnel.

column1: You don't seem to be close enough for that.
column2: You don't seem to be close enough for that.

column1: You pitch some sand into the funnel.
column2: You pitch some sand into the funnel.

column1: You will need to be on the ground in order to do that.
column2: You will need to be on the ground in order to do that.

column1: You capture the Air Elemental in the bellows and proudly put them away.
column2: 你用風箱捕住了氣元素精靈,得意地把它收了起來。

column1: The swift Air Elemental easily avoids the bellows.
column2: 敏捷的氣元素精靈輕鬆地避開了風箱。

column1: This place lies somewhere between weird and infinity.  Much more toward weird.
column2: This place lies somewhere between weird and infinity.  Much more toward weird.

column1: You think about it, and then think better about it.  You don't really want to get the guy upset.  You'd probably wind up with ants in your pants, egg on your face, and pie in your eye.
column2: 你考慮了一下,還是打消了念頭。你並不真想惹惱那傢伙。否則你大概會落得個坐立難安、顏面盡失、自討沒趣的下場。

column1: You uncurled me some whirl from Dervish?  Oh, frabjous day, hoorah, hooray!  Here's the Dinars that I must pay.
column2: You uncurled me some whirl from Dervish?  Oh, frabjous day, hoorah, hooray!  Here's the Dinars that I must pay.

column1: You don't dare while the shopkeepers watching.  He might throw a pie at you.
column2: You don't dare while the shopkeepers watching.  He might throw a pie at you.

column1: You don't say?  And I sure won't.
column2: 真的假的?反正我是不會說出去的啦。

column1: You have my last pair there, Claire, and I haven't got a spare.
column2: 那是我最後一副囉,親愛的,我可沒備用的了。

column1: What do I look like, a department store?
column2: What do I look like, a department store?

column1: I fear you can't procure that here, Dear.
column2: I fear you can't procure that here, Dear.

column1: Well, we can bargain all you like, Mike, but that won't change the fact that you already bought the only pair I had.
column2: 好啦,你愛怎麼殺價都行,可這也改變不了——我那唯一一副早被你買走啦。

column1: By golly, it is!  There's a silver lining on the underside of Keapon's cloud.
column2: 我的天,還真是!姬朋這朵烏雲底下,總算透出一線光明。

column1: The fact that the clouds on the ceiling don't move leads you to the conclusion that they were not painted on with an air brush.
column2: The fact that the clouds on the ceiling don't move leads you to the conclusion that they were not painted on with an air brush.

column1: Talk about topsy-turvy.  The sun shines up from the floor onto the clouds above.
column2: Talk about topsy-turvy.  The sun shines up from the floor onto the clouds above.

column1: No sweat, Babette.
column2: 小事一樁,小寶貝。

column1: It's sad, Dad, but if you need to climb to the sublime, you need a rope, Dope.
column2: 說來遺憾啊老兄,可你要是想往那高處攀,就少不了一條繩子,傻小子。

column1: I happen to have a Magic Rope for sale, direct from some Hindoo who did the best he kin do.
column2: I happen to have a Magic Rope for sale, direct from some Hindoo who did the best he kin do.

column1: I see by your outfit that you know some magic. You see by my outfit that I know some, too. We can see by our outfits that we both know magic. I guess that we two must eschew the Hoodoo.
column2: I see by your outfit that you know some magic. You see by my outfit that I know some, too. We can see by our outfits that we both know magic. I guess that we two must eschew the Hoodoo.

column1: Hello sailor, new in town?  So how ya doin, Bruin?
column2: Hello sailor, new in town?  So how ya doin, Bruin?

column1: Had any WITty conversation?  Been Chanting with the Enchantress yet?
column2: Had any WITty conversation?  Been Chanting with the Enchantress yet?

column1: Seen all the sights, Justin, or just citing all the scenes?
column2: 景點都逛遍了沒啊,還是光在那兒紙上談兵?

column1: Sell you a spell, Nell?
column2: 賣你道法術如何呀,小妞?

column1: It sure LOOKED real!
column2: It sure LOOKED real!

column1: You looks awfully familiar and familiarly awful.
column2: 你瞧著怪眼熟的,也眼熟得怪嚇人的。

column1: Haven't you been here before?
column2: 你以前是不是來過這兒呀?

column1: You still don't have the skill, Phil, so I'll spare you the bill.
column2: 你火候還不到啊老兄,這筆帳我就先不跟你算清。

column1: Say Jay, what the hey?  The rope you've got is one and the same.
column2: Say Jay, what the hey?  The rope you've got is one and the same.

column1: Well, the tag says 15 dinars, but I guess I can take 14 for 'em...
column2: Well, the tag says 15 dinars, but I guess I can take 14 for 'em...

column1: Naaaaaah, I guessed wrong.  15 dinars it is, then.
column2: Naaaaaah, I guessed wrong.  15 dinars it is, then.

column1: Whew!  Smooth talking, Dude.  Ya got the gift for gab, Tab.
column2: Whew!  Smooth talking, Dude.  Ya got the gift for gab, Tab.

column1: Tell ya what I'm going ta do.  Seein' as you beein' a Heero and all, I'll reduce the price by %d whole simoleons.
column2: Tell ya what I'm going ta do.  Seein' as you beein' a Heero and all, I'll reduce the price by %d whole simoleons.

column1: (Dinars, that is.  Dough, Flo.  Shapeir Buck an Ear.)
column2: (Dinars, that is.  Dough, Flo.  Shapeir Buck an Ear.)

column1: If you want to do some dealing, then pick a better deck.
column2: 想做點買賣,就拿副像樣點的牌來。

column1: I laid my cards on the table, Grable, and if you're able, then ante up.
column2: 我已經把牌攤在桌上了,有本事你就掏錢下注吧。

column1: Pay or belay, Jay.
column2: 付錢,不然就閃邊去。

column1: Ah, I have that here, Dear.
column2: 啊,我這兒有,親愛的。

column1: Possible, Probable, my blue hen, Make it appear as I say when!
column2: Possible, Probable, my blue hen, Make it appear as I say when!

column1: Nothing up my sleeve...
column2: 我袖子裡可什麼都沒藏喔……

column1: Presto, Change-o, Re-arrange-o!
column2: Presto, Change-o, Re-arrange-o!

column1: Eins, Zwei, Drei, Vier, Make it snappy und bring it here!
column2: Eins, Zwei, Drei, Vier, Make it snappy und bring it here!

column1: Need more money, Honey.  Your moolah's too low, Moe.
column2: 得再多點錢哪,寶貝。你這荷包也太扁了吧,老兄。

column1: The sum doesn't balance.  COD or DOA.  Pay, or go away.
column2: The sum doesn't balance.  COD or DOA.  Pay, or go away.

column1: So solly, Chollie.  This money's funny.
column2: 真不好意思啊老兄,這錢有問題。

column1: You need to go get some dough from the Money Changer, Stranger.
column2: You need to go get some dough from the Money Changer, Stranger.

column1: Here you go, Joe.  Hope you're happy, Pappy.
column2: 給你囉,老兄。希望你滿意啦,大爺。

column1: The spell looks and feels very familiar.  In fact, you realize that you already knew it!  What a waste of money!
column2: The spell looks and feels very familiar.  In fact, you realize that you already knew it!  What a waste of money!

column1: Now let me see where I last saw it.  I'll have to look for it in the back room.  I'll be back in a flash with the stash, Nash!
column2: 讓我想想上回放哪去了。我得進後頭那間屋子翻一翻。我一溜煙就把貨拿來,等著喔!

column1: Silly me.  I had it here the whole time.
column2: 瞧我這記性。它一直都在這兒呢。

column1: Abracabadra and didgery-doo, Make the thing appear for you!
column2: Abracabadra and didgery-doo, Make the thing appear for you!

column1: But I see you already have some dirt, Kurt.
column2: 不過我看你身上已經有點土了呀,老兄。

column1: You put the pot of dirt away.
column2: 你把那盆土收了起來。

column1: Buon giorno!  Good and tag!  Buenas Dias, Senor Caws.  I haven't seen you in so long.
column2: Buon giorno!  Good and tag!  Buenas Dias, Senor Caws.  I haven't seen you in so long.

column1: Hello!  Bone Jar!  Hi, guy!  Set a spell, take your shoes off!  What may I do to you?
column2: Hello!  Bone Jar!  Hi, guy!  Set a spell, take your shoes off!  What may I do to you?

column1: Konnichiwa!  Come and tell me who!  Hola!  How now, brown cow?
column2: 空你機哇!來告訴我是誰!喔啦!最近如何呀,花斑牛?

column1: Top o' the morning to ya!  Mar Haba!  What's New, Pussycat?
column2: Top o' the morning to ya!  Mar Haba!  What's New, Pussycat?

column1: Hail, fellow, well met!  What da ya know, Joe?
column2: 幸會幸會,老兄!最近如何呀,老喬?

column1: Ahoy, Goy!  Howdy, Duty.  What's up, Doc?
column2: 嘿唷,老兄!安安,任務。怎麼啦,博士?

column1: A River Dirty, Gerty!  Adieu to you!  'Bye for now, purple cow.
column2: A River Dirty, Gerty!  Adieu to you!  'Bye for now, purple cow.

column1: Ah, be a toe, Joe!  See ya later, alligator.
column2: Ah, be a toe, Joe!  See ya later, alligator.

column1: Bone Sewer, Sir!  After a while, Crocodile.
column2: Bone Sewer, Sir!  After a while, Crocodile.

column1: Hasta La Pasta!  Brain those nachos!  See ya soon, Raccoon!
column2: Hasta La Pasta!  Brain those nachos!  See ya soon, Raccoon!

column1: Au revoir, Pa!  Way to go, Pinocchio!
column2: 再會啦,老爹!幹得好,小木偶!

column1: TTFN - Ta-ta for now!  Bon Voyage!
column2: TTFN - Ta-ta for now!  Bon Voyage!

column1: How do you do, adieu.  Good night and don't let the Bed Bugs bite!
column2: How do you do, adieu.  Good night and don't let the Bed Bugs bite!

column1: Nharak sa'id!  Aloha Oe!  Well come in!  Gesundheit!  Tip a canoe and how do you do?
column2: Nharak sa'id!  Aloha Oe!  Well come in!  Gesundheit!  Tip a canoe and how do you do?

column1: Pleased to meet you and I mean to please you!
column2: 很高興認識你,我也存心要讓你滿意!

column1: Welcome to my little shop of wonders!  Wonder what shop it is?  It's magic, of course!
column2: 歡迎光臨我這間小小的奇妙店鋪!好奇這是什麼店?當然是賣魔法的囉!

column1: I am Keapon Laffin, Proprietor.  You must be Nobile Caws.   Proud to know you, Mister Caws.  Shapeir always hopes for heroes.
column2: I am Keapon Laffin, Proprietor.  You must be Nobile Caws.   Proud to know you, Mister Caws.  Shapeir always hopes for heroes.

column1: If you need to know some knowledge about the magic way, then go and see Aziza who'll enchant you if you stay.
column2: If you need to know some knowledge about the magic way, then go and see Aziza who'll enchant you if you stay.

column1: Watch out, she's testy, though.
column2: 不過可得小心,她脾氣挺火爆的。

column1: So long, fair well, Oaf, we da same!
column2: 再會啦,珍重囉,大笨蛋,咱倆是一夥的!

column1: Good-bye!  See you real soon, Effendi Caws I like you.
column2: Good-bye!  See you real soon, Effendi Caws I like you.

column1: Heard the one about the fire, sire?  It's a torrid story.
column2: 聽過那則關於火的故事沒,大爺?那可是個火辣辣的故事。

column1: The fire elemental had a seething passion for a sultry old flame who burned him once.
column2: The fire elemental had a seething passion for a sultry old flame who burned him once.

column1: Seems his spark was worse than his spite.  There ardor be a law.
column2: 看來他那把火比他的怨氣還旺。這份熱情啊,真該立法管管。

column1: At least there will be a hot time in the old town tonight.
column2: 至少今晚這老城裡,鐵定要熱鬧上一場。

column1: I don't mean to bluster, buster, but if you're in a hurry, Cain, you need to get down to earth before you blow up.
column2: 我不是要嚇唬你啦,老兄,可你要是趕時間哪,得先腳踏實地,才不會把自己給炸了。

column1: Then if you bellow, fellow, the breeze is sure to freeze.
column2: 那麼,只要你鼓動風箱,小老弟,那陣風保準給你凍住。

column1: How's the Chinook, Schnook?
column2: 那陣熱風吹得怎樣啊,傻小子?

column1: I heard the gusty Air Elemental has a tempestuous relationship with his own twister.
column2: 我聽說那陣風大的氣元素,跟他自己那道龍捲風鬧得正轟轟烈烈呢。

column1: They had a whirlwind affair, but it's winding down, if you catch my drift.
column2: 他倆談了場旋風般的戀愛,不過眼下風頭正漸漸消下去,你聽得懂我的弦外之音吧?

column1: The Dervish of the desert sent a bedouin to my boudoir, and I hear he needs a hero for to help him bait a beast.
column2: The Dervish of the desert sent a bedouin to my boudoir, and I hear he needs a hero for to help him bait a beast.

column1: You have to watch the old codger - he's being cagey, but Dervish is my command.
column2: You have to watch the old codger - he's being cagey, but Dervish is my command.

column1: They say an earthy elemental is aground, but he just wants to be left a loam.
column2: They say an earthy elemental is aground, but he just wants to be left a loam.

column1: What a clod.  He's not very gneiss.
column2: 真是個土包子。對人一點也不「片麻」客氣。

column1: This town's sure going to rock tonight, but I suppose you prefer old Terra Firma (the more firmer, the less terror).
column2: 今晚這城肯定要被搖個天翻地覆,不過我猜你還是偏愛腳踏實地的老大地(地越穩,人越不慌)。

column1: Arrrrrhhh, me bucko, if you be thirstin' to sponge off the maritime maiden, you'll need to air out a few things first, or you'll be seein' the waters pout, arrrrrhhh!
column2: 呀啊啊——小子哎,你要是渴著想揩那「海上姑娘」的油哪,得先把幾樁事透透氣,不然你就等著瞧那水鬧脾氣吧,呀啊啊!

column1: Water it be today, matey?
column2: 今天「水」事如意嗎,夥計?

column1: Did you sea the swell elemental that's made such a splash in the fountain?
column2: Did you sea the swell elemental that's made such a splash in the fountain?

column1: She's awfully soppy and tends to gush, but I tell you, Eddy, and I don't mean to deluge you, there's an undercurrent of still water running steep.
column2: 她濕答答的,還動不動就嘩嘩傾瀉,不過我跟你說啊,我可不是想拿話淹你,這底下可有一股深沉的暗流靜靜湧著呢。

column1: If I were you, I'd rain away.
column2: 要是我啊,我可就溜之大「雨」嘍。

column1: If you haven't seen her already today, the End Chant Dress wishes to chat.
column2: If you haven't seen her already today, the End Chant Dress wishes to chat.

column1: You need to make a final bow before Auntie Azizie's alter-ego.
column2: You need to make a final bow before Auntie Azizie's alter-ego.

column1: Speaking of finals, Lionel, so you're graduating to Raseir.
column2: 說到收尾哪,老兄,看來你要「畢業」前往拉希爾囉。

column1: Be ware, Larry, I hear it's hairy there, so watch your derriere.
column2: Be ware, Larry, I hear it's hairy there, so watch your derriere.

column1: Happy trails to you, until we meet again.
column2: 一路順風囉,咱們後會有期。

column1: Buy those banditos!  Good luck, Buck!
column2: 去收拾那些土匪吧!祝你好運囉,老弟!

column1: Goat got your throat or just lost your voice?  You might check the Flossed and Crowned for missing teeth.
column2: Goat got your throat or just lost your voice?  You might check the Flossed and Crowned for missing teeth.

column1: Asleep at the heel and slipping into slumber?  I deplore being a bore, so just don't snore, senor.
column2: Asleep at the heel and slipping into slumber?  I deplore being a bore, so just don't snore, senor.

column1: Resting on your Laurels, Hardy, or dreaming of days, Hays?
column2: Resting on your Laurels, Hardy, or dreaming of days, Hays?

column1: Cousin to Santa and Lost, aren't you?
column2: Cousin to Santa and Lost, aren't you?

column1: Clip, clop to the Magic shop to see the owner flip his flop.  I sell spells, can't you tell?
column2: Clip, clop to the Magic shop to see the owner flip his flop.  I sell spells, can't you tell?

column1: Wonder of Wonders and will wonders never cease?  You'll wonder where the yellow went, and whether geese have fleece.
column2: Wonder of Wonders and will wonders never cease?  You'll wonder where the yellow went, and whether geese have fleece.

column1: Properly and proudly I propound with all propriety.  Certainly and soundly, I speak with all sobriety.  I am the very model of a major modern magic man (well, Gnome, actually).
column2: Properly and proudly I propound with all propriety.  Certainly and soundly, I speak with all sobriety.  I am the very model of a major modern magic man (well, Gnome, actually).

column1: Do I know Yorick?  He's my cousin, Laspo.  Ah, Laspo Yorick, I knew him, hero.
column2: Do I know Yorick?  He's my cousin, Laspo.  Ah, Laspo Yorick, I knew him, hero.

column1: I'm a little low on spells in stock, but I do have scrolls of Detect Magic for a negligible 10 dinars, Trigger for only 15, Force Bolt for a measly 20, Levitate for a mere 30 dinars, and Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle for a paltry 25 dinars.
column2: I'm a little low on spells in stock, but I do have scrolls of Detect Magic for a negligible 10 dinars, Trigger for only 15, Force Bolt for a measly 20, Levitate for a mere 30 dinars, and Erasmus' Razzle Dazzle for a paltry 25 dinars.

column1: If you have a need for nudging or a bent for bouncing, this spell is swell.  It can punch, it can push, it can even beat around the bush.  All this for a mere 20 dinars.
column2: If you have a need for nudging or a bent for bouncing, this spell is swell.  It can punch, it can push, it can even beat around the bush.  All this for a mere 20 dinars.

column1: Detect will select what you suspect but can't inspect.  Expect the effect to wreck the protection by creating a defect in its perfect reflection.
column2: 「偵測」能替你挑出那些你疑神疑鬼卻又查不出的東西。瞧好這效果如何破解防護——只消在那完美的倒影上戳出個破綻。

column1: The Trigger spell is like a witch's switch with a hitch, Rich.  It makes the last magic cast in the past happen fast.  Not only that, Brat, but Trigger makes you hoarse.
column2: 「觸發」這法術啊,就像女巫手裡帶機關的開關,老兄。它能讓你方才施過的最後一道法術瞬間再現。不止如此呢,小鬼,「觸發」還會讓你嗓子發啞喔。

column1: It's got its ups and downs, but if you need a lift, this will get your feet up off the ground.
column2: 它有起有落啦,可你要是需要往上飄一飄,這玩意兒能讓你雙腳離地。

column1: A shining example of a scintillating spell.  It brings a tear to the eyes of the other guys who spy it.
column2: 這可是耀眼法術裡的閃亮典範。誰瞄上一眼,都要被閃得熱淚盈眶。

column1: Whose bed am I in?  What kind of personal question is that?  Oh, bedouin!  He's a desert drifter who shifts with sifting sands.
column2: Whose bed am I in?  What kind of personal question is that?  Oh, bedouin!  He's a desert drifter who shifts with sifting sands.

column1: Come here, Oh stranger, and save us from danger.  It's a dirty job, and I'm glad you're here to do it, Pruitt.  People say you're pretty neat, Pete.
column2: Come here, Oh stranger, and save us from danger.  It's a dirty job, and I'm glad you're here to do it, Pruitt.  People say you're pretty neat, Pete.

column1: Actually, Pete was a friend of mine who tangled with a wizard.  Now pizza elemental.
column2: Actually, Pete was a friend of mine who tangled with a wizard.  Now pizza elemental.

column1: You do?  Do what?  How do you do the hoodoo that you do so well?
column2: 是喔?做啥?你那一手把戲到底是怎麼變的呀,變得這麼順手?

column1: You have a dearth of earth?  That's worth my mirth.  I do have some Fooler's Earth in stock, though.
column2: 你缺土啊?這倒值得我樂一樂。不過嘛,我店裡確實還有點「障眼土」的存貨。

column1: Noticed my note, mister?  It is der wish for the whirl of a Dervish.  I will pay 1500 centimes (in dinars, of course) for such a thing.  All you have to do is find it.
column2: 瞧見我那張告示了沒,先生?我心心念念想要苦行僧那旋轉的本事。為了這玩意兒,我願意付一千五百生丁(當然,折成第納爾算)。你只要去把它找來就成。

column1: Be it ever so humble, there's no face like Gnome.
column2: Be it ever so humble, there's no face like Gnome.

column1: Erasmus is a wizard I respect.  He can out-pun almost anyone and he jests with the best.  Between Erasmus and Fenrus, it's hard to get a joke in edgewise.  Fenrus is an aristo-rat at that, too.  Their humor is no rumor.
column2: 伊拉斯謨是位我敬重的巫師。論玩雙關他幾乎打遍天下無敵手,說笑也是箇中高手。在伊拉斯謨跟芬魯斯兩個之間,你連插句笑話的空檔都搶不到。芬魯斯那傢伙啊,還是隻貴族「鼠」呢。他倆的幽默可不是空穴來風。

column1: Hard to learn a spell unless you have a scroll.  Once it's read; it's in your head, Fred.
column2: Hard to learn a spell unless you have a scroll.  Once it's read; it's in your head, Fred.

column1: Behold the beast of burden, it travels in the herd, then it frequently is heard when a Saurus is absurd, men.
column2: Behold the beast of burden, it travels in the herd, then it frequently is heard when a Saurus is absurd, men.

column1: Sorry, Charlie.  I don't have a scroll for that Wizard Spell.  It tends to disappear fast.
column2: Sorry, Charlie.  I don't have a scroll for that Wizard Spell.  It tends to disappear fast.

column1: A magic user went to school, so he could learn the magic rule.  And when he learned what he had heard, he had become a great Wizard.  (He went, to WIT, of course.)
column2: A magic user went to school, so he could learn the magic rule.  And when he learned what he had heard, he had become a great Wizard.  (He went, to WIT, of course.)

column1: It's used to make dark stains on light clothes.  If you need it to ground old Blowhard, it's yours for a song.  Dust in the Wind.  All you need is dust in the wind.
column2: It's used to make dark stains on light clothes.  If you need it to ground old Blowhard, it's yours for a song.  Dust in the Wind.  All you need is dust in the wind.

column1: If you are inclined to climb, a Magic Rope can give you hope.  To make it rise, close your eyes, raise your arms high, and watch it fly.
column2: If you are inclined to climb, a Magic Rope can give you hope.  To make it rise, close your eyes, raise your arms high, and watch it fly.

column1: If the time is right, you're in a plight, you can get it across if you give it a toss.  Burma Shave.
column2: 時機若對,你陷困境,輕輕一擲,便能解圍。——Burma Shave

column1: It's a good tool, fool, but remember the rule - the Magic can't last, and on your last cast, it'll go fast - so don't waste it, or you might find yourself hanging in air with nothing there.
column2: It's a good tool, fool, but remember the rule - the Magic can't last, and on your last cast, it'll go fast - so don't waste it, or you might find yourself hanging in air with nothing there.

column1: We have a special on Magic Ropes this year.  It usually costs 12,500 centimes.  For a limited time only, though, it's only 125 dinars.  So do you wanta scope a rope, Miss Anthrope?
column2: We have a special on Magic Ropes this year.  It usually costs 12,500 centimes.  For a limited time only, though, it's only 125 dinars.  So do you wanta scope a rope, Miss Anthrope?

column1: Oh, those old things?  I could never get them to work correctly.  But then again, I've never worn them.
column2: 喔,那些老古董啊?我從來就沒能讓它們好好運作過。不過話說回來,我也從沒真的戴過就是了。

column1: Oh Cod, it makes me Eel when people Carp and Whale just for the Halibut, with no Porpoise.  I'm Net that kind of Buoy, Sea?  I'm Shore tired of Gills in School with their Tails of Roe.
column2: 老天「鱈」,人家一沒「鯨」由就在那兒又「鯉」怨又「鯢」嚎,真叫我渾身「鰻」不自在。我可「鮃」不是那種人,「魚」道嗎?我「鯊」是受夠了那些成天滿口「鱈」話的傢伙了。

column1: (Coral, Bob, and Brine made me say this.  Tanks for rising to the bait and falling for it, hook, line, and sinker!)
column2: (都是珊瑚、鮑伯跟那一缸鹽水逼我說的啦。多「漁」你上鉤咬餌,還連魚鉤、魚線、鉛墜整套吞下肚!)

column1: (We're REALLY fishing here, folks.)
column2: (各位,我們這可是「真的」在釣魚喔。)

column1: Air, air, it's everywhere.  Way down here, and way up there.  In your bed and in my head and going anywhere it's led, Dunderhead.
column2: 空氣啊空氣,無所不在。低低在這兒,高高在那兒。在你的床上,在我的腦袋裡,被牽到哪兒就飄到哪兒,傻瓜。

column1: My knowledge is scanty, Auntie.
column2: 我懂的可不多哪,大姊。

column1: If you need to know the answer, then ask another, Prancer.
column2: 要想知道答案哪,你就去問問別人吧,小蹦跳。

column1: Shapeir is a city, small and quaint and pretty.  Shapeir is a land, large and great and grand.
column2: 夏皮爾是座城,小巧、古樸又秀麗。夏皮爾是片地,遼闊、偉大又壯麗。

column1: Raseir, I fear, is very queer, and if you do not interfere, I think that it will disappear.
column2: 拉希爾啊,我怕得很,那地方詭異透頂,你要是再不出手干預,我看它遲早要憑空消失。

column1: Djinni, Genie, Jeannie, Djinn; the desert's where they all begin.  Djinni, Ifreet, Marid, Djann; Magic's where they all began.
column2: Djinni, Genie, Jeannie, Djinn; the desert's where they all begin.  Djinni, Ifreet, Marid, Djann; Magic's where they all began.

column1: The Fire Elemental has a weakness for water.  He lives in the fast lane, so you'll have to get him restrained if you want to make him remain, so that you can get him contained.  To get the flame to smother, don't ask me, go ask another.
column2: 火元素的弱點就是水。他過的是快節奏的日子,所以你想讓他乖乖待著,就得先把他制住,這樣才好把他關起來。想讓那火苗熄滅嘛,可別問我,去問別人吧。

column1: Ahoy, me boy, and shiver me timbers.  Ya got what it takes to make a lady less limber.  Pieces of eight, mate, and blow the maid down, sweep her off her feet and then clear out of town.
column2: 喂,小子,我的老天爺啊。你還真有本事讓一位姑娘渾身發軟。金幣一塊塊,夥計,把那姑娘哄得神魂顛倒,讓她拜倒在你腳下,然後你再溜出城去。

column1: If you're led to behead an airhead, don't let him lead the chase.  Glue his shoes with goo, Blue, and throw mud in his face.  It's fair to snare the air there, then put him in his place.
column2: 要是你被指派去砍掉某個風一般的呆頭,可別讓他帶著你滿場跑。拿黏糊糊的膠把他鞋子黏死,再朝他臉上甩泥巴。在那兒設陷阱困住那陣風,合情合理,然後好好教訓他一頓。

column1: There is no better way to arouse a rock's ire, than to fight with a light and to smite him with fire.
column2: There is no better way to arouse a rock's ire, than to fight with a light and to smite him with fire.

column1: Matter Monster, made by man.  Chaos tossed across the land.  Catch them, grab them if you can.  Then they're there at your command.
column2: Matter Monster, made by man.  Chaos tossed across the land.  Catch them, grab them if you can.  Then they're there at your command.

column1: Griffin, Jackal, TerrorSaurus, Ghoul;  learn their ways in Adventurer's School.  Brigand, Djinni, Scorpion, all;  ask for info at the Guild Hall.
column2: Griffin, Jackal, TerrorSaurus, Ghoul;  learn their ways in Adventurer's School.  Brigand, Djinni, Scorpion, all;  ask for info at the Guild Hall.

column1: Magic, magic everywhere.  Find it at Aziza's lair.  Magic, magic, wait a bit.  Find it in the soul of WIT.  Magic, magic in the stars.  The astrologer can see that far.
column2: Magic, magic everywhere.  Find it at Aziza's lair.  Magic, magic, wait a bit.  Find it in the soul of WIT.  Magic, magic in the stars.  The astrologer can see that far.

column1: Magic, magic, where'd it begin?  Go and ask the mighty Djinn.  Magic, Magic, where'd it stop?  Look around my magic shop.
column2: Magic, magic, where'd it begin?  Go and ask the mighty Djinn.  Magic, Magic, where'd it stop?  Look around my magic shop.

column1: The Sultan sits upon this throne, I ponder if he's ever home?  The Sultan sits inside his palace.  Is he there? Well, go ask Alice. (If you can find her - Last we met she had a chat with a cat and chatter with the Hatter.  It's a wonder, man.)
column2: The Sultan sits upon this throne, I ponder if he's ever home?  The Sultan sits inside his palace.  Is he there? Well, go ask Alice. (If you can find her - Last we met she had a chat with a cat and chatter with the Hatter.  It's a wonder, man.)

column1: The Emir of Raseir did disappear a year ago.
column2: 拉希爾的埃米爾,一年前確實憑空消失了。

column1: If you want to be WITty, then search in the city.  The nitty-gritty to this ditty:  you need magic to find it, 'twas designed by committee.
column2: 你要是想沾上巫師技術學院(W.I.T.)的「智」氣,那就到城裡頭去找吧。這支小曲兒的關鍵就在:你得有魔法才找得到它,畢竟那是一群人合議設計出來的。

column1: You have to be a hero if you want to play the palace.  The Sultan is selective about whom he will see.
column2: 你想進那宮殿可得是個英雄才行。蘇丹對見什麼人可是挑得很。

column1: It's sand, man.  Hot, Scott.  Dry, Guy.
column2: 就是沙啊,老兄。又燙,又乾,沒別的。

column1: The din of the bazaar is filled with the dinar.  If you want to pay a cent, Dean, you have to have a centime.
column2: 市集裡那喧鬧聲,滿是第納爾的銅臭味。你要是想付個一分錢,老兄,那你手裡就得有生丁才行。

column1: The Plaza of the Fighters is west of here, Greer.  There you'll find the hall, Paul.
column2: The Plaza of the Fighters is west of here, Greer.  There you'll find the hall, Paul.

column1: The Kattas trail into the Katta's Tail Inn, he says cattily, as he goes from the feline to the ridiculous.
column2: 卡塔族都拖著尾巴溜進「貓尾客棧」啦——他賊頭賊腦地說,從貓樣一路扯到沒個正經。

column1: In the summer, in the desert, it is hot 'til day is done.  Only mad dogs and hero men go out in the noonday sun.
column2: 盛夏的沙漠,熱到日落都不歇。也只有瘋狗跟英雄,敢頂著正午的大太陽出門。

column1: The center of the city, Mitty.  The water for the town, Clown.
column2: 城的正中央哪,小傻瓜。打水的地方哪,小笨蛋。

column1: Don't quote me, but Aziza has a waterbed and fiddles with fish.
column2: 別說是我講的喔——聽說阿姿莎睡水床,還愛擺弄她那些魚。

column1: There was a Money Changer and her name was Dinarzad.  It's understood that she was goood and very, very Baad.  You can find her at the end of Dinar Tarik, my lad.
column2: There was a Money Changer and her name was Dinarzad.  It's understood that she was goood and very, very Baad.  You can find her at the end of Dinar Tarik, my lad.

column1: Do not hurry.  Do not wait.  The astrologer will tell your fate.  He is not near.  He is not far.  He's at the end of Tarik of Stars.
column2: Do not hurry.  Do not wait.  The astrologer will tell your fate.  He is not near.  He is not far.  He's at the end of Tarik of Stars.

column2: Auntie Azizie lives off the east end of Shmali Tarik.  Mind your P's and Q's when you are there, or she might send you you-know-where.

column1: He's not a bad poet, but don't let him know it.  It might go from bad to verse.
column2: 他這詩人還不賴啦,可別讓他知道——不然只會從糟糕變成「更詩」糟糕。

column1: I roll scrolls and sell spells.
column2: 我捲卷軸,也賣法術。

column1: Purrfect Purrsons.
column2: Purrfect Purrsons.

column1: The Dervish of the Desert oversees his own oasis as he sits upon the sands by the shore of his small spring.  It is somewhere south of Shapeir and in inches a bit east, If you're going to go and gab with him you'd better take a beast.
column2: The Dervish of the Desert oversees his own oasis as he sits upon the sands by the shore of his small spring.  It is somewhere south of Shapeir and in inches a bit east, If you're going to go and gab with him you'd better take a beast.

column1: Is that you, Kareem?
column2: 是你嗎,卡林姆?

column1: Yesh, Fathher.
column2: Yesh, Fathher.

column2: Lucky for you, Kareem was too inebriated to see you.   Better not press your luck next time.  Find a good place to hide.

column1: You look pretty silly hiding in the corner.  You were just lucky that Kareem was very, very drunk.
column2: You look pretty silly hiding in the corner.  You were just lucky that Kareem was very, very drunk.

column2: 你聽見外頭傳來一個聲音。

column1: Now, is it this door?
column2: 那麼,是這扇門嗎?

column1: I think I just passed the alleyway.  But was I coming or going?
column2: 我好像剛走過那條巷子。可我這是要回家,還是要出門來著?

column1: What was it father told me?  If the key around my neck opens the lock... Ah!  This must be my house.
column2: What was it father told me?  If the key around my neck opens the lock... Ah!  This must be my house.

column1: You hear a key turning in the lock.
column2: 你聽見鑰匙在鎖孔裡轉動的聲音。

column1: Is that you, Abdul?
column2: 是你嗎,阿布杜?

column1: I think so, Father.
column2: 我想是吧,父親。

column1: Father, why is your secret hiding place open?
column2: 父親,你的祕密藏寶處怎麼是開著的?

column1: WHAT??!!!!
column2: WHAT??!!!!

column1: I wonder why father would have left the carpet rolled up?  Oh well...
column2: I wonder why father would have left the carpet rolled up?  Oh well...

column1: You were lucky that Abdul wasn't smart enough to suspect a buglary.  Next time, you'd best replace the rug.
column2: You were lucky that Abdul wasn't smart enough to suspect a buglary.  Next time, you'd best replace the rug.

column1: It's a good thing you are lucky, because you sure pick lousy places to hide.
column2: 幸虧你運氣好,因為你挑的藏身地點實在是爛透了。

column1: Your senses tell you that it might be safe to come out now.
column2: Your senses tell you that it might be safe to come out now.

column1: Have we met before?
column2: 咱們以前見過嗎?

column1: I'm Abdul, the youngest son of Abu bin Ma'amar.
column2: 我是阿布杜,阿布·賓·馬瑪爾家最小的兒子。

column1: Why are you fooling with father's secret hiding place which I'm not supposed ever to mention?
column2: Why are you fooling with father's secret hiding place which I'm not supposed ever to mention?

column1: What are you doing in our house this time of night?
column2: What are you doing in our house this time of night?

column1: Kamar!  See what foolishness your brother is speaking of.
column2: 卡瑪爾!快來瞧瞧你弟弟在胡說八道些什麼。

column1: Grab him!
column2: 抓住他!

column1: Grab whom?  Oh, him!
column2: 抓住誰?喔,他呀!

column1: You hear two men talking outside the door.
column2: 你聽見門外有兩個人在交談。

column1: Wasn't that fun today?  It isn't often we get to torture a thief.  If you ask me, criminals have too many rights these days.
column2: 今天玩得夠爽吧?難得有個小偷可以拷打。要我說啊,這年頭罪犯的權利還真是太多了。

column1: Boy, yeah!  Can't wait 'till tomorrow when we get to cut off his OTHER hand!!  Har har.
column2: Boy, yeah!  Can't wait 'till tomorrow when we get to cut off his OTHER hand!!  Har har.

column1: Yeah, then he'll have no RIGHTS LEFT!  Ho ho ho.
column2: Yeah, then he'll have no RIGHTS LEFT!  Ho ho ho.

column1: See ya tomorrow, Jabbie, old buddy!
column2: 明天見啦,老兄賈比!

column1: Right! Ho ho ho!!
column2: Right! Ho ho ho!!

column1: (back-slap)
column2: (back-slap)

column1: Oh, rat-dung!  You made me drop my keys.  Where'd they go?
column2: 哎呀,該死!被你害得鑰匙掉了。跑哪去了?

column1: Here they are.  See you tomorrow, Baddhi.
column2: 在這兒。明天見,巴迪。

column1: Kamar!  Come here!  There is a thief in the house!

column1: Yes, Father.  But why is the carpet not over the secret trapdoor?
column2: 是,父親。可是地毯怎麼沒蓋在那道暗門上?

column1: WHAT??!!!

column1: Is that you, Kamar?

column1: You hear another voice.

column1: No, Father.
column2: 不是,父親。

column1: But Faddher, that wasn' me who jusht said that.
column2: 可是爹啊,剛剛說話的不是我啦。

column2: 喔,我還以為我人在這間呢。看來我準是在另一個房間。

column1: No, Fathher.

column1: Kamar!  There is someone in the house!
column2: 卡瑪爾!屋裡有人!

column2: You're so tired you can barely stand.

column2: Now *that* took some doing!  You entered your fake initiation combat in such bad condition that your opponent managed to kill you with a wooden sword.  Nice work.  Take two aspirin, get some rest, and restore your game to a previous (and more alert) condition.

column2: You are shown the secret handshake, you learn the secret password:  "Might is Right!", and you are given the secret membership token with your name, rank, and serial number engraved upon it.

column2: Pesky saurus!

column2: You are not likely to live long if you go that way.  One cannot travel in the desert unless one is better prepared, or joins a caravan.

column1: You are riding your saurus.
column2: You are riding your saurus.

column2: You left your saurus out in the desert.  You would never find him if you went out looking.  Better hope he wanders in like the guard said.

column2: Those sauruses don't belong to you.

column2: Your saurus is already in the stable.

column1: You don't have a saurus.
column2: You don't have a saurus.

column2: The Dervish, passing a message through one of the local bedouins, has sent for you.  The Dervish says, "Man who looks for trouble comes to right place." He also says, "Good comes from evil when dark is enlightened."

column1: So, you are the stranger the merchants are talking about.  The gate to Shapeir is always open, but never have I seen an entrance to the city like yours.  To fly in on a carpet, it must be grand.
column2: 這麼說,你就是商人們口中那位陌生人。夏皮爾的城門向來敞開,可我從沒見過有人像你這樣進城的。乘著飛毯而來,那場面想必氣派非凡。

column1: If you are exploring the desert, you should visit the Dervish at the Oasis.  He is a very wise man.
column2: If you are exploring the desert, you should visit the Dervish at the Oasis.  He is a very wise man.

column1: It is good that you have returned safely from the desert.
column2: 你能從沙漠平安歸來,真是太好了。

column2: Thank heavens that thee hast survived the night desert.  It can be truly dangerous when the sun has set.

column2: 日安,閣下。

column1: May thee be granted a happy evening, Effendi.
column2: 願您有個愉快的夜晚,閣下。

column1: There is a caravan which will pass by us six days from now.  If you wish to journey to Raseir, you will need to join it.
column2: 六天之後,會有一支商隊路過此地。你若想前往拉希爾,就得跟著它走。

column1: Good day, Hero.
column2: 日安,英雄。

column1: May thee walk safely this evening, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 願汝今夜行路平安,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: The caravan to Raseir will leave here at the light of dawn in two days time.
column2: The caravan to Raseir will leave here at the light of dawn in two days time.

column1: Hero, the Caravan to Raseir leaves here in two days time, granting that there are no elementals in the city.
column2: Hero, the Caravan to Raseir leaves here in two days time, granting that there are no elementals in the city.

column1: The Caravan traveling to Raseir will depart the morning after this day, oh Hero.
column2: The Caravan traveling to Raseir will depart the morning after this day, oh Hero.

column1: The Caravan to Raseir will leave with the dawn of tomorrow's sun, Hero.
column2: The Caravan to Raseir will leave with the dawn of tomorrow's sun, Hero.

column1: May your journey tomorrow be a safe one, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 願你明日的旅途平安順遂,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: I see you left your saurus out in the desert.  Most sauruses find their way back here to the stables.
column2: I see you left your saurus out in the desert.  Most sauruses find their way back here to the stables.

column1: All sauruses must be stabled in the corral.  Only if you dismount may you enter the city.
column2: All sauruses must be stabled in the corral.  Only if you dismount may you enter the city.

column1: My name is Uns al-Wujud, which means "Delight in Existing Things".
column2: My name is Uns al-Wujud, which means "Delight in Existing Things".

column1: May it please you, I am called "Abdallah bin Tahir".
column2: May it please you, I am called "Abdallah bin Tahir".

column1: The desert is a very dangerous place for any man.
column2: 對任何人而言,沙漠都是個極其危險的地方。

column1: In the middle of the day, the sun will burn one.  At night, many foul monsters prowl in search of easy prey.
column2: 正午時分,烈日會把人烤焦。入夜後,許多兇惡的怪物會四處遊蕩,尋找好下手的獵物。

column1: The dunes are all alike, and it is easy to lose one's way.  Only if one is properly prepared can one endure, or when one travels in a caravan.
column2: 沙丘座座相似,人很容易迷失方向。唯有準備周全,或是隨商隊同行,才撐得下來。

column1: It is my duty to protect this city against monsters that approach it.
column2: 保護這座城市、抵禦來犯的怪物,是我的職責。

column1: The brigands of the desert and the swift TerrorSaurus make travel by day in the desert even more dangerous.  The brigands will happily kill you for whatever you carry  . The TerrorSaurus will hunt you for your flesh.
column2: The brigands of the desert and the swift TerrorSaurus make travel by day in the desert even more dangerous.  The brigands will happily kill you for whatever you carry  . The TerrorSaurus will hunt you for your flesh.

column1: Only the brave and foolish travel the desert alone in the daytime.
column2: 白天還敢獨自橫越沙漠的,不是勇者,就是傻子。

column1: The deadly black scorpion, the Jackals who are as men, the terrible al-ghuls (what you would call ghouls!) -- these creatures roam the desert at night.
column2: The deadly black scorpion, the Jackals who are as men, the terrible al-ghuls (what you would call ghouls!) -- these creatures roam the desert at night.

column1: Only a man of great courage or foolhardiness would go off in the desert alone after the sun has set.
column2: 日落之後還敢獨自闖進沙漠的,不是膽識過人,就是有勇無謀。

column1: Ali Fakir said after you left that he had sold his last saurus and must be going.
column2: Ali Fakir said after you left that he had sold his last saurus and must be going.

column1: The Seller of Sauruses is a strange man.  I have not heard one who can talk faster when trying to sell a saurus.
column2: The Seller of Sauruses is a strange man.  I have not heard one who can talk faster when trying to sell a saurus.

column1: A caravan is many travelers journeying together.  It is much less dangerous that way.
column2: 所謂商隊,就是一大群旅人結伴上路——這樣一來,危險可就少多了。

column1: You can see the riding sauruses in the stable over there.
column2: You can see the riding sauruses in the stable over there.

column1: They are intelligent and brave creatures, willing to obey the slightest command from their master.  They will take you wherever you wish, and come to your aid against monsters.
column2: They are intelligent and brave creatures, willing to obey the slightest command from their master.  They will take you wherever you wish, and come to your aid against monsters.

column1: Best of all, they can always find their way back to where they are stabled, so you need never get lost.

column1: Personally, I'm a merchant.  I sell sauruses.  I'm the local saurus source.  Would you be interested in buying one?  I can make you a deal on a late model with all the options thrown in.  Options like saddle, bridle, two feet, ...
column2: Personally, I'm a merchant.  I sell sauruses.  I'm the local saurus source.  Would you be interested in buying one?  I can make you a deal on a late model with all the options thrown in.  Options like saddle, bridle, two feet, ...

column1: I'm Ali Fakir.  Honest Ali Fakir.  Honest Ali T. Fakir.  I bet you don't know what the T. stands for?  Edgar.  But you were close though.  My name is Honest Ali Fakir.  Pleased to meet you.
column2: 我是阿里·法基爾,老實人阿里·法基爾,老實人阿里·T·法基爾。我賭你猜不出那個 T 代表什麼?答案是「艾德加」。不過你也猜得挺接近了。我叫老實人阿里·法基爾,幸會幸會。

column1: You know, the stuff you need when you go to the diner to buy dinner around here.  Or maybe you don't know.  In that case, when you get some dinars, I'll be glad to trade some dandy centimes for them, at say, one for ten.
column2: 你曉得的,就是在這一帶上小館子買晚飯時用得著的玩意兒。也許你不曉得。那這樣吧,等你弄到幾枚第納爾,我很樂意拿幾枚漂亮的生丁跟你換,比方說,一換十。

column1: I've seen an awful lot of sauruses and I've seen a lot of awful sauruses.
column2: I've seen an awful lot of sauruses and I've seen a lot of awful sauruses.

column1: The saurus is the ship of the desert, and you can't ship it far enough to suit me.  All a saurus takes is a little grass (say, a bale), some water (around 20 gallons), and a little love, and it will follow you anywhere, which can get mighty embarrassing at times, believe me.
column2: The saurus is the ship of the desert, and you can't ship it far enough to suit me.  All a saurus takes is a little grass (say, a bale), some water (around 20 gallons), and a little love, and it will follow you anywhere, which can get mighty embarrassing at times, believe me.

column1: Picture yourself, mounting your very own saurus for the first time.  You get up, you fall off, you mount up again and sit proud in the saddle, knowing you can ride with the best of them.
column2: Picture yourself, mounting your very own saurus for the first time.  You get up, you fall off, you mount up again and sit proud in the saddle, knowing you can ride with the best of them.

column1: It makes cents to carry centimes here.
column2: 在這兒帶著生丁,可是「分分(cents)」精打細算的明智之舉。

column1: Ah, I wondered when we would get around to a reasonable question.  Do you have any idea how much a Saurus is worth?  What price glory?  Never mind that now.  I'll be happy to sell you a saurus for 100 dinars, but I'll settle for 50.
column2: Ah, I wondered when we would get around to a reasonable question.  Do you have any idea how much a Saurus is worth?  What price glory?  Never mind that now.  I'll be happy to sell you a saurus for 100 dinars, but I'll settle for 50.

column1: It grieves me to the last degree that my knowledge of that subject could never be of any use to you.
column2: 我懂的那點東西對你毫無用處,這讓我難過到了極點。

column1: I regret it very much, but I can not help you with that.
column2: 我深感抱歉,但這件事我幫不了你。

column1: The duty of a guard in Shapeir is to make certain all people are safe from violence or ill-doing.
column2: 夏皮爾衛兵的職責,是確保所有人免遭暴力與惡行。

column1: It is said that the duty of the guards of Raseir is to make certain all people obey the many laws.
column2: 據說拉希爾衛兵的職責,就是確保所有人都遵守那一條條律法。

column1: There is little I can do about such a thing except to help the merchants get away and try to keep the fire from spreading.
column2: 這種事我能做的不多,頂多幫商人們脫身,再設法不讓火勢蔓延。

column1: The Water Elemental destroys all who go near her.
column2: 水元素精靈會毀滅一切靠近她的人。

column1: An Air Elemental grows in power the more it blows.
column2: 風元素精靈颳得越狂,力量就越驚人。

column1: The Earth Elemental crushes everything in its path.
column2: 土元素精靈會輾碎一切擋路之物。

column1: I know only that it is something to be greatly feared.
column2: 我只知道,那是個極其可怕的東西。

column1: The Magic Shop owner might help you with such questions.
column2: 魔法店的老闆或許能解答你這類問題。

column1: The Sultan (May He Reign Forever) is truly wise and powerful.
column2: The Sultan (May He Reign Forever) is truly wise and powerful.

column1: The Sultan's army went out to discover what happened to the Emir of Raseir.  A great magic stopped them, and few returned.
column2: 蘇丹派軍隊去查拉希爾埃米爾的下落。一股強大的魔法擋住了他們,生還者寥寥無幾。

column1: The palace is a marvelous place to work.
column2: The palace is a marvelous place to work.

column1: Traveling through the desert is much safer in a caravan.
column2: Traveling through the desert is much safer in a caravan.

column1: The coins of Shapeir are stamped with an image of the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Rule Forever).
column2: The coins of Shapeir are stamped with an image of the Sultan Harun Al-Rashid (May He Rule Forever).

column1: It is foolish to ask that of me.
column2: 拿這種事問我,未免太傻了。

column1: The Adventurer's Guild is on the west end of the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 冒險者公會在戰士廣場的西端。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn is in the Gate Plaza.
column2: 「卡塔之尾客棧」在城門廣場。

column1: The summer heat is dangerous for those who are not used to it.
column2: 對不習慣的人來說,這夏日酷熱十分危險。

column1: The Fountain is the heart of the city.
column2: The Fountain is the heart of the city.

column1: It is said that in Raseir, it is death to break the smallest of laws.
column2: 據說在拉希爾,哪怕違犯最小的律法,也是死罪。

column1: Her shop is at the south end of Dinar Tarik.
column2: 她的店在 Dinar Tarik 的南端。

column1: His shop is at the end of the Tarik of Stars.
column2: 他的店在「群星之街」(Tarik of Stars)的盡頭。

column1: I have been by her house on Kudra Darb.  It has an eye painted upon the door.
column2: I have been by her house on Kudra Darb.  It has an eye painted upon the door.

column1: His poetry is very good.  I listen to him when he reads in the Plaza.
column2: His poetry is very good.  I listen to him when he reads in the Plaza.

column1: There are merchants for almost anything in this town.
column2: 這鎮上幾乎什麼東西都有商人在賣。

column1: Only the Katta can stand to work the Bazaars in the summer.  The rest of the year, you cannot walk through the Plazas, there are so many merchants.
column2: Only the Katta can stand to work the Bazaars in the summer.  The rest of the year, you cannot walk through the Plazas, there are so many merchants.

column1: Foo.
column2: Foo.

column1: One must never travel in the desert without a source of water.  You will need to buy a waterskin from one of the merchants.
column2: 進沙漠絕不可沒有水源。你得向某位商人買個水袋才行。

column1: A compass is useful to keep from getting lost.  Food supplies to keep away hunger are a good idea.
column2: 羅盤能幫你免於迷路,很管用。備些口糧以防飢餓,也是個好主意。

column1: The one thing that will aid you most to stay alive in the desert is a good riding saurus.
column2: The one thing that will aid you most to stay alive in the desert is a good riding saurus.

column1: It costs but 50 centimes to stable a saurus there.  It shall be watered and fed regularly.
column2: It costs but 50 centimes to stable a saurus there.  It shall be watered and fed regularly.

column1: It is against our laws to bring a saurus into the city, since it can be fierce in defense of an owner.
column2: It is against our laws to bring a saurus into the city, since it can be fierce in defense of an owner.

column1: The Dervish lives in the Oasis to the South.  He knows everything that goes on in the desert.
column2: The Dervish lives in the Oasis to the South.  He knows everything that goes on in the desert.

column1: A skareen is the distance from here to over there.
column2: 一個「斯卡林」,就是從這裡到那邊的距離。

column1: Bedouins are nomads whose home is the entire desert.
column2: Bedouins are nomads whose home is the entire desert.

column1: The Oasis is a place in the desert where there is life-giving water.  It is five skareen lengths to the South and three skareen lengths to the east from the town overlook.
column2: The Oasis is a place in the desert where there is life-giving water.  It is five skareen lengths to the South and three skareen lengths to the east from the town overlook.

column1: Dunes are the waves of the desert.
column2: 沙丘,就是沙漠的波浪。

column1: I have seen a Griffin flying out over the desert.  It must have a nest somewhere near here.
column2: 我見過一頭獅鷲飛越沙漠。牠的巢一定就在這附近某處。

column1: Griffins tend to nest near the mountains.
column2: 獅鷲多半在山邊築巢。

column1: The bazaar is wherever the merchants are.  In the summer only the kattas can stand the heat.  Other times of year, you cannot move in the plazas for so many stands and shops.
column2: The bazaar is wherever the merchants are.  In the summer only the kattas can stand the heat.  Other times of year, you cannot move in the plazas for so many stands and shops.

column1: It has been said that you are going to journey to Raseir.  The Katta all hope for your safety and wish for your success, Friend of the Katta.
column2: 聽說您即將動身前往拉希爾。卡塔族都祈願您一路平安、馬到成功,卡塔之友。

column1: The thing of Earth was most fearsome.  The very city trembled from it.   We are most fortunate that Shema and Shameen brought you here.
column2: The thing of Earth was most fearsome.  The very city trembled from it.   We are most fortunate that Shema and Shameen brought you here.

column1: It was most brave and wise of you to defeat the thing of air, Hero of Shapeir.  Again, the city is safe through your actions.
column2: 您擊敗那團風怪,既英勇又睿智,夏皮爾的英雄。城市再一次因您的作為而平安無事。

column1: Hero of the North, a thousand thanks for defeating the thing of fire.   We were forced to flee for our lives when it set the stand of Alichica aflame.
column2: 北方的英雄,萬分感謝您擊敗了那團火怪。它把阿利奇卡的攤子點著時,我們只能拚命逃命。

column1: Whatsa matta you?  You no like a big deal when you see it?
column2: 你是咋了?好東西擺眼前你還不識貨?

column1: You can't see inside the waterskin.
column2: You can't see inside the waterskin.

column1: waterskin?  What waterskin?
column2: waterskin?  What waterskin?

column1: My name, he is Alichica
column2: My name, he is Alichica

column1: Ehhh...you wood knot want it.
column2: Ehhh...you wood knot want it.

column1: Sorry boss, all I got is a buncha burnt wood.
column2: Sorry boss, all I got is a buncha burnt wood.

column1: Hey!  What you think this is, a charity?  You ain't gotta the money.
column2: 喂!你當這是在做慈善哪?你錢都不夠。

column1: Wait a minute!  What you think I am, a money changer?   You money, itsa no good.  You need to getta this changed to dinars and centimes before you can buya something.
column2: Wait a minute!  What you think I am, a money changer?   You money, itsa no good.  You need to getta this changed to dinars and centimes before you can buya something.

column2: 喂,你!想買東西就過來這兒!

column2: It's hot, it's red, it's something you cook with, it burned my stand down.

column2: 你瞧著就是個體面人,我這兒可有筆好買賣等著你!

column1: Special today only, I sell you a genuine Made-in-Japan, Glow-in-Dark Turban which says "My father went to Shapeir and all he brought me was..."
column2: Special today only, I sell you a genuine Made-in-Japan, Glow-in-Dark Turban which says "My father went to Shapeir and all he brought me was..."

column1: But maybe you gotta no kids?  Then what you want is what I gotta right here--a "Shapeir in a Bottle".  You shake it and you see Shapeir in a sandstorm.  I sell it justa for you for only twice what it worth.
column2: But maybe you gotta no kids?  Then what you want is what I gotta right here--a "Shapeir in a Bottle".  You shake it and you see Shapeir in a sandstorm.  I sell it justa for you for only twice what it worth.

column1: The stand seems to have been badly burned.
column2: The stand seems to have been badly burned.

column1: The odd character at this stand seems vaguely familiar.
column2: 這攤子上那個古怪傢伙,看著有點面熟。

column1: I am sorry, but I really cannot afford to give you any more free flowers.  Please visit the Money Changer.
column2: I am sorry, but I really cannot afford to give you any more free flowers.  Please visit the Money Changer.

column1: You will need to go to the Changer of Money to trade your coins for those of Shapeir.  But since you are a Hero to my people, you may have some of my flowers anyway.
column2: You will need to go to the Changer of Money to trade your coins for those of Shapeir.  But since you are a Hero to my people, you may have some of my flowers anyway.

column1: Since you are a friend of my people, then you may have some flowers even though you have not the money.
column2: Since you are a friend of my people, then you may have some flowers even though you have not the money.

column1: I hope these flowers will bring you happiness.
column2: 願這些花能為您帶來歡喜。

column1: You put the flowers away.
column2: You put the flowers away.

column1: Please approach my flower stand if you wish to buy my flowers, Effendi.
column2: Please approach my flower stand if you wish to buy my flowers, Effendi.

column2: My name is Lisha, which means "Quiet One" in the language of the Katta.

column1: A strange sort of person, even for a human.
column2: 真是個怪人——即便以人類的標準來看也一樣。

column1: The strange man decided to leave town with his friend of the golden hair and strange social behavior.  He said something about going to "Fredonia."
column2: The strange man decided to leave town with his friend of the golden hair and strange social behavior.  He said something about going to "Fredonia."

column1: Flowers of beauty, flowers of fragrances most wonderful.   Flowers to bring cheer to your day.
column2: 美麗的花,芬芳到不可思議的花。能讓你一整天心花怒放的花。

column1: The musician seems too busy to answer you.
column2: 那樂師似乎忙得無暇答理你。

column1: You're not close enough to the musician.
column2: You're not close enough to the musician.

column1: You don't dare, with such a vicious snake so near.
column2: You don't dare, with such a vicious snake so near.

column2: You see a Katta musician playing some sort of musical instrument and charming a dangerous-looking snake.  There are some brass coins thrown onto his carpet.

column2: 那條蛇某天拐錯了彎,結果聽起一個自以為是班尼·古德曼的卡塔族人吹奏。

column1: You need a snake like you need a five-pound desert mosquito of your very own.
column2: 你需要一條蛇,就跟你需要一隻自己專屬的五磅重沙漠蚊子一樣(根本不需要)。

column1: Charmed, I'm sure.
column2: 幸會幸會,真是榮幸。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn sign has been burned beyond recognition
column2: The Katta's Tail Inn sign has been burned beyond recognition

column1: The sign says that the door leads to the Katta's Tail Inn.
column2: 招牌上寫著,這扇門通往「卡塔之尾」客棧。

column1: Fire sale today!   I sella you real wood burned by real Fire Elemental.  Be the first on your block to owna this.
column2: Fire sale today!   I sella you real wood burned by real Fire Elemental.  Be the first on your block to owna this.

column1: You!  Stranger!  You justa in luck.  I gotta everything you need right here.
column2: 喂!外地人!你運氣好得很哪。你要的東西,我這兒樣樣都有。

column2: 你要地圖?我這兒有地圖!

column2: 那樂師一邊演奏,一邊點頭微笑。

column2: You pay for the compass and put it away.

column2: Sorry, boss--I justa ran outa them.  Maybe you come back tomorrow and I still notta have it.

column1: Lemme see if I got one.
column2: 讓我瞧瞧還有沒有貨。

column1: Here you go, boss.  One map for %s.
column2: Here you go, boss.  One map for %s.

column1: You pay for the map and put it away.
column2: You pay for the map and put it away.

column1: Gronk!!
column2: Gronk!!

column1: Will you please take this Saurus back to the stables?   I have been unable to move it back there.   It seems to have been waiting for you.
column2: 能否請您把這頭索魯斯帶回馬廄?我怎麼也牽不動牠,牠似乎一直在等您。

column1: The Saurus is perfectly willing to follow you back to the stables.
column2: 這頭索魯斯非常樂意跟著你回馬廄。

column1: You're not close enough to the beggar.
column2: You're not close enough to the beggar.

column1: He doesn't need it.
column2: He doesn't need it.

column1: Centime for the poor, Effendi?  Centime for the poor?
column2: 行行好,賞個小錢給窮人吧,閣下?賞個小錢給窮人吧?

column1: Alas, Effendi, money from the land of Spielburg is of no use to such as I.  You need to exchange it for the coin of our realm.
column2: Alas, Effendi, money from the land of Spielburg is of no use to such as I.  You need to exchange it for the coin of our realm.

column1: You have no food for even yourself.
column2: You have no food for even yourself.

column1: What a wonder!  Is it a dream?  Oh, Effendi, there is no end to your goodness.
column2: 真是奇蹟!莫非是夢?啊,閣下,您的善心沒有止境。

column1: Thank you, thank you, Effendi.  You shame me with your kindness.
column2: 謝謝您,謝謝您,閣下。您的善意讓我受之有愧。

column1: Forgive me, Effendi.  I am not worthy of your kindness.
column2: 請原諒我,閣下。我當不起您的善意。

column1: You have lured the Fire Elemental away from the Plaza.  Now you must try to weaken and capture it.
column2: You have lured the Fire Elemental away from the Plaza.  Now you must try to weaken and capture it.

column1: You're already laying it out as fast as you can!
column2: You're already laying it out as fast as you can!

column1: There's no need to drop more incense -- the Fire Elemental is well inside the alley now.
column2: 不必再丟薰香了——火元素精靈現在已經好好待在巷子裡了。

column1: Unless you feel like scraping some off the alley floor, you'd better get used to the fact that you are incenseless.
column2: Unless you feel like scraping some off the alley floor, you'd better get used to the fact that you are incenseless.

column1: You should lead the Elemental farther into the alley.
column2: 你應該把元素精靈再往巷子深處引。

column1: Ok, "You worthless, second-hand, unpolishable, thriftstore-hatched piece of unwanted brass!  Your kind makes me sick!  (and various grumblings)."  How's that?
column2: Ok, "You worthless, second-hand, unpolishable, thriftstore-hatched piece of unwanted brass!  Your kind makes me sick!  (and various grumblings)."  How's that?

column1: You drop the lamp.  It is very hurt and angry.   "I have a new flame now," you say to the lamp, "and she's the light of my life.  You couldn't hold a candle to her."  But inside your heart burns with the loss of your bright young friend, the lamp.
column2: 你放下油燈。它又傷心又憤怒。「我現在有了新的火焰,」你對油燈說,「她是我生命中的光。你拍馬也比不上她。」但你心裡其實正為失去這位明亮的小夥伴——油燈——而隱隱作痛。

column1: You have no water.
column2: You have no water.

column1: You should let the elemental get farther into the alley first.
column2: You should let the elemental get farther into the alley first.

column1: The Elemental is too far away to douse.
column2: The Elemental is too far away to douse.

column1: You barely manage to escape the Fire Elemental's searing heat.
column2: 你勉強逃過了火元素精靈灼人的高熱。

column2: 被火烤可真讓你「火」大,對吧?歡迎加入我們這個只收灰燼成員的尊榮俱樂部。

column1: As you pick up the lamp and rub it, a small flame appears.  When you rub it again, the flame goes away.  You now have a magic lamp.  You put the lamp safely away in your backpack.
column2: 你撿起油燈擦了擦,一小簇火焰冒了出來。再擦一次,火焰便消失了。你得到了一盞神燈。你把油燈妥善地收進背包裡。

column1: You put the lamp away.
column2: You put the lamp away.

column1: You don't see a lamp here.
column2: You don't see a lamp here.

column1: You can see the Fire Elemental burning brightly inside the brass lamp.
column2: You can see the Fire Elemental burning brightly inside the brass lamp.

column1: You don't see one here.
column2: You don't see one here.

column1: Without any means of containment, the Fire Elemental makes a hasty retreat back into the plaza.
column2: 由於沒有任何能收容它的器具,火元素精靈急忙退回了廣場。

column1: You gonna ask me questions, or you gonna buy what I sell?  I no make-a living off of answers.
column2: 你是要問我問題,還是要買我賣的東西?我可不是靠回答問題過活的。

column1: You needa answer to that, you aska someone else.
column2: 那問題你想要答案,去問別人吧。

column1: This is a nice-a town, a nice-a land.
column2: 這是個好地方,好城好土哪。

column1: I understand the sightseeing business is notta too good there.
column2: 我聽說那兒的觀光生意不太好做哪。

column1: The Fire Elemental, he burna down this place.  I guess maybe he no like-a souvenirs.
column2: 那火元素啊,把這地方燒個精光。我猜大概是他不愛紀念品吧。

column1: Thatsa what goes in the Elementary Canal.
column2: 那是流進「元素運河」裡的東西啦。

column1: Thatsa what inherits the wind when-a Elemental makes a will.
column2: 元素立遺囑時,繼承到的就是這陣風囉。

column1: Thatsa dirty word to me.
column2: 那詞兒對我來說可是髒話。

column1: You take-a something and you make it alive.  Then you make-a the something dig a ditch.  Thatsa Elementary Canal.
column2: 你拿個東西讓它活過來,然後叫這東西去挖溝渠。這就是「元素運河」啦。

column1: Actually, itsa like-a the alphabet.  You know, "H-I-J-K-Elemental-P.
column2: Actually, itsa like-a the alphabet.  You know, "H-I-J-K-Elemental-P.

column1: I gotta great coconut ashtray with his face on it.  You putta your cigarette in his mouth and smoke rings come outa his ears.
column2: 我這兒有個超棒的椰殼煙灰缸,上頭刻著他的臉。你把香菸塞進他嘴裡,菸圈就會從他耳朵裡冒出來。

column1: You wanna know where the Palace is, justa look on the map.  You finda the palace right by the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: You wanna know where the Palace is, justa look on the map.  You finda the palace right by the Plaza of the Palace.

column1: Sorry, I don't carry vans.
column2: 抱歉,我這兒不賣廂型車。

column1: Isn't that where you take-a ice cream and putta whipped cream and fudge onna top?
column2: 那不就是拿冰淇淋,上頭再加鮮奶油跟巧克力醬的那玩意兒嗎?

column1: I can no sell you something unless you gotta some dinars or centimes.
column2: 你得有點第納爾或生丁,我才能賣東西給你。

column1: I tella you where to find the Guild Hall.  You go outa the Plaza here and take-a the street until you can turna left.  You go left until you can no go more.  Then you go right.
column2: I tella you where to find the Guild Hall.  You go outa the Plaza here and take-a the street until you can turna left.  You go left until you can no go more.  Then you go right.

column1: You needa my map to find it.
column2: 你得靠我的地圖才找得到它。

column1: I gotta carved statue of Shapeir that tells you what the weather is like.  You leave it outa doors.  Then you picka it up.  If itsa wet, then the day is rainy.  If itsa warm, then the day is hot.
column2: 我這兒有尊夏皮爾雕像,能告訴你天氣如何。你把它擺在門外,然後撿起來摸摸看。要是濕的,那今天就是下雨;要是熱的,那今天就是大熱天。

column1: The way to the fountain?  Take-a the road Junub Tarik.  Keep going until you get wet.  Then you finda the fountain.
column2: 去噴泉的路?走「朱努布塔里克」那條路,一直走到你被淋濕為止,那就找到噴泉啦。

column1: I heard that there was a stranger in town who asks a lotta questions.
column2: 我聽說城裡來了個外地人,問題多得不得了。

column1: You want to find the Money Changer's, right?  No problem, boss.
column2: You want to find the Money Changer's, right?  No problem, boss.

column1: Well, you go north until you see a way to go left, and you take-a the way.  Now you go until you see a way to go right, and you take-a thatta way.
column2: 這樣啊,你往北走,看到能往左的路就走那條。接著一直走,看到能往右的路,就走那條。

column1: You go until you can see a way to go right and you go to the right.  Then you go until you can go to the right again.  Thatsa right way.  Go thatta way until you can turn right.  Go right until you come to a place where you can go right again.  Turn left.  Follow the road and you no can miss it.
column2: You go until you can see a way to go right and you go to the right.  Then you go until you can go to the right again.  Thatsa right way.  Go thatta way until you can turn right.  Go right until you come to a place where you can go right again.  Turn left.  Follow the road and you no can miss it.

column1: To find the Astrologer, you go uppa the street until you can turn right.  Then you go downa the street until you can turn right.  When you see a door at the end of the street, you finda your place.
column2: 要找占星師,你沿街往上走到能右轉為止,再沿街往下走到能右轉為止。看見街尾那扇門時,你就找著地方了。

column1: You think maybe you go visit the Enchantress, huh?  Well, go north from the Plaza of the Fountain.  When you come to a place where you can go south, go thatta way.  Keep going until you go north for two times.  The next time you go north, her house is the first door on the west.
column2: You think maybe you go visit the Enchantress, huh?  Well, go north from the Plaza of the Fountain.  When you come to a place where you can go south, go thatta way.  Keep going until you go north for two times.  The next time you go north, her house is the first door on the west.

column1: I notta know where he lives.
column2: 我不曉得他住哪兒。

column1: I sella you what you need.  What more can you want?
column2: 你要啥我賣啥,你還能要求啥?

column1: You gotta watch them.  When you make-a deal with a Katta, it always seem fair.  They musta be up to something.
column2: 你可得提防著他們。跟卡塔人做生意,表面上總是公道得很。準是在打什麼鬼主意。

column1: My name, he is Alichica.
column2: 我的名字嘛,叫阿利奇卡。

column1: Hey you!  I gotta justa thing!  A keychain with real Shapeir brass centime on the end.  Only costa you one dinar.
column2: 喂喂!我這兒有個好東西!鑰匙圈尾端掛著貨真價實的夏皮爾黃銅生丁幣,只要一第納爾。

column1: This you gotta see!  The ashtray of Shapeir made from imported coconut shell, hand-carved by Katta natives into face of the Sultan!  You notta finda this anywhere else!
column2: 這你非看不可!夏皮爾煙灰缸,進口椰殼製,卡塔原住民親手雕成蘇丹的臉!別處你絕對找不到!

column1: Special today only -- "Guide to Sightseeing in Beautiful Raseir".  Only costa you one dinar since it only gotta one page.
column2: Special today only -- "Guide to Sightseeing in Beautiful Raseir".  Only costa you one dinar since it only gotta one page.

column1: Since you my besta customer, I make you a deal.  I sella you a duck.
column2: 看在你是我最好的顧客份上,我給你個優惠。我賣你一隻鴨。

column1: I have-a here what you need 'cause you so smart!  Bookends!  It gotta donkey head on one side anda ass on the other.  Makesa great gift.
column2: 我這兒有你需要的東西,因為你聰明嘛!書檔!一邊是驢頭,另一邊是驢屁股。送禮一流。

column1: Genuine saurus foot trashcan for Mother's Day!  Show your mother what you thinka her.
column2: Genuine saurus foot trashcan for Mother's Day!  Show your mother what you thinka her.

column1: Why a duck?  I tella you why a duck.
column2: 為啥是鴨子?我來告訴你為啥是鴨子。
