column1: Have patience!  The bartender doesn't seem too interested in moving quickly.
column2: Have patience!  The bartender doesn't seem too interested in moving quickly.

column1: It looks more like a Djinn Sling.
column2: It looks more like a Djinn Sling.

column1: It looks fizzy and dangerous.
column2: 看起來冒著泡,挺危險的。

column1: It looks more like coffee.
column2: It looks more like coffee.

column1: It looks like brown ooze.
column2: 看起來像一灘褐色的黏液。

column1: The drink is sweet, with a very bitter aftertaste.
column2: 這酒甜甜的,後勁卻苦得很。

column1: You drink the bitter, thick liquid.
column2: 你喝下那濃稠苦澀的液體。

column1: It has an odd, almost chemical, taste.
column2: 喝起來有股怪味,幾乎像化學藥劑似的。

column1: You feel strange for a few seconds, but the feeling passes.  Ferrari starts to ask some very probing questions, but you evade most of them.
column2: 你恍惚了幾秒,那感覺隨即過去。費拉里開始旁敲側擊地盤問,但大半問題都被你含糊帶了過去。

column1: You are a very closed-mouth individual.  You handle your drink very well.  I trust you will handle yourself in Raseir as well.
column2: 你這人嘴可真嚴。酒量也好得很。我信你在拉希爾,也能把自己照看得一樣好。

column1: You feel a bit odd.  Signor Ferrari smiles at you and starts to question you.  He is particularly interested in Shapeir, and questions you thoroughly.  You are having a difficult time trying not to say too much.
column2: 你覺得有點不對勁。費拉里先生衝你一笑,開始盤問起來。他對夏皮爾尤其感興趣,問得鉅細靡遺。你費了好大勁,才忍住沒說漏嘴。

column1: Humm, there is much to what you say, and much more to what you don't say, I suspect.
column2: 唔,你說的話裡頭大有文章,而你沒說的,只怕更耐人尋味。

column1: You start to feel a bit dizzy.  Signor Ferrari smiles at you and starts to ask questions about yourself.  You find yourself answering, telling your friend all about your adventures.  He is particularly interested in Shapeir, and questions you thoroughly.  After a while, though, you start to get a headache.  Ferrari frowns.
column2: You start to feel a bit dizzy.  Signor Ferrari smiles at you and starts to ask questions about yourself.  You find yourself answering, telling your friend all about your adventures.  He is particularly interested in Shapeir, and questions you thoroughly.  After a while, though, you start to get a headache.  Ferrari frowns.

column1: You have led a most interesting and charmed life, it seems.  But it would take a miracle to restore Raseir, and Khaveen has outlawed miracles.  You are very dangerous to me, for your actions will have serious repercussions.  Nevertheless, you may have your uses.
column2: You have led a most interesting and charmed life, it seems.  But it would take a miracle to restore Raseir, and Khaveen has outlawed miracles.  You are very dangerous to me, for your actions will have serious repercussions.  Nevertheless, you may have your uses.

column1: Now, now.  Pleasure before business.  Here come our drinks even as we speak.
column2: 行了行了。先享樂,再談正事。瞧,咱們的酒這不就上來了。

column1: Let us drink to our fortunate meeting!
column2: 來,為咱們這場有緣的相逢乾一杯!

column1: I am very pleased to make your acquaintance.  I have heard much about you already.
column2: 能認識你我非常高興。關於你的事,我早就聽說了不少。

column1: Ugarte, here, provides an invaluable service to this community.  He obtains for others things which are otherwise unattainable.
column2: Ugarte, here, provides an invaluable service to this community.  He obtains for others things which are otherwise unattainable.

column1: For a nominal fee, of course.
column2: 當然嘍,得收一筆象徵性的費用。

column1: It seems he has some information for you.
column2: 看樣子,他手上有些消息要給你。

column1: A rumor, perhaps, that may influence your actions.  I will be happy to share such information for, say, five dinars?
column2: 也許是個能左右你下一步的傳言。這消息我很樂意分享——要不就五第納爾?

column1: Well, seeing as you are a hero and all, perhaps we could settle for say, 3 dinars?
column2: Well, seeing as you are a hero and all, perhaps we could settle for say, 3 dinars?

column1: I am sorry, but I am just a poor businessman.  Surely a hero such as yourself can spare a few measly dinars for information which might save his life?
column2: 抱歉啊,我不過是個窮做買賣的。像您這樣的英雄,總擠得出區區幾枚第納爾,換一條也許能保命的消息吧?

column1: Forgive me, %s, but I do not accept foreign coin.
column2: 請見諒,%s,小的不收外地的錢幣。

column1: You find you do not have the money.  Ugarte quickly loses interest in you.
column2: 你發現自己根本沒這筆錢。烏加特立刻對你失了興致。

column1: Well, what kind of hero are you?  You come into town acting like you are a wealthy man, and make poor honest businessmen such as myself work for nothing.
column2: Well, what kind of hero are you?  You come into town acting like you are a wealthy man, and make poor honest businessmen such as myself work for nothing.

column1: Khaveen has sent the word out to his men that you are to be watched at all times.  It seems that, ah, Someone is very interested in your actions.  You should be aware that Khaveen does not usually greet strangers at the gate.  You were expected.
column2: 哈維因已經放話給手下,要他們時時刻刻盯著你。看來啊,「某位人物」對你的一舉一動很感興趣。你該明白,哈維因平常可不會親自到城門口迎接陌生人。你的到來,是早在意料之中的。

column1: Why would He take an interest?
column2: 祂為什麼會感興趣?

column1: There is the little matter of a prophecy to be fulfilled and this one may be the one.
column2: There is the little matter of a prophecy to be fulfilled and this one may be the one.

column1: I suspected before that you were a very dangerous man to have around, %s.  It seems I am correct.
column2: 我先前就懷疑,留你在身邊是個很危險的人物,%s。看來我沒猜錯。

column1: Still, it will be very interesting to see how this game plays out.  You may stay tonight in the first room past the door on the left.
column2: 不過呢,瞧瞧這場好戲怎麼收場,倒也挺有意思。今晚你可以住在左邊那道門進去的第一間房。

column1: You may live to regret arriving here with no money.  Do you think to live on charity?  Wilmer, show our poor hero here to quarters more fitting his purse.
column2: 身上沒帶錢就跑來這兒,你早晚會後悔的。你以為能靠施捨過活?威爾默,給咱們這位窮英雄安排個更配得上他錢包的住處。

column1: Just tell me if you have the bird!
column2: 你倒是說,那隻鳥到底在不在你手上!

column1: Of all the djinn joints in all the world, you had to walk into this one.
column2: Of all the djinn joints in all the world, you had to walk into this one.

column1: A hush comes over the bar as people notice you and eye you sullenly.
column2: A hush comes over the bar as people notice you and eye you sullenly.

column1: Come join me here, my dear sir.  You are still a novelty in Raseir, and I take a vested interest in all things new.  It is time we discussed some things together, you and I.
column2: 過來陪我坐坐,我親愛的先生。在拉希爾你還是個新鮮人物,而我對一切新事物都頗有興趣。咱倆,是時候談談一些事了。

column1: It is a pity about our friend Ugarte, is it not?  I can see you are as broken up by the affair as I am.
column2: 咱們那位朋友烏加特的事,真是可惜啊,是不是?我看得出,你跟我一樣為這事傷透了心。

column1: However, it seems he asked someone the wrong questions about you.  That was just a performance put on for your benefit this morning.
column2: 不過嘛,看樣子他為了打聽你的事,問錯了人。今早那一齣,不過是專演給你看的一場戲罷了。

column1: Khaveen has known about Ugarte's water smuggling for a long time.  A most dangerous man, Khaveen.  Almost as dangerous as you are.
column2: 哈維因早就知道烏加特走私水的勾當了。哈維因這人,危險得很。差不多跟你一樣危險。

column1: Do you have it?  Do you have the bird?
column2: 你拿到了嗎?那隻鳥在你手上嗎?

column1: Will you look what crawled in from the desert?  He has been out in the sun so long, his hair has gone yellow!
column2: 瞧瞧這是從沙漠裡爬進來的什麼玩意兒?在太陽底下曬太久,頭髮都曬黃啦!

column1: If you wish to discuss something to our mutual advantage, come sit down.
column2: 想談點對咱倆都有好處的事,就過來坐下。

column1: Now, now, Wilmer, he is a stranger to our fair city.  You wouldn't want to give the wrong opinion about the hospitality of Raseir.
column2: 行了行了,威爾默,他可是初來咱們這座好城的外鄉人。你總不想讓人對拉希爾的待客之道留下壞印象吧。

column1: Come here, stranger.  I see you have already made the acquaintance of Khaveen, our illustrious Captain of the Raseirian Guard, so perhaps you already have an idea of Raseirian hospitality.  Come here, sit down, and let us have a little chat, you and I.
column2: Come here, stranger.  I see you have already made the acquaintance of Khaveen, our illustrious Captain of the Raseirian Guard, so perhaps you already have an idea of Raseirian hospitality.  Come here, sit down, and let us have a little chat, you and I.

column1: You give Signor Ferrari the bird.  A puzzled look crosses his face, and suddenly you realize that he was referring to the Black Falcon.
column2: 你朝費拉里先生比了根中指。他臉上閃過一絲疑惑,你這才猛然意識到——他說的「鳥」指的是那隻黑獵鷹。

column1: Bring it here, quickly.  Give it to me!
column2: 快拿過來,給我!

column1: No?  No?  What do you mean, "No?"
column2: No?  No?  What do you mean, "No?"

column1: You leave Ferrari a sorely disappointed man.
column2: 你走的時候,留下一個失望透頂的費拉里。

column1: Perhaps you did not hear me.  I asked if you have the bird.
column2: 也許你沒聽清。我問你,那隻鳥在不在你手上。

column1: I said give me the bird!
column2: 我說了,把鳥給我!

column1: You slept fitfully, dreaming of rat-like monsters pulling at you.  In your dream, you felt cruelly exposed, but you re-covered.
column2: 你睡得很不安穩,夢見一群老鼠似的怪物在拉扯你。夢裡你覺得自己赤裸裸地暴露在外,毫無遮掩,但你又把自己重新蓋好了。

column1: Signor Ferrari sends his regrets, but he had some rather disturbing news.  He will talk to you this evening.
column2: 費拉里先生托我向你致歉,他收到了一些頗為棘手的消息。今晚他再跟你談。

column1: As you enter, the sounds of the bar go still.
column2: 你一進門,酒館裡的喧鬧聲頓時靜了下來。

column1: It has been a pleasure dealing with you, my dear sir.
column2: 跟您打交道真是愉快,我親愛的先生。

column1: You are led out of the Blue Parrot, where you are quickly arrested for vagrancy.  You spend a long while in Khaveen's dungeon, after all.
column2: You are led out of the Blue Parrot, where you are quickly arrested for vagrancy.  You spend a long while in Khaveen's dungeon, after all.

column1: Ferrari is fat, but his patience is thin.  Your delay has cost you his hospitality.  Without a place to spend the night you are arrested for vagrancy and thrown into Khaveen's dungeons.
column2: Ferrari is fat, but his patience is thin.  Your delay has cost you his hospitality.  Without a place to spend the night you are arrested for vagrancy and thrown into Khaveen's dungeons.

column1: You wander out of the inn and are promptly arrested for vagrancy.  Had you not angered Signor Ferrari, you might have received a friendly warning first.
column2: 你晃出旅店,立刻就因遊蕩罪被捕。要不是你惹惱了費拉里閣下,說不定還能先得到一句善意的警告呢。

column1: As you leave the room, you watch the jovial expression harden to a glare.  It is obvious that Signor Ferrari is not used to being ignored.  You also find that you are no longer welcome at the Blue Parrot Inn.  Since you have no place to spend the night, you are soon arrested for vagrancy.  It is hard to be a hero in the bottom of a dungeon.
column2: As you leave the room, you watch the jovial expression harden to a glare.  It is obvious that Signor Ferrari is not used to being ignored.  You also find that you are no longer welcome at the Blue Parrot Inn.  Since you have no place to spend the night, you are soon arrested for vagrancy.  It is hard to be a hero in the bottom of a dungeon.

column1: Ferrari looks at you, his eyes glinting with malign greed.  He says he will not be thwarted by some half-baked hero.  Suddenly you feel something behind you and find Wilmer grinning with two thin daggers against your back.  The last words you hear are "The Falcon.  I finally have the Falcon."
column2: Ferrari looks at you, his eyes glinting with malign greed.  He says he will not be thwarted by some half-baked hero.  Suddenly you feel something behind you and find Wilmer grinning with two thin daggers against your back.  The last words you hear are "The Falcon.  I finally have the Falcon."

column1: Ferrari looks at you, his eyes glinting with malign greed.  He says he will not be thwarted by some half-baked hero.  Suddenly you feel something behind you and find Wilmer grinning as he presses two thin daggers into your back.  The last words you hear are "It seems I must keep searching for The Falcon."
column2: Ferrari looks at you, his eyes glinting with malign greed.  He says he will not be thwarted by some half-baked hero.  Suddenly you feel something behind you and find Wilmer grinning as he presses two thin daggers into your back.  The last words you hear are "It seems I must keep searching for The Falcon."

column1: Even as you start, you feel a dagger strike you and see another gleam in Wilmer's hand before he throws it.  Then you see nothing at all.
column2: 你才剛動,就感到一把匕首扎進身體,還瞥見威爾默手裡另一把寒光一閃,接著便被擲了出來。然後,你眼前一片漆黑。

column1: When Signor Ferrari realizes that in fact you do not have the black Falcon, he frowns and motions quickly to Wilmer.  Before you can turn, you hear the characteristic "thuk" of a knife imbedding itself into a back.  Your back.
column2: When Signor Ferrari realizes that in fact you do not have the black Falcon, he frowns and motions quickly to Wilmer.  Before you can turn, you hear the characteristic "thuk" of a knife imbedding itself into a back.  Your back.

column1: Slurred speech... unsteady gait... Yup.  You're dead, alright.
column2: Slurred speech... unsteady gait... Yup.  You're dead, alright.

column1: If you don't mind me saying so, you look like a man with a mission.  I like that in a man.
column2: 恕我直言,你看上去像個身負使命的人。我就欣賞這種人。

column1: But perhaps a word of caution.  In a city such as Raseir, there are many depths to the problems.
column2: 不過容我提醒一句。在拉希爾這樣的城市裡,問題往往深不見底。

column1: Do not try to change things here too quickly.
column2: 別想太快改變這裡的一切。

column1: You will find that the system is not so bad, once you get used to it.
column2: 你會發現,這套規矩一旦習慣了,其實也沒那麼糟。

column1: However, it would be very dangerous to try to alter that system.   You might make enemies.
column2: 然而,妄想去動搖這套規矩可是極其危險的。你會樹敵。

column1: Enough of such morbid thoughts, though.  Perhaps we should share a drink in honor of our new relationship.
column2: 不過,別再想這些晦氣事了。也許我們該共飲一杯,慶祝咱倆的新交情。

column1: Would you prefer Raseirish Coffee, or a Djinn Sling?
column2: Would you prefer Raseirish Coffee, or a Djinn Sling?

column1: A wise choice.  I dislike a man who doesn't drink.
column2: 明智之選。我可不喜歡滴酒不沾的男人。

column1: It reveals a distinct lack of trust.  And you can trust me, my good sir.
column2: 這分明是信不過人哪。可你大可信我,我的好先生。

column1: A most prudent decision upon your part.
column2: 你這決定下得真是明智。

column1: Wilmer, a drink for our young friend.
column2: 威爾默,給我們這位年輕朋友來杯喝的。

column1: Wilmer, the coffee.
column2: 威爾默,上咖啡。

column1: Ah, you are obviously a very busy man, as am I.  Perhaps we can talk over drinks another time.  I will not detain you longer.   Wilmer, show our friend to the door.
column2: 啊,你顯然是個大忙人,我也一樣。或許改日我們能邊喝邊聊。我不再耽擱你了。威爾默,送我們的朋友出去。

column1: I wish to tell you a story, a fable that has a moral.
column2: 我想說個故事給你聽,一則蘊含寓意的寓言。

column1: How you choose to react to this moral is your own life.
column2: 你要怎麼看待這寓意,那是你自己的人生。

column1: As you well know, the old Emir was deposed last year, and his brother took over the Palace.
column2: 你也很清楚,老埃米爾去年被罷黜了,他的兄弟接掌了王宮。

column1: Raseir used to be a place with very few laws and restrictions; a place where the streets were filled with people and merchants and water flowed free from the fountain.
column2: 拉希爾從前是個律法與管制都極少的地方;街上滿是人潮與商賈,泉水自噴泉裡汩汩流淌,任人取用。

column1: That has changed with the coming of Khaveen and his master.
column2: 隨著哈維因和他主子的到來,這一切都變了。

column1: A new order has been established.  The weak have perished or fled.
column2: 新的秩序已然建立。弱者不是喪命,就是逃離。

column1: Only the strong who adapted remain.  Those that could not adapt were destroyed.
column2: 只有懂得順應的強者留了下來。無法順應的,都被剷除了。

column1: You are strong, but it remains to be seen how adaptable you are.
column2: 你很強,但你究竟有多懂得順應,還有待瞧瞧。

column1: It would be a great shame for you to wind up in Khaveen's dungeon.
column2: It would be a great shame for you to wind up in Khaveen's dungeon.

column1: Enough of that for now.  I have someone I would like you to meet.
column2: 這話題先到這兒。我有個人想介紹你認識。

column1: Ugarte, I would like to introduce you to %s.
column2: 烏加特,容我向你介紹%s。

column1: A simple Yes or No will suffice.
column2: A simple Yes or No will suffice.

column1: As I have said before, there is no room for amateurs in this city.
column2: 我說過了,這座城市容不下外行人。

column1: They tend to call attention to themselves, and this gives the profession a bad name.
column2: 那些人總愛招搖,反倒給這一行敗壞了名聲。

column1: If you are as skilled as I have heard, then I might have use for you.
column2: 要是你真如傳聞中那般身手了得,我或許用得上你。

column1: However, first you must prove yourself.  Are you willing to do for me a small favor?
column2: 不過,你得先證明自己。你願不願意替我辦件小事?

column1: I confess I am a bit disappointed in you, sir.  It seems I expected too much.
column2: 老實說,我對你有點失望,先生。看來是我期望太高了。

column1: Pity.  Ah, well, there will be others.  Good day, sir.
column2: 可惜。唉,算了,還會有別人的。再會,先生。

column1: I wish you to bring me a small ornament to prove your skills.
column2: 我要你帶一件小飾品來,證明你的本事。

column1: This ornament is the black figure of a bird, a falcon.
column2: 這件飾品是一隻黑色鳥形雕像,一隻獵鷹。

column1: I am an admirer of birds, you see.
column2: 你瞧,我是個愛鳥之人。

column1: The place is the window on your right as you first enter the Fountain Plaza from here.
column2: The place is the window on your right as you first enter the Fountain Plaza from here.

column1: The bird is kept in a locked cabinet in that room.
column2: 那隻鳥被鎖在那房間的櫃子裡。

column1: If you return this to me, I will make you my right-hand man.
column2: 你若把這個還給我,我就讓你當我的左右手。

column1: Wilmer there just does not have quite what it takes to give me what I want.
column2: 那邊那個威爾默,就是沒那本事給我想要的東西。

column1: To get past the guards outside, you have only to make the 'sign'.
column2: To get past the guards outside, you have only to make the 'sign'.

column1: Those guards are well paid to ignore my friends.
column2: Those guards are well paid to ignore my friends.

column1: However, any other guard you meet will not be so discreet.
column2: 不過,你遇上的其他衛兵可就沒這麼通融了。

column1: If you are caught, I will know nothing of your actions, of course.
column2: 你要是被逮,我對你的所作所為自然一概不知。

column1: Now seems like an excellent time for such a venture.
column2: 現在似乎正是幹這檔事的大好時機。

column1: Good luck, sir, and I do hope you are as skilled as I suspect.
column2: 祝你好運,先生。我真心希望你和我猜想的一樣有本事。

column1: Do you know what we have here?
column2: 你知道我手上這是什麼嗎?

column1: The sole reason I came to this desert-driven country.
column2: 這正是我來到這個風沙之國的唯一理由。

column1: For seventeen years, I have sought the black bird.
column2: 整整十七年,我一直在尋找那隻黑鳥。

column1: I alone knew that this bird was worth a sultan's ransom.
column2: 唯有我知道,這隻鳥值得上一位蘇丹的贖金。

column1: Let me look, let me see.
column2: 讓我瞧瞧,讓我看看。

column1: It seems that the joke is upon me.
column2: 看來這玩笑是開在我頭上了。

column1: This is not the real black Falcon.
column2: This is not the real black Falcon.

column1: There were four of these made.  Only one had any value.
column2: 這玩意兒一共做了四隻,只有一隻是真有價值的。

column1: It seems that I must continue my search.
column2: 看來我得繼續尋找下去了。

column1: If you are interested, I would not be adverse to having such a resourceful and skilled person such as yourself at my side.
column2: 若你有意,我倒不反對讓你這般足智多謀、身手不凡的人留在身邊。

column1: If you could but learn to curve those unfortunate heroic tendencies, you would make an excellent partner.
column2: If you could but learn to curve those unfortunate heroic tendencies, you would make an excellent partner.

column1: Leave me, now.  I have a good deal of planning to do.
column2: 現在,退下吧。我有一大堆事要謀劃。

column1: You should answer my question.  Would you prefer our famous Raseirish Coffee or a Djinn Sling?
column2: 你該回答我的問題。你想來杯我們著名的拉希爾咖啡,還是精靈賓治?

column1: The guards start to yell for reinforcements, and before you know it, you are surrounded and overwhelmed.  They drag you to the dungeons of Raseir for a most unpleasant stay.
column2: 衛兵們開始大喊著叫援兵,你還沒回過神來,就已經被團團圍住、徹底制伏。他們把你拖進拉希爾的地牢,讓你嚐嚐最難受的滋味。

column1: You are not permitted to ask questions in Raseir.
column2: 在拉希爾,你不准發問。

column1: You will only succeed in attracting more guards.
column2: 你只會招來更多衛兵。

column1: It remains a dismal sight.
column2: 那景象依舊一片淒涼。

column1: This first view of the city does not impress you.  The people of this city seem to have little civic pride.  The walls are grey from smoke and dirt, and the plaster is chipping off.
column2: This first view of the city does not impress you.  The people of this city seem to have little civic pride.  The walls are grey from smoke and dirt, and the plaster is chipping off.

column1: The Raseirian sky is ablaze with stars.
column2: The Raseirian sky is ablaze with stars.

column1: The sky is so bright that it hurts your eyes.
column2: The sky is so bright that it hurts your eyes.

column1: The plaza could use a good sweeping.
column2: The plaza could use a good sweeping.

column1: I will ask the questions around here. Learn your place!
column2: 這兒由我來發問。認清你的身分!

column1: It is forbidden for anyone to be out at night.  Return to the inn.
column2: It is forbidden for anyone to be out at night.  Return to the inn.

column1: You there!  Halt!  It is forbidden for you to be out at night.  You are guilty of breaking the law of Raseir.
column2: 那邊那個!站住!夜間外出是禁止的。你觸犯了拉希爾律法。

column1: The guard looks down, then turns aside and ignores you.
column2: The guard looks down, then turns aside and ignores you.

column1: There's no time for that.
column2: 沒空管那個。

column1: Oh, a wise guy, eh?
column2: 喔,自作聰明是吧?

column1: Stay where you are stranger, or you'll discover how sharp a weapon can be.
column2: Stay where you are stranger, or you'll discover how sharp a weapon can be.

column1: So you are the one they call the Hero of Shapeir.  Well, understand this: We don't need no stinking Heroes around here.  Do I make myself clear?
column2: So you are the one they call the Hero of Shapeir.  Well, understand this: We don't need no stinking Heroes around here.  Do I make myself clear?

column1: Do I make myself clear?
column2: 我說得夠明白了嗎?

column1: Stubborn,aren't you?  Well, perhaps some time spent in prison would teach you some manners.  To avoid prison in Raseir, you must learn to obey orders.
column2: Stubborn,aren't you?  Well, perhaps some time spent in prison would teach you some manners.  To avoid prison in Raseir, you must learn to obey orders.

column1: See that you obey all the rules posted around town.  Rule number one:  Strangers must always carry their Visa.  Do you have your Visa?
column2: See that you obey all the rules posted around town.  Rule number one:  Strangers must always carry their Visa.  Do you have your Visa?

column1: Already breaking the rules, are you?
column2: 這就開始壞規矩了是吧?

column1: Here is your Visa.  Don't leave town without it.
column2: Here is your Visa.  Don't leave town without it.

column1: No one without papers is allowed to pass!
column2: 沒有證件的一律不准通過!

column1: No one is permitted to leave the city today without Letters of Envoy, by order of Khaveen.
column2: No one is permitted to leave the city today without Letters of Envoy, by order of Khaveen.

column1: Your papers are in order.  You may pass.
column2: 你的證件沒問題,可以通過。

column1: You have no Visa?  Then you may not pass!
column2: You have no Visa?  Then you may not pass!

column1: It is too late for that, hero, you're outnumbered.
column2: It is too late for that, hero, you're outnumbered.

column1: You briefly consider struggling with the guards, but realize that there are just too many of them.
column2: 你一度想跟衛兵們搏一搏,卻發覺他們人數實在太多了。

column1: Guards!!  There is an intruder in the Gate Plaza.  To me!!!
column2: Guards!!  There is an intruder in the Gate Plaza.  To me!!!

column1: Kuwait a minute.  Visa please!
column2: 等等(科威特)一下嘛。簽證請出示!

column1: You realize that you have left your Visa in the desert, so you improvise.
column2: You realize that you have left your Visa in the desert, so you improvise.

column1: Your papers are in order.  By order of Khaveen, you must have a pleasant stay.
column2: 你的證件沒問題。奉哈維因之命,祝你住得愉快。

column1: What's this?  We don't take Dinar's Club here.
column2: 這是什麼?我們這兒可不收「第納爾俱樂部卡」。

column1: You are turned back into the desert.
column2: 你被趕回了沙漠。

column1: Do you not know it is forbidden to be out after dark in Raseir?  Show me your visa.
column2: 你不曉得在拉希爾天黑後外出是禁止的嗎?把你的簽證拿出來。

column1: You show the guard your visa.
column2: 你向衛兵出示簽證。

column1: Caught without your visa, you are hauled off to prison for a long, long stay.
column2: 你因沒帶簽證被逮個正著,被拖去大牢,要關上好長好長一段日子。

column1: You must go immediately to the Blue Parrot Inn.  If you are found again outside at night, you will be taken to prison.
column2: 你必須立刻前往藍鸚鵡客棧。要是再被發現夜間在外遊蕩,就把你關進大牢。

column1: The guard sullenly escorts you back to the Blue Parrot Inn.
column2: 衛兵臭著一張臉,把你押回藍鸚鵡客棧。

column1: As you enter Khaveen's room again, and as your eyes once again become accustomed to the darkness, you perceive a dramatic difference.  Khaveen and his bodyguards are waiting for you with swords drawn and smiles on their faces.
column2: As you enter Khaveen's room again, and as your eyes once again become accustomed to the darkness, you perceive a dramatic difference.  Khaveen and his bodyguards are waiting for you with swords drawn and smiles on their faces.

column1: You see several people gathered in the south part of the Plaza.
column2: You see several people gathered in the south part of the Plaza.

column1: Forget it.  It's too late.
column2: Forget it.  It's too late.

column1: Dirty, dirty, dirty.
column2: Dirty, dirty, dirty.

column1: The Plaza seems to be busy today.
column2: The Plaza seems to be busy today.

column1: The fountain in Raseir's Plaza of the Fountain is cracked and dry.
column2: 拉希爾噴泉廣場上的這座噴泉,早已龜裂乾涸。

column1: Do not bother the guards.
column2: Do not bother the guards.

column1: There is no one to talk to.
column2: There is no one to talk to.

column1: It's too dangerous around here right now.  More guards could arrive at any moment.
column2: It's too dangerous around here right now.  More guards could arrive at any moment.

column1: Something in the back of your mind tells you to hold on to the Water Elemental.
column2: 你心底深處有個聲音提醒你,先別放掉那隻水元素。

column1: This is none of your business.  Go away.
column2: 這不關你的事。走開。

column1: The guards of Raseir look like they are itching for a fight.
column2: The guards of Raseir look like they are itching for a fight.

column1: That would only bring more guards.
column2: 那只會招來更多衛兵。

column1: You were told that you may not pass.  Turn now or die!
column2: 已經跟你說過不准通過了。立刻掉頭,否則受死!

column1: No one may pass until Khaveen gives the order.
column2: 哈維因沒下令之前,誰都不准通過。

column1: You there, Stop!
column2: You there, Stop!

column1: It is forbidden to be out at night.  You are in violation of the laws of Raseir.
column2: 夜間外出乃是禁令。你已經觸犯了拉希爾的律法。

column1: Guards!!  There is an intruder in Fountain One.  To me!!
column2: Guards!!  There is an intruder in Fountain One.  To me!!

column1: Hey, what are the gates doing open?
column2: 欸,大門怎麼開著?

column1: I can see .......!
column2: I can see .......!

column1: Guards!  To the gates!!
column2: Guards!  To the gates!!

column1: Hey, what are you doing there?  Don't you know it's against the law?
column2: 欸,你在那邊幹嘛?難道你不知道那是違法的嗎?

column1: Well, since they know we're here, what do you do now, Master?
column2: 好吧,既然他們已經發現我們了,主人,你現在打算怎麼辦?

column1: Halt, In the name of the Law!
column2: Halt, In the name of the Law!

column1: You there!  Halt!!
column2: You there!  Halt!!

column1: Hey, where do you think you are going, Master?  Iblis is over in the Palace.  We haven't got much time.  He's getting more powerful.
column2: 欸,主人,你以為你要往哪兒去?伊布利斯就在宮殿那邊。我們時間不多了。他的力量越來越強。

column1: This Plaza faces the front of the Emir's Palace.  It is well guarded.
column2: This Plaza faces the front of the Emir's Palace.  It is well guarded.

column1: You need to do something about the guards first.
column2: 你得先想辦法對付那些衛兵。

column1: Move out in front of the balcony first.
column2: 先移到陽台前方。

column1: Master, we must hurry onward.
column2: 主人,我們得快點趕路。

column1: Move up to a pillar first.
column2: 先移到柱子旁。

column1: No, Master. You don't need to do that now, let's go.
column2: No, Master. You don't need to do that now, let's go.

column1: Master, you don't need to jump down here.
column2: Master, you don't need to jump down here.

column1: Master, don't go back down there. We must hurry.
column2: 主人,別再下去那邊了。我們得趕緊。

column1: Now would not be a good time for that.
column2: 現在做那件事不是時候。

column1: No Master!  You don't have to fight them.  We must stop Iblis!
column2: No Master!  You don't have to fight them.  We must stop Iblis!

column1: There are no guards to fight.
column2: 沒有衛兵可以打了。

column1: There are too many guards for that.
column2: There are too many guards for that.

column1: Don't fight them tonight!
column2: 今晚別跟他們動手!

column1: The pillars are too slick to climb.
column2: 那些石柱太滑了,爬不上去。

column1: If you could see him, you would.
column2: If you could see him, you would.

column1: You're not near the gate.
column2: 你離大門還遠著呢。

column1: You will have to deal with the guards first.
column2: You will have to deal with the guards first.

column1: You find the gate has been secured on the other side.
column2: 你發現這道門已從另一側鎖死。

column1: Silly Master.  We don't have time for that now.  We need to hurry.
column2: Silly Master.  We don't have time for that now.  We need to hurry.

column1: Master, just hurry and get inside the palace.  We don't have much time.
column2: Master, just hurry and get inside the palace.  We don't have much time.

column1: The plaza in front of the palace gates is kept much cleaner than all the others.
column2: The plaza in front of the palace gates is kept much cleaner than all the others.

column1: No Master, we must get through.
column2: No Master, we must get through.

column1: As you fall in defeat, you hear the Djinni cry "No!   Iblis is loose!"  You dimly hear noise in the background as Iblis rises above the Palace.
column2: As you fall in defeat, you hear the Djinni cry "No!   Iblis is loose!"  You dimly hear noise in the background as Iblis rises above the Palace.

column1: As you fall in defeat, you hear the Djinni cry, "No!  Iblis is loose!"  You dimly hear noise in the background as Iblis rises above the Palace.
column2: As you fall in defeat, you hear the Djinni cry, "No!  Iblis is loose!"  You dimly hear noise in the background as Iblis rises above the Palace.

column1: The other guard can suddenly see again.
column2: 另一名衛兵突然又看得見了。

column1: The guards look more relaxed.
column2: 衛兵們看起來放鬆了些。

column1: That wouldn't be useful while the guards are active.
column2: 趁衛兵還活蹦亂跳時用這招,沒什麼用。

column1: There is a strong sense of Magic beyond the Palace walls.
column2: There is a strong sense of Magic beyond the Palace walls.

column1: You find that the gate has been secured on the other side.
column2: You find that the gate has been secured on the other side.

column1: You can't stop now.  Iblis is starting to awaken!
column2: You can't stop now.  Iblis is starting to awaken!

column1: You move deeper into the shadows to avoid the guards.
column2: 你往陰影深處退去,以躲開衛兵的視線。

column1: Now!  Guards, get that intruder!!
column2: Now!  Guards, get that intruder!!

column1: A swarm of five guards come storming out of the palace after you.  You fight valiantly and desperately against overwhelming odds.
column2: A swarm of five guards come storming out of the palace after you.  You fight valiantly and desperately against overwhelming odds.

column1: Suddenly, the Djinni cries "No!  Iblis is loose!"  You and the guards turn as you hear a rumbling and crashing from inside the Palace.
column2: Suddenly, the Djinni cries "No!  Iblis is loose!"  You and the guards turn as you hear a rumbling and crashing from inside the Palace.

column1: Hey, this guy knows Magic.
column2: Hey, this guy knows Magic.

column1: Guards, To me!
column2: Guards, To me!

column1: What is going on?  Guards!  The gate!!
column2: What is going on?  Guards!  The gate!!

column1: Go get him, Elija-One!  I'll watch you make short work of this hero.
column2: Go get him, Elija-One!  I'll watch you make short work of this hero.

column1: Come out and fight me like a man!
column2: 有種出來,像個男子漢跟我打一場!

column1: You have no choice but to fight.
column2: 你別無選擇,只能應戰。

column1: My eyes!  My eyes!  I can't see!
column2: 我的眼睛!我的眼睛!我看不見了!

column1: No, MY eyes!  MY eyes are blinded!
column2: 不對,是「我」的眼睛!瞎掉的是「我」的眼睛!

column1: I should think I would know if I were blind!
column2: 要是我瞎了,我自己怎麼會不曉得!

column1: If I put your eyes out, you'll think blind!
column2: 等我把你的眼珠子戳出來,你就知道什麼叫瞎了!

column1: Why, I oughta....!
column2: Why, I oughta....!

column1: Oh, no you don't.  MY eyes!
column2: 喔,你少來。是「我」的眼睛!

column1: I'm tellin' ya, I can't see!
column2: 我跟你說啦,我看不見了!

column1: I'm not gonna say it again.  I'm blinded!
column2: 我不想再講第二遍。瞎的是我!

column1: I would if I could see ya!!
column2: I would if I could see ya!!

column1: Timing is everything, and you chose the wrong time to get caught.  You are being hauled back to the dungeon as time runs out.
column2: 時機就是一切,而你偏偏挑了最糟的時候被逮。時間一分一秒流逝,你被拖回了地牢。

column1: What is going on down there?
column2: What is going on down there?

column1: You have no need to pick locks up here, but you better get moving.
column2: 上了這兒,你用不著撬什麼鎖,不過你最好快點行動。

column1: The door is barred from the inside.  You'll have to find another way into the Palace.
column2: 這扇門從裡頭閂上了。你得另找路進宮。

column1: You have no need to bust down doors up here, but you better get moving.
column2: You have no need to bust down doors up here, but you better get moving.

column1: You don't have the strength to break down the door.
column2: 你的力氣還不夠把這扇門撞開。

column1: Sneaking up on a Raseirian guard is never a good idea.   Especially when he has been hand-picked by Khaveen.
column2: 想偷襲拉希爾的衛兵,從來就不是好主意。尤其這名衛兵還是哈維因親手挑出來的。

column1: Khaveen cries out in pain and the guard summons other guards who quickly subdue you.  Khaveen makes certain that you will never bother him again.
column2: Khaveen cries out in pain and the guard summons other guards who quickly subdue you.  Khaveen makes certain that you will never bother him again.

column1: Your attack awakens Khaveen who alerts the guards in the other room.  You are quickly overwhelmed.  It would be best if you don't announce your presence when attempting to be a burglar.
column2: Your attack awakens Khaveen who alerts the guards in the other room.  You are quickly overwhelmed.  It would be best if you don't announce your presence when attempting to be a burglar.

column1: Someone is asleep nearby, surrounded by thin curtains.  The occupant of this bedchamber must collect weapons, for there are many attached to the walls.
column2: 不遠處有人睡著,四周圍著薄薄的帷幔。這間臥房的主人想必愛收藏兵器,牆上掛了好多。

column1: The floor is made of loosely joined hardwood boards.
column2: 地板是用一塊塊接得鬆鬆垮垮的硬木板鋪成的。

column1: What looked at first like a woman sleeping is actually a man with a beard.
column2: What looked at first like a woman sleeping is actually a man with a beard.

column1: Get closer to the cabinet.
column2: Get closer to the cabinet.

column1: Pity.  You don't have any oil.
column2: Pity.  You don't have any oil.

column1: The noise of the breaking glass did not go unnoticed.  Khaveen called his guards who quickly overcame you.  Your end was not a happy one.
column2: 玻璃碎裂的聲響並沒能瞞過誰。哈維因喚來衛兵,三兩下便把你制伏。你的下場可不怎麼好看。

column1: The guard was not asleep, and he didn't get his job by being slow to anger.  Your movements triggered his highly-honed killer instinct.  At least your end was a quick one.
column2: 那衛兵並沒睡著,他能坐這位子,可不是靠脾氣好混來的。你的動作觸動了他磨得鋒利的殺手本能。至少你死得乾脆。

column1: As you move towards Khaveen, a board squeaks and Khaveen turns to look at you.  Your weapon can't stop him from starting to shout.  You are quickly overwhelmed and dragged off to the dungeon, but at least you have some satisfaction knowing Khaveen will no longer trouble Raseir.
column2: As you move towards Khaveen, a board squeaks and Khaveen turns to look at you.  Your weapon can't stop him from starting to shout.  You are quickly overwhelmed and dragged off to the dungeon, but at least you have some satisfaction knowing Khaveen will no longer trouble Raseir.

column1: You are already hiding as well as you can.
column2: You are already hiding as well as you can.

column1: You're already standing up.
column2: You're already standing up.

column1: As your eyes adjust, you find yourself in a bedroom.  Someone is asleep nearby, surrounded by thin curtains.  The occupant of this bedchamber must collect weapons, for there are many attached to the walls.
column2: 待眼睛適應後,你發覺自己身在一間臥房。不遠處有人睡著,四周圍著薄薄的帷幔。這間臥房的主人想必愛收藏兵器,牆上掛了好多。

column1: As you hold the bird in your hand, for a moment you wonder, "Is this the stuff dreams are made of?"  You decide:  "Nah," and put it away.
column2: As you hold the bird in your hand, for a moment you wonder, "Is this the stuff dreams are made of?"  You decide:  "Nah," and put it away.

column1: The hinges have been oiled.
column2: 鉸鏈已經上過油了。

column1: The lock yields to your skill.
column2: 那鎖敵不過你的身手,應聲而開。

column1: Screeack
column2: Screeack

column1: This room's thin carpet does little to muffle the noise made by the loose floor boards beneath it.
column2: 這房間薄薄的地毯,壓根蓋不住底下鬆動地板發出的聲響。

column1: As you and the man look at each other, you realize just whose house this is.   Khaveen calls for his guards, and you are quickly overwhelmed. Khaveen then takes a sword and personally makes certain you'll never steal again.
column2: As you and the man look at each other, you realize just whose house this is.   Khaveen calls for his guards, and you are quickly overwhelmed. Khaveen then takes a sword and personally makes certain you'll never steal again.

column1: What was that noise?
column2: 那是什麼聲音?

column1: What noise, Sir?
column2: 什麼聲音,大人?

column1: The Guards from the other room calmly arrest you and place you in prison for a short, but unpleasant time.  Khaveen is not kind to those that break into his house and use magic against him, but you do not live to tell about it.
column2: The Guards from the other room calmly arrest you and place you in prison for a short, but unpleasant time.  Khaveen is not kind to those that break into his house and use magic against him, but you do not live to tell about it.

column1: The guard quickly calls in other guards, and you are overwhelmed.  Khaveen then takes a sword and personally makes certain you'll never steal again.  The last thing you see is the smile on Khaveen's face.
column2: The guard quickly calls in other guards, and you are overwhelmed.  Khaveen then takes a sword and personally makes certain you'll never steal again.  The last thing you see is the smile on Khaveen's face.

column1: Never mind, go back to your post.
column2: 算了,回你崗位上去。

column1: The cabinet door is locked.
column2: 櫥櫃門上了鎖。

column1: The cabinet door is closed.
column2: The cabinet door is closed.

column1: Your spell has no effect after passing through the bed's veils.
column2: Your spell has no effect after passing through the bed's veils.

column1: You can't Fetch the cabinet.
column2: You can't Fetch the cabinet.

column1: The black bird is too heavy to Fetch.  You wonder what it could be made of that would make it so heavy.
column2: 那隻黑鳥太重,「取物」術搬不動。你納悶它到底是什麼做的,怎會這麼重。

column1: You already have the statue of the bird.
column2: You already have the statue of the bird.

column1: The door to the cabinet is not open.
column2: 櫥櫃的門沒開。

column1: The window is always open.  The climate here is always hot.
column2: 這扇窗總是開著,這兒的氣候向來炎熱。

column1: Creeeak!
column2: Creeeak!

column1: The cabinet door's hinges make a loud noise.
column2: 櫥櫃門的鉸鏈發出好大的聲響。

column1: The sleeper appears to be stirring.  Perhaps you should not move until after he has gone back to sleep.
column2: 睡著的人似乎動了動。或許你該等他重新睡熟了再行動。

column1: You find you cannot move. The man before you has a low, pleasant voice, and eyes like a snake.
column2: You find you cannot move. The man before you has a low, pleasant voice, and eyes like a snake.

column1: %s, I am your friend.
column2: %s, I am your friend.

column1: Remember your friend, Ad Avis.
column2: 記住你的朋友,阿德·阿維斯。

column1: Remember my voice.  Remember my face.
column2: 記住我的聲音,記住我的臉。

column1: Trust me, I am your friend.
column2: 相信我,我是你的朋友。

column1: You try to struggle, but your body will not obey.
column2: 你試圖掙扎,身體卻不聽使喚。

column1: You cannot even close your eyes, but you try to avoid his gaze. There is something about his eyes, though...
column2: 你連眼睛都閉不上,只能拚命想避開他的目光。可是他那雙眼睛,總有種說不出的魔力……

column1: I am your friend, and you trust me.
column2: 我是你的朋友,你信任我。

column1: Trust in my words.  Trust in my eyes.
column2: 信任我的話語,信任我的雙眼。

column1: Look in my eyes, and trust me.
column2: 看著我的眼睛,信任我。

column1: You look at his eyes, and cannot look away.  They seem to glow.  His words seem to whirl around your head.
column2: 你望進他的眼睛,移不開目光。那雙眼彷彿在發光,他的話語在你腦中盤旋打轉。

column1: Obey now your friend, Ad Avis.
column2: 現在,聽從你的朋友——阿德·阿維斯。

column1: Obey now my words.  Obey now my voice.
column2: 聽從我的話語,聽從我的聲音。

column1: Trust in your friend, and Obey me.
column2: Trust in your friend, and Obey me.

column1: You find yourself relaxing.  You are with a friend.  His eyes are gentle, and his smile warms you.
column2: 你感到全身放鬆下來。你與一位朋友同在。他的眼神溫和,笑容暖入你心。

column1: %s, you know me.  I am your friend, Ad Avis, who helped you escape from the dungeons of Raseir.
column2: %s, you know me.  I am your friend, Ad Avis, who helped you escape from the dungeons of Raseir.

column1: Together we seek to aid our friend, Emir Arus.  I was his trusted Vizier, remember?
column2: Together we seek to aid our friend, Emir Arus.  I was his trusted Vizier, remember?

column1: You find yourself nodding at this humble, gentle man.
column2: 你不自覺地對這位謙遜溫和的人點了點頭。

column1: To end the evil reign of Khaveen and restore our friend the Emir Arus, together we must go out into the desert.
column2: 要終結哈維因的邪惡統治,讓我們的朋友埃米爾阿魯斯重掌大權,我們就得一同踏進沙漠。

column1: We must journey to the Forbidden City, and recover the magic item that will break the spell upon the Emir.
column2: We must journey to the Forbidden City, and recover the magic item that will break the spell upon the Emir.

column1: We NEED to get the statue of Iblis!
column2: We NEED to get the statue of Iblis!

column1: Yes, you know this is something you've got to do, but you frown as you try to remember something you once heard about Iblis.
column2: 沒錯,你知道這是你非做不可的事——可是你皺起眉頭,努力回想自己曾聽過關於伊布利斯的某些傳聞。

column1: You remember the statue of Iblis, don't you?  It has remarkable powers, and can break any spell it touches.
column2: You remember the statue of Iblis, don't you?  It has remarkable powers, and can break any spell it touches.

column1: We need to take the statue to Arus, our friend.  Together, let us save the city of Raseir!
column2: 我們得把雕像帶去交給我們的朋友阿魯斯。讓我們攜手拯救拉希爾城!

column1: He puts his arm on your shoulders and you smile.  Here is a man of great wisdom that you have trusted and respected, and he needs your help.
column2: 他把手搭上你的肩,你露出微笑。眼前這位充滿智慧的人,是你一向信任又敬重的對象,而此刻他需要你的幫助。

column1: Together you will save the Emir.  You and Ad Avis will get the statue of Iblis.
column2: Together you will save the Emir.  You and Ad Avis will get the statue of Iblis.

column1: You see an open doorway.
column2: You see an open doorway.

column1: The door is securely barred from the inside.
column2: The door is securely barred from the inside.

column1: You see an open door.
column2: You see an open door.

column1: There is an uninteresting-looking door here.
column2: There is an uninteresting-looking door here.

column1: The room is lavishly appointed in the manner of an Emir's palace.
column2: 這房間布置得富麗堂皇,儼然是埃米爾宮殿的氣派。

column1: You, sir, are a cad.  Get lost!
column2: 先生,你真是個下流胚子。滾開!

column1: The Slave Woman glares at you.
column2: The Slave Woman glares at you.

column1: You wouldn't want to take advantage of me, would you?
column2: 你不會想佔我便宜吧,嗯?

column1: Well, you can't.  So there.
column2: 哼,你辦不到。就是這樣。

column1: Good.  I would have had to refuse.
column2: 很好,不然我只能拒收了。

column1: Please, Hero.  You must help me.
column2: 拜託,英雄,你一定要幫我。

column1: You're already wearing them.
column2: You're already wearing them.

column1: They obviously improve your looks.  Zayishah just giggles at you.
column2: 它們顯然讓你好看了些。札伊莎只是對你咯咯直笑。

column1: You put on the Ali Fakir Genuine X-Ray Glasses.
column2: You put on the Ali Fakir Genuine X-Ray Glasses.

column1: You take them off and put them away.
column2: You take them off and put them away.

column1: As you start, you see fear in the Slave Woman's eyes.
column2: As you start, you see fear in the Slave Woman's eyes.

column1: Guards!  Guards!  Come quickly!  My mistress is in danger.
column2: 衛兵!衛兵!快來啊!我的女主人有危險!

column1: Guards quickly burst into the room.  They drag you off to prison and Khaveen personally oversees your stay.  It is not long.
column2: Guards quickly burst into the room.  They drag you off to prison and Khaveen personally oversees your stay.  It is not long.

column1: I have all the clothes I need, thank you.
column2: 我需要的衣服都齊了,謝謝你。

column1: You exchange clothes with Zayishah since you no longer have any spare ones.  You don't look very Sheik in her veil, but it's a nice fit.  Suddenly the guards break in and drag you off to the Harem.  In two weeks, you are married to Khaveen, and you both live happily ever after.
column2: You exchange clothes with Zayishah since you no longer have any spare ones.  You don't look very Sheik in her veil, but it's a nice fit.  Suddenly the guards break in and drag you off to the Harem.  In two weeks, you are married to Khaveen, and you both live happily ever after.

column1: The clothes first, please.
column2: 麻煩先把衣服給我。

column1: Please, you must help me.  Take off your clothes.
column2: 求求你,你一定要幫我。把你的衣服脫下來。

column1: I must leave the city before I am forced to marry Khaveen.   I have heard you are a hero.  With your help, I can be out of the city before nightfall.
column2: 我必須趁著還沒被逼嫁給哈維因之前離開這座城。聽說你是個英雄,有你相助,我天黑前就能出城。

column1: I wish to disguise myself as you and leave with your Visa, so please, let me exchange clothes with you.
column2: I wish to disguise myself as you and leave with your Visa, so please, let me exchange clothes with you.

column1: You Discover you have no Visa, so you offer her your Master's Card.  She Sears you with her gaze, then beats you to a pulp with a Macy and takes your money.  You are unable to Ward off the blows and are soon J. C. Penniless.
column2: You Discover you have no Visa, so you offer her your Master's Card.  She Sears you with her gaze, then beats you to a pulp with a Macy and takes your money.  You are unable to Ward off the blows and are soon J. C. Penniless.

column1: Please, Hero, your Visa.  There isn't much time.
column2: Please, Hero, your Visa.  There isn't much time.

column1: You start to take off your vest, and then you realize that Shema packed you a change of clothes.  You hand her your spare outfit.
column2: 你正要脫下背心,忽然想起舍瑪幫你準備了一套換洗衣物。於是你把那套備用衣服遞給了她。

column1: Thank you so very much.
column2: 真是太謝謝你了。

column1: How do I look?  Do you think this will fool anybody?  It's so nice not to have to wear a stupid veil.
column2: 我看起來怎麼樣?你覺得這身能騙過人嗎?不用再戴那條蠢面紗,感覺真好。

column1: Please, in order to leave the city I need one more thing, although it will be dangerous for you.  I must have your Visa.
column2: Please, in order to leave the city I need one more thing, although it will be dangerous for you.  I must have your Visa.

column1: Thank you ever so much.  In return for your favors, I can only give you a token.
column2: 太感謝你了。為了報答你的恩情,我能給的只有這一點小小心意。

column1: Here is my mirror.  You have but to show it to any woman in the Palace and she will aid you.
column2: 這是我的鏡子。你只要拿給王宮裡任何一位女子看,她就會幫助你。

column1: Again, my thanks and may fortune favor you.  Farewell.
column2: 再次謝謝你,願好運與你同在。再會了。

column1: Wait until she clears the alleyway before you leave, Effendi.
column2: 閣下,等她走出巷子,你再離開。

column1: What goes on here?
column2: 這裡出什麼事了?

column1: Guards!  To me!
column2: 衛兵!到我這兒來!

column1: You are dragged away to the dungeons of Raseir.
column2: 你被拖進了拉希爾的地牢。

column1: If you need a place of safety in the Palace sometime, the Harem will welcome you for the aid you gave my mistress.  I shall tie a veil from the battlements at night to guide you.
column2: If you need a place of safety in the Palace sometime, the Harem will welcome you for the aid you gave my mistress.  I shall tie a veil from the battlements at night to guide you.

column1: Thank you greatly, Effendi Hero.
column2: 萬分感謝,英雄閣下。

column1: You are met outside by several guards, who cheerfully drag you away to the dungeons of Raseir.
column2: You are met outside by several guards, who cheerfully drag you away to the dungeons of Raseir.

column1: I hope with all my heart that you can restore Raseir.
column2: 我打從心底盼著你能重建拉希爾。

column1: Oh, it is so good to know my uncle is still alive.  He is such a kind and friendly man.
column2: 啊,得知我叔叔還活著,真是太好了。他是個多麼和善親切的人。

column1: That is most interesting, but I must hurry.
column2: 真是有意思,可是我得趕緊走了。

column1: I must hurry before the Eunuchs discover I am gone.  Khaveen will have the city searched.  Please, may I have your Visa?
column2: I must hurry before the Eunuchs discover I am gone.  Khaveen will have the city searched.  Please, may I have your Visa?

column1: She looks pretty busy right now.
column2: 她現在看起來忙得很。

column1: Zayishah's servant pays no attention to you.
column2: 札伊夏的侍女對你不理不睬。

column1: Please, hero, we must hurry.  The guards will be searching for me soon.  I need your clothes.
column2: Please, hero, we must hurry.  The guards will be searching for me soon.  I need your clothes.

column1: The guards will be here any minute.
column2: 衛兵隨時都會到。

column1: Suddenly, the guards walk in and grab all of you, just as Zayishah had warned.  Zayishah is returned to the Harem.   You are taken to the dungeon where Khaveen is waiting for you.
column2: Suddenly, the guards walk in and grab all of you, just as Zayishah had warned.  Zayishah is returned to the Harem.   You are taken to the dungeon where Khaveen is waiting for you.

column1: The guards will be coming soon in search of Zayishah.  You must not be seen talking with me, Effendi.  It is forbidden.
column2: 衛兵很快就會來找札伊夏。閣下,絕不能讓人看見你跟我說話,這是違禁的。

column1: He has a snake's eyes, and hates all women.
column2: 他長著一雙蛇眼,憎恨天下所有女人。

column1: I have been a servant to her since she was little, and her slave since Ad Avis made slaves.  I will do anything for her.
column2: 打從她還小,我就一直是她的侍女;自阿德·阿維斯立下奴隸制後,我又成了她的奴婢。為了她,我什麼都願意做。

column1: Khaveen will not marry Zayishah.  I will kill him myself before I will let him have her.
column2: 哈維因休想娶到札伊夏。在他得逞之前,我會親手了結他。

column1: The Eunuchs watch the women to make sure they cannot leave the Harem.
column2: The Eunuchs watch the women to make sure they cannot leave the Harem.

column1: No man is allowed in the Harem.  The women there knows how to keep secrets.
column2: No man is allowed in the Harem.  The women there knows how to keep secrets.

column1: There are many who resent Ad Avis and Khaveen, and wish to restore the city.
column2: There are many who resent Ad Avis and Khaveen, and wish to restore the city.

column1: It is forbidden for a man to speak to a woman unless she is his mother, wife, or slave.
column2: 男人不准和女子說話,除非對方是他的母親、妻子或奴婢。

column1: I know nothing.
column2: 我什麼都不知道。

column1: I can tell you but little.
column2: 我能告訴你的也不多。

column1: Zayishah will be safe there.
column2: 札伊夏在那裡會很安全。

column1: The city has been destroyed by Khaveen and Ad Avis.
column2: 這座城已被哈維因和阿德·阿維斯毀了。

column1: Khaveen.
column2: Khaveen.

column1: The Sultan (May-he-reign-for ever) will care for Zayishah in Shapeir, if she can but get there.
column2: The Sultan (May-he-reign-for ever) will care for Zayishah in Shapeir, if she can but get there.

column1: The Emir is a weak fool who thinks he rules this city.
column2: 埃米爾是個軟弱的蠢蛋,還以為自己當真統治著這座城。

column1: There are many secret ways through the Palace.
column2: 穿越王宮的密道多得很。

column1: Zayishah knows the Caravan Master.  He will take her to Shapeir.
column2: 札伊夏認識商隊隊長,他會帶她去夏皮爾。

column1: The desert is a safer place for Zayishah than this city.
column2: 對札伊夏來說,沙漠都比這座城安全。

column1: Do you not know better than I?
column2: 難道你知道的不比我多嗎?

column1: If the fountain was still running, Khaveen would not have such power as he has now.  He controls who gets water.
column2: 要是噴泉還在流,哈維因就不會有今天這般權勢。是他掌控著誰能取得水源。

column1: Khaveen finds great pleasure in the pain of others.
column2: 哈維因以他人的痛苦為樂。

column1: There is one Katta left in the city.  He is with the underground.
column2: There is one Katta left in the city.  He is with the underground.

column1: I am called Mayzun.
column2: 人家都叫我梅珍。

column1: He is a brutal man.  I do not like the way he looks at me.  One day, he took my father and me down to the dungeons to watch the torturing of prisoners.  He had the same kind of look as he watched their pain.  I will not marry him.
column2: 他是個殘暴的人。我討厭他看我的那種眼神。有一天,他帶著我父親和我下到地牢,看囚犯受刑。他望著那些人痛苦時,眼裡也是同樣的神情。我絕不嫁給他。

column1: My father has given in to Khaveen and all his lies.  I am to be married in two weeks when Khaveen takes over the city and Ad Avis leaves.
column2: 我父親已向哈維因和他那一套謊言屈服了。再過兩週,等哈維因接管這座城、阿德·阿維斯離開,我就要被嫁出去。

column1: My father is Ali Al-Din Hasan.  He is the Emir of this city, at least in name, but he has no control over anything, not even in the Palace.  He is but a puppet for Khaveen and Ad Avis.
column2: My father is Ali Al-Din Hasan.  He is the Emir of this city, at least in name, but he has no control over anything, not even in the Palace.  He is but a puppet for Khaveen and Ad Avis.

column1: In your clothes, I can leave the city.  I have special letters of envoy signed by Khaveen.  I need only your Visa to be free.
column2: In your clothes, I can leave the city.  I have special letters of envoy signed by Khaveen.  I need only your Visa to be free.

column1: Mayzun is my servant woman.  She is also my friend.  I trust her with my life.
column2: 梅珍是我的侍女,也是我的朋友。我把性命都託付給她。

column1: I am the Emir's Daughter, Zayishah.
column2: I am the Emir's Daughter, Zayishah.

column1: I fear and hate him more than Khaveen.  It is he who destroyed my Uncle Arus and made Raseir the terrible place it now is.  Ad Avis was my uncle's Vizier.  There are only two passions to Ad Avis: complete Power, and complete Control.  He will stop at nothing to gain those.
column2: I fear and hate him more than Khaveen.  It is he who destroyed my Uncle Arus and made Raseir the terrible place it now is.  Ad Avis was my uncle's Vizier.  There are only two passions to Ad Avis: complete Power, and complete Control.  He will stop at nothing to gain those.

column1: Ad Avis is a master of magic and exults in showing off how powerful he is.  He has boasted that soon he will have the power to rule the world.  I can only hope he is mistaken.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯是個魔法大師,最愛炫耀自己有多強大。他誇口說,用不了多久他就會擁有統治世界的力量。我只能祈禱他是想錯了。

column1: This city is a model of how Ad Avis thinks the world should be run. Ad Avis will be the ruler, of course.  He has my father under a spell and my father will do anything Ad Avis says.
column2: 這座城就是阿德·阿維斯心目中世界該有的樣板。當然,統治者非他莫屬。他對我父親下了咒,父親如今對他言聽計從。

column1: They are the guards for the women in the Harem.
column2: 他們是看守後宮女眷的衛兵。

column1: All the women must stay there now, hiding their faces behind veils. Did you know it is forbidden for a man to speak to a woman unless she is his mother, daughter, or wife?  It is all Ad Avis's doing.  He must really hate women.
column2: All the women must stay there now, hiding their faces behind veils. Did you know it is forbidden for a man to speak to a woman unless she is his mother, daughter, or wife?  It is all Ad Avis's doing.  He must really hate women.

column1: Please, I have so little time.
column2: 拜託,我的時間真的不多了。

column1: There is nothing I can say about that.
column2: 那件事我無可奉告。

column1: I will travel with the caravan back to Shapeir.  I know the Sultan Harun al Rashid.  He will let me stay at his Palace.
column2: 我會跟著商隊回到夏皮爾。我認識蘇丹哈倫·拉希德,他會讓我住進他的王宮。

column1: This was a beautiful place to live a year ago.  All of these changes took place because of Ad Avis.
column2: 一年前,這裡還是個適合安居的好地方。這一切的改變,全是因為阿德·阿維斯。

column1: There are but two monsters in this city.  One is Khaveen, the other Ad Avis.
column2: 這座城裡只有兩頭惡魔。一頭是哈維因,另一頭是阿德·阿維斯。

column1: I do not know what magic Ad Avis has, but it is clear he is a Wizard.  You must not get near him unless you are protected against spells.
column2: I do not know what magic Ad Avis has, but it is clear he is a Wizard.  You must not get near him unless you are protected against spells.

column1: The Sultan is like a father to me.  I will be glad to see him again and listen to his poetry.
column2: 蘇丹待我如同父親一般。能再見到他、聽他吟詩,我會很高興。

column1: My father would never overthrow his brother, no matter what foul rumors you have heard.  My father is just a puppet for Ad Avis.
column2: 不管你聽到什麼難聽的謠言,我父親絕不會推翻自己的兄長。他只是阿德·阿維斯的傀儡罷了。

column1: The Palace is filled with guards and all women must stay in the Harem.
column2: The Palace is filled with guards and all women must stay in the Harem.

column1: The Caravan Master is my friend.  He taught me how to ride when I was little.
column2: 商隊隊長是我的朋友。我小時候,是他教會我騎乘的。

column1: I know how to survive in the desert, it is my home.  Before Ad Avis took over the city, I roamed it freely at will.  The caravan is but a day's ride ahead of me.  I will catch it easily.
column2: I know how to survive in the desert, it is my home.  Before Ad Avis took over the city, I roamed it freely at will.  The caravan is but a day's ride ahead of me.  I will catch it easily.

column1: I have some money and some jewels, but I may need them to bribe some guards and survive outside.  I know my father will reward you greatly, if things ever return to normal around here.
column2: 我手上有些錢和珠寶,不過這些可能得拿去賄賂衛兵、撐過在外的日子。要是這裡哪天恢復正常,我父親一定會重重報答你。

column1: I have heard that the inn is but a den of thieves.  It certainly pays a fortune in bribes to Khaveen.
column2: 我聽說那間客棧根本是個賊窩。它向哈維因進貢的賄款肯定是一筆天文數字。

column1: I have no time to speak of trifles, I must escape quickly.
column2: 我沒空閒聊瑣事,得趕緊逃走。

column1: The Fountain of the city dried up just after my Uncle Arus disappeared.
column2: The Fountain of the city dried up just after my Uncle Arus disappeared.

column1: I do not know the truth of this rumor, but I have heard that Ad Avis is very much afraid of fire.
column2: 這傳言是真是假我不清楚,但我聽說阿德·阿維斯非常怕火。

column1: The Katta used to fill this city, but they were driven out.  I think Khaveen is allergic to them.
column2: 這座城從前到處都是卡塔族,後來卻被趕了出去。我猜哈維因大概對他們過敏。

column1: My name is Zayishah, daughter of Emir Ali al-Din Hasan.
column2: 我叫札伊夏,是埃米爾阿里·阿丁·哈桑的女兒。

column1: You hear the sound of guards talking just outside the door.
column2: 你聽見就在門外,有衛兵在交談。

column1: The Katta cries out in pain, causing the guards to rush in.   They take you to a cell from which you can find no escape.
column2: The Katta cries out in pain, causing the guards to rush in.   They take you to a cell from which you can find no escape.

column1: The cell is made from quarried rock, with massive iron bars and a huge gate.  Despite the heat outside, it is very cold in here.
column2: The cell is made from quarried rock, with massive iron bars and a huge gate.  Despite the heat outside, it is very cold in here.

column1: Thank you, for your kindness.  Now it's time to think about escaping.
column2: Thank you, for your kindness.  Now it's time to think about escaping.

column1: Thank you for offering, but it appears you are out of food.  But that's not the important issue here, it is time to think about escaping.
column2: Thank you for offering, but it appears you are out of food.  But that's not the important issue here, it is time to think about escaping.

column1: My heart says that you are a friend, yet my mind fears you are a trap.  Is there not some token you can show me to prove what you say?
column2: 我的心說你是朋友,我的腦卻怕你是個圈套。你可有什麼信物能拿給我,證明你所言不虛?

column1: The katta just stares suspiciously at you.
column2: The katta just stares suspiciously at you.

column1: You hear the sounds of guards talking just outside the outer door.
column2: You hear the sounds of guards talking just outside the outer door.

column1: The bars are made of thick iron.  Bending them would be out of the question.
column2: The bars are made of thick iron.  Bending them would be out of the question.

column1: As you struggle with the astonishingly tough katta, the guards enter and separate you both, taking you to a cell from which you cannot escape.
column2: As you struggle with the astonishingly tough katta, the guards enter and separate you both, taking you to a cell from which you cannot escape.

column1: The Katta raises his eyebrows in surprise, but then resumes his silent, suspicious stare.
column2: The Katta raises his eyebrows in surprise, but then resumes his silent, suspicious stare.

column1: Thank you, my friend.
column2: Thank you, my friend.

column1: That does not seem to be a sign the Katta recognizes, for he watches you with amusement.
column2: That does not seem to be a sign the Katta recognizes, for he watches you with amusement.

column1: But he soon resumes his silent stare.
column2: But he soon resumes his silent stare.

column1: Which one?  There are many here, but none of them appear to be moveable.
column2: Which one?  There are many here, but none of them appear to be moveable.

column1: Nobody can stare down a katta.  After several seconds, you become bleary-eyed and turn away.
column2: Nobody can stare down a katta.  After several seconds, you become bleary-eyed and turn away.

column1: Your equipment is on the stone table.
column2: Your equipment is on the stone table.

column1: You had better leave quickly.  There's no time for a search now.
column2: You had better leave quickly.  There's no time for a search now.

column1: Good idea, but how?  Where?
column2: Good idea, but how?  Where?

column1: You look around and see a large stone whose mortar has been loosened.
column2: You look around and see a large stone whose mortar has been loosened.

column1: Now that you've made friends and told tales, it's time to think about escaping.
column2: 如今你已交了朋友、敘過往事,該想想怎麼脫身了。

column1: You've already shown him your pin.
column2: 你已經把胸針給他看過了。

column1: The cell door is already open.
column2: 牢門早就開著了。

column1: You find only the Katta and some straw.
column2: You find only the Katta and some straw.

column1: You find nothing but straw.
column2: You find nothing but straw.

column1: You find a slightly rusty nail in a crack between the stones of the floor.
column2: You find a slightly rusty nail in a crack between the stones of the floor.

column1: Remembering your Advanced Adventuring lessons, you set to work on the cell door lock, using the %s as a lockpick
column2: Remembering your Advanced Adventuring lessons, you set to work on the cell door lock, using the %s as a lockpick

column1: You don't know how to pick locks.
column2: 你不會撬鎖。

column1: You find that the guards took all your tools when they searched you.  Your equipment is beyond reach.  You'll need to find something else.
column2: 你發現衛兵搜身時把你所有的工具都拿走了。你的裝備根本搆不著。你得另想辦法。

column1: You can climb the bars easily, but that won't help you escape.
column2: You can climb the bars easily, but that won't help you escape.

column1: The bars are made of heavy iron and it would take far too long to bend or break them, even with your great strength.
column2: 這些欄杆是厚重的鐵條打的,就算憑你那身蠻力,要把它們扳彎或弄斷,也得耗上老半天。

column1: The heavy iron bars don't even budge.
column2: 那些厚重的鐵欄杆紋風不動。

column1: After you, my friend.  I will humbly follow.
column2: 您先請,朋友。我恭敬地跟在後頭。

column1: I would follow you gladly.  But first we must escape this cell.
column2: 我樂意跟隨你。但我們得先逃出這間牢房。

column1: The Katta isn't going anywhere he doesn't want to go.
column2: The Katta isn't going anywhere he doesn't want to go.

column1: Go ahead.  The door's open.
column2: Go ahead.  The door's open.

column1: That could be hard to do while locked inside a jail cell.
column2: That could be hard to do while locked inside a jail cell.

column1: After being dragged to the dungeon of the palace, you were roughly searched, beaten, and questioned by the guards.  They warned you that Khaveen will be coming soon to personally question you...
column2: 你被拖進王宮地牢後,衛兵粗暴地搜身、毒打,還逼問了你一頓。他們警告你,哈維因很快就會親自來審問你……

column1: ...and that you won't enjoy it.
column2: ……而那場面,你肯定不會好受。

column1: The katta watches you calmly with a mixture of interest and suspicion.
column2: The katta watches you calmly with a mixture of interest and suspicion.

column1: I have heard from others in the Underground that there is a concealed passage in the room outside this cell.
column2: 小的聽地下組織的其他人說過,這間牢房外頭的房間裡,藏著一條暗道。

column1: Looks as though you could have used more body-building practice.  You'll just have to try again.
column2: 看來你平日的健身練得還不夠。只能再試一次了。

column1: Looks like you should have eaten your Wheeties this morning.  You did feel the door give a little when you hit it this time.
column2: 看來你今早該多吃幾碗飯補補力氣。不過這回你撞上去,倒是真感覺到門鬆動了一點點。

column1: You don't seem to be strong enough to force the door.  You'll have to try something else.
column2: 你的力氣似乎還不足以把門硬撞開。得換個別的法子才行。

column1: A pin like that can only be seen by a friend, such is its magic.  It is Katta magic.  You must be very trusted by my people to gain such a thing.
column2: A pin like that can only be seen by a friend, such is its magic.  It is Katta magic.  You must be very trusted by my people to gain such a thing.

column1: Well, Katta-friend, let us escape this very dangerous place.
column2: 那麼,卡塔之友,咱們快逃離這個極其危險的地方吧。

column1: Ah, then you are indeed a friend in a place where we both need friends.  We need to escape from this prison.
column2: 啊,那麼在這你我都需要朋友的地方,你果真是個朋友。我們得逃出這座牢獄。

column1: The guards come in and take you to a cell you can't escape from.
column2: 衛兵走了進來,把你押進一間插翅難逃的牢房。

column1: You find a secret panel on the back wall.
column2: 你在後牆上發現了一塊暗板。

column1: The stone slides back to reveal a hidden passageway.
column2: 石塊向後滑開,露出一條隱密的通道。

column1: Before you escape, perhaps you should get your equipment.
column2: 在逃走之前,或許你該先把裝備拿上。

column1: You find yourself crawling through a twisty passage for what seems like forever.  When it comes to a dead end, you force open another panel and find yourself in the streets of Raseir.
column2: You find yourself crawling through a twisty passage for what seems like forever.  When it comes to a dead end, you force open another panel and find yourself in the streets of Raseir.

column1: Thanks to your great strength, we can leave immediately.
column2: 多虧您這身蠻力,咱們這就能走了。

column1: The entrance to the passageway should be here.....
column2: The entrance to the passageway should be here.....

column1: I have helped others escape through the secret passageways.
column2: 小的曾幫過其他人從這些暗道逃出去。

column1: Yes, it is here.  Lend your strong shoulder to the task.
column2: 沒錯,就在這兒。借您那壯實的肩膀使把勁吧。

column1: Do not fear.  I will lead you to safety through the passageways, Katta-friend.
column2: 別怕。小的會領您穿過暗道,平安脫身,卡塔之友。

column1: Perhaps, before you leave, you should pick up your equipment over there near the cell.
column2: 或許,在動身之前,您該先把擱在牢房那邊的裝備拿上。

column1: But do not dally too long, or the guards will surely come.
column2: 不過可別磨蹭太久,否則衛兵準會趕來。

column1: The katta leads you through dark passages under the Palace to a secret exit into the Raseir streets.  Sharaf warns you that tomorrow night the Underground plans to attack the Palace and overthrow the Emir.
column2: The katta leads you through dark passages under the Palace to a secret exit into the Raseir streets.  Sharaf warns you that tomorrow night the Underground plans to attack the Palace and overthrow the Emir.

column1: He shows you the passage that leads back towards the Blue Parrot Inn and reminds you to be wary of guards.  Then he bids you good luck and scurries off.
column2: He shows you the passage that leads back towards the Blue Parrot Inn and reminds you to be wary of guards.  Then he bids you good luck and scurries off.

column1: You and Sharaf wander the passages beneath the Palace.  Without your equipment, you both perish in the darkness.
column2: You and Sharaf wander the passages beneath the Palace.  Without your equipment, you both perish in the darkness.

column1: You wander the passages beneath the Palace.  Without your equipment, you perish in the darkness.
column2: 你在王宮底下的通道裡四處摸索。少了裝備,你葬身在這片黑暗之中。

column1: You find yourself crawling through a twisting passage for what seems like forever.  When it comes to a dead end, you force open another panel and find yourself in the streets of Raseir.
column2: You find yourself crawling through a twisting passage for what seems like forever.  When it comes to a dead end, you force open another panel and find yourself in the streets of Raseir.

column1: You recover all of your equipment.
column2: 你取回了所有的裝備。

column1: The rock is much too heavy for your spell to effect it.
column2: 這塊石頭太重了,你的法術奈何不了它。

column1: The walls in the cell are solid, mortered stone.
column2: The walls in the cell are solid, mortered stone.

column1: Which one?  There are many here.
column2: Which one?  There are many here.

column1: Your belongings go up in a puff of smoke.
column2: 你的家當化作一縷青煙,消失無蹤。

column1: You already have your equipment.
column2: You already have your equipment.

column1: That is difficult to accomplish from inside the cell.
column2: 待在牢房裡,這事可不好辦。

column1: You can't get to your equipment from where you are.
column2: You can't get to your equipment from where you are.

column1: The cell's door is already open.
column2: The cell's door is already open.

column1: The cell's door is already closed.
column2: 牢門早就關上了。

column1: Why?  You're trying to escape, remember?
column2: 幹嘛?你可是想越獄的,別忘了。

column1: That's a very good idea.  But how?
column2: 這主意挺不錯。可是,要怎麼弄呢?

column1: Your spell has attracted the attention of the guards.
column2: 你的法術引來了衛兵的注意。

column1: That wouldn't do you any good here.
column2: 在這兒那對你沒半點好處。

column1: Of such things, I do not know what to say.
column2: 這種事,我實在不知該說什麼。

column1: I'm afraid I can add but little to your knowledge.
column2: I'm afraid I can add but little to your knowledge.

column1: Shapeir is a fine city, but it is not the home that Raseir was to the Katta.
column2: 夏皮爾是座好城市,但對卡塔族來說,它終究不是拉希爾那樣的家園。

column1: The grandmothers of our grandmothers' grandmothers were born in this place.  Katta have lived here before there was even a city. No one can keep the Katta from their home.  The Katta will come back.
column2: The grandmothers of our grandmothers' grandmothers were born in this place.  Katta have lived here before there was even a city. No one can keep the Katta from their home.  The Katta will come back.

column1: The magic of the Katta is subtle - an oil lamp that never burns out, a door that always opens to your touch, a pin that can only be seen by friends.
column2: The magic of the Katta is subtle - an oil lamp that never burns out, a door that always opens to your touch, a pin that can only be seen by friends.

column1: The Sultan of Shapeir is very powerful, but too far away to aid Raseir.
column2: 夏皮爾的蘇丹勢力強大,可惜離得太遠,救不了拉希爾。

column1: The new Emir is but a fool and the Underground will soon depose him.
column2: The new Emir is but a fool and the Underground will soon depose him.

column1: There are many passages under the Palace.  Many times I have traveled through them to help release prisoners.
column2: 王宮底下有許多密道。我多次穿行其中,幫忙救出囚犯。

column1: The Blue Parrot Inn is a meeting place for dogs who lick Khaveen's boots and then growl at him behind his back.
column2: 藍鸚鵡客棧是一群走狗碰頭的地方——當面舔哈維因的靴子,背地裡又對他齜牙咧嘴。

column1: The fountain of Raseir was once as beautiful and ever-flowing as Shapeir's.  However, since the darkness came, it has ceased to flow.
column2: 拉希爾的噴泉曾經和夏皮爾的一樣美麗、終年不竭。可是自從黑暗降臨,它就再也不流了。

column1: It is rumored that Khaveen is but a tool for someone more cruel and powerful.  Someone who uses magic to rule Raseir.
column2: 傳言哈維因不過是某個更殘忍、更強大之人的傀儡——那人用魔法統治著拉希爾。

column1: We are little folk, yes.  Still, we can bring down the State.
column2: We are little folk, yes.  Still, we can bring down the State.

column1: My name, Sharaf, means "honor".
column2: My name, Sharaf, means "honor".

column1: There is no mistaking the art of my people.  That is a very valuable gift you have.  The guards did not take it from you because they could not see it while you are wearing it.  Only a friend or one who would be your friend can see such a pin.
column2: 我族的手藝絕對假不了。你手上那件可是無價之寶。衛兵沒從你身上拿走它,是因為只要你戴著,他們就看不見。這種別針,只有朋友、或是願意與你為友的人才看得見。

column1: The resistance consists of many of the guards and townsfolk who rebel against the tyranny of the new Emir.
column2: 反抗軍裡有不少是不堪新埃米爾暴政、起來造反的衛兵和鎮民。

column1: There is a concealed passageway somewhere outside this cell.  I have helped others escape by passageways before, but this is the first I have been trapped in a cell.  I am not easily captured.
column2: 這牢房外某處藏著一條密道。我以前靠密道幫不少人逃過,可這還是頭一回換我被關進牢裡。要抓住我可沒那麼容易。

column1: The things which you carried were placed on the table over there.
column2: The things which you carried were placed on the table over there.

column1: He is very brave and honorable.  He swore as the Katta were driven from Raseir that he would find a Hero to restore our homeland.  It seems he must have found one.
column2: 他既勇敢又重榮譽。卡塔族被趕出拉希爾時,他發誓要找到一位英雄,重建我們的家園。看來他真的找到了。

column1: Shema is my cousin.  She is very bright and knows how to run an inn.  She is also a good judge of character and would know a true hero when she saw you.
column2: 舍瑪是我的表親。她非常聰明,懂得怎麼經營客棧,看人也很準,一眼就能認出誰是真英雄——比如你。

column1: Khaveen is the most feared man in all Raseir, although rumor has it he is not the most powerful.
column2: 哈維因是全拉希爾最令人聞風喪膽的人,不過傳言說,他並不是權勢最大的那一個。

column1: The guards are just outside the door over there, and will be returning soon.
column2: 衛兵就在那扇門外,馬上就要回來了。

column1: I was captured when a former member of the Underground betrayed us, and the guards found where I stayed.
column2: 地下組織裡一個叛徒出賣了我們,衛兵查出我藏身的地方,我就這麼被抓了。

column1: Kattas understand honor in ways most humans do not appreciate.  Honor cannot be given or won by contest.  It comes to you only through diligently placing your own needs below concern for others.
column2: Kattas understand honor in ways most humans do not appreciate.  Honor cannot be given or won by contest.  It comes to you only through diligently placing your own needs below concern for others.

column1: There is little trust in Raseir, for betrayal is everywhere.  You must show me something that I know is from the Katta.
column2: There is little trust in Raseir, for betrayal is everywhere.  You must show me something that I know is from the Katta.

column1: The sky is so bright, that it hurts your eyes.
column2: The sky is so bright, that it hurts your eyes.

column1: This looks like a nice, quiet spot.
column2: 這地方看起來挺不錯,又安靜。

column1: Don't bother the guards.
column2: Don't bother the guards.

column1: Do not interfere!
column2: 別來礙事!

column1: Hero, you must help me!  Do something!
column2: 英雄,你一定要救救我!想想辦法呀!

column1: The swords of the guards press deeper into your skin.
column2: 衛兵的刀刃更深地壓進你的皮肉。

column1: There are no heroes in this town.  You of all men should know that, Ugarte.
column2: 這座城裡沒有什麼英雄。烏加特,你最該清楚這一點才對。

column1: I know very little.  What is going on?  I am innocent of any wrong-doing.
column2: I know very little.  What is going on?  I am innocent of any wrong-doing.

column1: If you are so innocent, then what is this?
column2: 你既然這麼清白,那這是什麼東西?

column1: I don't know how that water bag could have gotten there.  Someone must have planted it on me.
column2: 我不知道那個水袋怎麼會在那裡。一定是有人栽贓給我的。

column1: Waterskins under your tunic, Ugarte?  You know that smuggling water is a crime against the State.  You also know what we do to criminals like you.
column2: Waterskins under your tunic, Ugarte?  You know that smuggling water is a crime against the State.  You also know what we do to criminals like you.

column1: No, no, please.  I am innocent!  I do not know how it got there.  No!
column2: 不,不,求求你們。我是清白的!我真不知道那東西怎麼會在那裡。不要啊!

column1: Hero....        Help!.....
column2: Hero....        Help!.....

column1: This is the fate of anyone who breaks the rules of the city.  Remember this, and see that you do not suffer the same fate.
column2: 這就是膽敢違反本城律法者的下場。給我記住,別讓自己落得同樣的結局。

column1: I hope you are smart enough to learn this lesson.  You do not want to see the dungeons of Raseir.
column2: 我希望你夠聰明,能把這堂課學進去。你可不會想見識拉希爾的地牢。

column1: You are in the Plaza across from the Palace gates.
column2: You are in the Plaza across from the Palace gates.

column1: Everywhere you look, dirty Plazas.
column2: Everywhere you look, dirty Plazas.

column1: Wow, What do you know?  It worked!  My magic really worked!
column2: 哇,你看看!成功了!我的魔法真的奏效了!

column1: We are now in what you call Raseir.  This is as close to Iblis as I could get.
column2: 我們現在就在你們所說的拉希爾。這已經是我能把你帶到離伊布利斯最近的地方了。

column1: There are some very powerful spells coming from that Palace over there.  We don't have much time!
column2: 那邊的宮殿裡傳出某種非常強大的法術。我們時間不多了!

column1: Well, what are you waiting for?  Let's go!
column2: 好啦,你還在等什麼?走吧!

column1: There is a soft whisper from the alleyway.
column2: 巷子裡傳來一陣輕聲的低語。

column1: Hero-friend!  What magic you have at your command!
column2: 英雄朋友!你手上掌握的可是好厲害的魔法啊!

column1: The UnderGround is prepared to attack the Palace.  If you need to enter there, we will provide some distraction for you.  However, we are almost ready, so you must hurry before you are caught in our battle.
column2: The UnderGround is prepared to attack the Palace.  If you need to enter there, we will provide some distraction for you.  However, we are almost ready, so you must hurry before you are caught in our battle.

column1: Sharaf silently sneaks off.
column2: 夏拉夫悄無聲息地溜走了。

column1: You can't stop now!  Iblis is starting to awaken!
column2: 你現在不能停下來!伊布利斯就要甦醒了!

column1: No, no!  This way.  This way!
column2: 不對,不對!這邊。走這邊!

column1: But Master, the door to the ritual chamber is already open!
column2: 可是主人,儀式廳的門已經開了呀!

column1: Now through the door at the top of the stairs is Iblis.  There is some sort of spell on the door, but I don't know what it is.  There is an awful lot of magic around here.
column2: Now through the door at the top of the stairs is Iblis.  There is some sort of spell on the door, but I don't know what it is.  There is an awful lot of magic around here.

column1: The candles are being lit in the other room.  When the last candle catches fire, it will be too late.  Please, Master, stop the man from freeing Iblis.
column2: The candles are being lit in the other room.  When the last candle catches fire, it will be too late.  Please, Master, stop the man from freeing Iblis.

column1: Hurry, Master!  You must follow me.
column2: 快,主人!你得跟著我走。

column1: You race through the palace passageways, pausing now and again to evade scurrying guards.  The Djinni of the Ring, for all of his forgetful nature, seems to have a clear idea of where he's taking you.
column2: 你在宮殿的通道間飛奔,不時停下腳步,躲避四處奔走的衛兵。指環之精靈雖然生性健忘,這回卻對該把你帶往何處,似乎有著清晰的概念。

column1: Through here, Master!  Iblis is close, and time is short.
column2: 從這邊走,主人!伊布利斯就在附近,時間不多了。

column1: No, Master, you can't!  We have to get to Iblis!
column2: 不行,主人,不能這樣!我們得趕去找伊布利斯啊!

column1: Please, we've got to do something.  Hurry!
column2: 拜託,我們得想想辦法。快點!

column1: Master!  Iblis is about to be summoned!  Don't just stand there, DO something!
column2: Master!  Iblis is about to be summoned!  Don't just stand there, DO something!

column1: Sneak around the balcony?  If you were down there, you might try sneaking.
column2: 繞著陽台潛行?你要是在下頭,倒可以試著潛行。

column1: Run around the balcony?  If you were down there, you might try running.
column2: 繞著陽台跑?你要是在下頭,倒可以試著跑跑。

column1: There is no time, Master.  Iblis will be free!
column2: There is no time, Master.  Iblis will be free!

column1: While he's alive?  Not likely.
column2: While he's alive?  Not likely.

column1: I fear you cannot reach the door handles from where you are standing.
column2: 恐怕從你站的地方,是搆不著那門把的。

column1: No, Master.  You must hurry through the door at the top of the stairs, or Ad Avis will release Iblis!
column2: No, Master.  You must hurry through the door at the top of the stairs, or Ad Avis will release Iblis!
